1: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
2: // MODULE: Embedder Reindex Orchestrator
3: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
4: 
5: import { randomUUID } from 'node:crypto';
6: import * as fs from 'node:fs';
7: import * as path from 'node:path';

12: import { EmbeddingProfile } from '@spec-kit/shared/embeddings/profile';

14: 
15: import { attachActiveVectorShard, initializeDb } from '../search/vector-index-store.js';
16: import { to_embedding_buffer } from '../search/vector-index-types.js';
17: import {
18:   ensureVecTableForDim,
19:   getActiveEmbedder,
20:   setActiveEmbedder,
21:   vecTableNameForDim,
22: } from './schema.js';

29: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
30: // 1. TYPE DEFINITIONS
31: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
32: 
33: export type ReindexJobStatus = 'queued' | 'running' | 'completed' | 'failed' | 'cancelled';
34: 
35: export interface ReindexJob {
36:   readonly id: string;
37:   readonly fromName: string;
38:   readonly toName: string;
39:   readonly toDim: number;
40:   readonly total: number;
41:   readonly processed: number;
42:   readonly status: ReindexJobStatus;
43:   readonly startedAt: string;
44:   readonly updatedAt: string;
45:   readonly error?: string;
46: }
47: 
48: export interface StartReindexOptions {
49:   readonly toName: string;
50: }
51: 
52: export interface ReindexRuntimeOptions {
53:   readonly db?: Database.Database;
54: }
55: 
56: interface ReindexInternalRuntimeOptions extends ReindexRuntimeOptions {
57:   readonly autoStart?: boolean;
58: }
59: 
60: type JobRow = Omit<ReindexJob, 'error'> & {
61:   readonly error: string | null;
62: };
63: 
64: interface MemoryRow {
65:   readonly id: number;
66:   readonly content_text: string | null;
67:   readonly title: string | null;
68:   readonly file_path: string | null;
69: }
70: 
71: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
72: // 2. CONSTANTS
73: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
74: 
75: const DEFAULT_BATCH_SIZE = 50;
76: const MIN_BATCH_SIZE = 1;
77: const MAX_BATCH_SIZE = 1_000;
78: const RESTORE_REQUEUE_DELAY_MS = 100;
79: const logger = createLogger('embedder-reindex');
80: const JOB_TABLE_SQL = `
81:   CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS embedder_jobs (
82:     id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
83:     from_name TEXT NOT NULL,
84:     to_name TEXT NOT NULL,
85:     to_dim INTEGER NOT NULL,
86:     total INTEGER NOT NULL,
87:     processed INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
88:     status TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(status IN ('queued', 'running', 'completed', 'failed', 'cancelled')),
89:     started_at TEXT NOT NULL,
90:     updated_at TEXT NOT NULL,
91:     error TEXT
92:   )
93: `;
94: 
95: const JOB_SELECT_COLUMNS = `
96:   id,
97:   from_name AS fromName,
98:   to_name AS toName,
99:   to_dim AS toDim,
100:   total,
101:   processed,
102:   status,
103:   started_at AS startedAt,
104:   updated_at AS updatedAt,
105:   error
106: `;
107: 
108: const runningJobs = new Set<string>();
109: 
110: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
111: // 3. HELPERS
112: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
113: 
114: export class InvalidDatabaseDirError extends Error {
115:   constructor(message: string) {
116:     super(message);
117:     this.name = 'InvalidDatabaseDirError';
118:   }
119: }
120: 
121: function resolveDb(db?: Database.Database): Database.Database {
122:   return db ?? initializeDb();
123: }
124: 
125: function nowIso(): string {
126:   return new Date().toISOString();
127: }
128: 
129: function readJobStatus(db: Database.Database, jobId: string): ReindexJobStatus | null {
130:   ensureJobTable(db);
131:   const row = db.prepare('SELECT status FROM embedder_jobs WHERE id = ?').get(jobId) as
132:     | { status?: ReindexJobStatus }
133:     | undefined;
134:   return row?.status ?? null;
135: }
136: 
137: function getBatchSize(): number {
138:   return parseBoundedEnv('EMBEDDER_REINDEX_BATCH_SIZE', DEFAULT_BATCH_SIZE, MIN_BATCH_SIZE, MAX_BATCH_SIZE);
139: }
140: 
141: function jobFromRow(row: JobRow): ReindexJob {
142:   const { error, ...job } = row;
143:   return error ? { ...job, error } : job;
144: }
145: 
146: function ensureJobTable(db: Database.Database): void {
147:   db.exec(JOB_TABLE_SQL);
148: }
149: 
150: function selectJob(db: Database.Database, jobId: string): ReindexJob | null {
151:   ensureJobTable(db);
152:   const row = db.prepare(`
153:     SELECT ${JOB_SELECT_COLUMNS}
154:     FROM embedder_jobs
155:     WHERE id = ?
156:   `).get(jobId) as JobRow | undefined;
157: 
158:   return row ? jobFromRow(row) : null;
159: }
160: 
161: function selectActiveJob(db: Database.Database): ReindexJob | null {
162:   ensureJobTable(db);
163:   const row = db.prepare(`
164:     SELECT ${JOB_SELECT_COLUMNS}
165:     FROM embedder_jobs
166:     WHERE status IN ('queued', 'running')
167:     ORDER BY started_at DESC, id DESC
168:     LIMIT 1
169:   `).get() as JobRow | undefined;
170: 
171:   return row ? jobFromRow(row) : null;
172: }
173: 
174: function setJobStatus(
175:   db: Database.Database,
176:   jobId: string,
177:   status: ReindexJobStatus,
178:   processed?: number,
179:   error?: string,
180: ): void {
181:   const params = {
182:     jobId,
183:     status,
184:     updatedAt: nowIso(),
185:     processed,
186:     error,
187:   };
188: 
189:   if (typeof processed === 'number' && typeof error === 'string') {
190:     db.prepare(`
191:       UPDATE embedder_jobs
192:       SET status = @status,
193:           updated_at = @updatedAt,
194:           processed = @processed,
195:           error = @error
196:       WHERE id = @jobId
197:     `).run(params);
198:     return;
199:   }
200: 
201:   if (typeof processed === 'number') {
202:     // A per-batch progress write must NOT clobber a terminal status set
203:     // by a concurrent cancelJob(). The only caller that passes processed without an error
204:     // is the in-loop 'running' progress update, which must only apply while the job is
205:     // still 'running' (or being re-affirmed as 'running'); never resurrect a 'cancelled'
206:     // (or 'completed'/'failed') row back to 'running'. Terminal transitions (completed /
207:     // failed) always carry the other branches and stay unconditional.
208:     if (status === 'running') {
209:       db.prepare(`
210:         UPDATE embedder_jobs
211:         SET status = @status,
212:             updated_at = @updatedAt,
213:             processed = @processed
214:         WHERE id = @jobId
215:           AND status = 'running'
216:       `).run(params);
217:       return;
218:     }
219:     db.prepare(`
220:       UPDATE embedder_jobs
221:       SET status = @status,
222:           updated_at = @updatedAt,
223:           processed = @processed
224:       WHERE id = @jobId
225:     `).run(params);
226:     return;
227:   }
228: 
229:   if (typeof error === 'string') {
230:     db.prepare(`
231:       UPDATE embedder_jobs
232:       SET status = @status,
233:           updated_at = @updatedAt,
234:           error = @error
235:       WHERE id = @jobId
236:     `).run(params);
237:     return;
238:   }
239: 
240:   db.prepare(`
241:     UPDATE embedder_jobs
242:     SET status = @status,
243:         updated_at = @updatedAt
244:     WHERE id = @jobId
245:   `).run(params);
246: }
247: 
248: function selectMemoryBatch(db: Database.Database, offset: number, limit: number): MemoryRow[] {
249:   return db.prepare(`
250:     SELECT id, content_text, title, file_path
251:     FROM memory_index
252:     ORDER BY id ASC
253:     LIMIT ? OFFSET ?
254:   `).all(limit, offset) as MemoryRow[];
255: }
256: 
257: function memoryText(row: MemoryRow): string {
258:   if (typeof row.content_text === 'string' && row.content_text.length > 0) {
259:     return row.content_text;
260:   }
261:   if (typeof row.title === 'string' && row.title.length > 0) {
262:     return row.title;
263:   }
264:   return row.file_path ?? '';
265: }
266: 
267: function getBatchPair(
268:   rows: MemoryRow[],
269:   embeddings: Float32Array[],
270:   index: number,
271: ): { readonly row: MemoryRow; readonly embedding: Float32Array } {
272:   const row = rows[index];
273:   const embedding = embeddings[index];
274:   if (!row || !embedding) {
275:     const missing: string[] = [];
276:     if (!row) missing.push('row=undefined');
277:     if (!embedding) missing.push('embedding=undefined');
278:     throw new Error(
279:       `Embedding batch entry missing at index ${index}: ${missing.join(', ')} ` +
280:         `(rows.length=${rows.length}, embeddings.length=${embeddings.length})`,
281:     );
282:   }
283:   return { row, embedding };
284: }
285: 
286: function writeVectors(
287:   db: Database.Database,
288:   tableName: string,
289:   rows: MemoryRow[],
290:   embeddings: Float32Array[],
291: ): void {
292:   const writeBatch = db.transaction(() => {
293:     const stmt = db.prepare(`
294:       INSERT OR REPLACE INTO ${tableName} (id, vec)
295:       VALUES (?, ?)
296:     `);
297: 
298:     for (let i = 0; i < rows.length; i += 1) {
299:       const { row, embedding } = getBatchPair(rows, embeddings, i);
300:       stmt.run(row.id, to_embedding_buffer(embedding));
301:     }
302:   });
303: 
304:   writeBatch();
305: }
306: 
307: function getDatabaseDir(db: Database.Database): string | null {
308:   const row = (db.prepare('PRAGMA database_list').all() as Array<{ name?: string; file?: string }>)
309:     .find((entry) => entry.name === 'main');
310:   if (!row?.file || row.file === ':memory:') {
311:     return null;
312:   }
313:   return path.dirname(row.file);
314: }
315: 
316: function getDatabasePath(db: Database.Database): string | null {
317:   try {
318:     const row = (db.prepare('PRAGMA database_list').all() as Array<{ name?: string; file?: string }>)
319:       .find((entry) => entry.name === 'main');
320:     if (!row?.file || row.file === ':memory:') {
321:       return null;
322:     }
323:     return row.file;
324:   } catch (_error: unknown) {
325:     return null;
326:   }
327: }
328: 
329: function isClosedDatabaseHandleError(error: unknown): boolean {
330:   const message = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
331:   return /database connection is not open|closed database|database handle is closed/i.test(message);
332: }
333: 
334: function scheduleJobAfterRestore(jobId: string, dbPath: string | null): void {
335:   setTimeout(() => {
336:     // Do not re-open the database while a checkpoint restore is still in
337:     // progress: initialize_db runs interrupted-restore recovery, which would
338:     // roll back the in-flight restore. Wait for the barrier to clear.
339:     if (getRestoreBarrierStatus()) {
340:       scheduleJobAfterRestore(jobId, dbPath);
341:       return;
342:     }
343:     try {
344:       enqueueJob(dbPath ? initializeDb(dbPath) : initializeDb(), jobId);
345:     } catch (error: unknown) {
346:       logger.warn('embedder reindex restore retry skipped', {
347:         event: 'embedder_reindex_restore_retry_skipped',
348:         jobId,
349:         error: error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error),
350:       });
351:     }
352:   }, RESTORE_REQUEUE_DELAY_MS).unref?.();
353: }
354: 
355: function requireDatabaseDir(db: Database.Database, operation: string): string {
356:   const databaseDir = getDatabaseDir(db);
357:   if (!databaseDir) {
358:     throw new InvalidDatabaseDirError(
359:       `Embedder reindex ${operation} requires a file-backed database; received an in-memory or anonymous database without a database directory`,
360:     );
361:   }
362:   return databaseDir;
363: }
364: 
365: function writeVectorsToKnn(
366:   db: Database.Database,
367:   rows: MemoryRow[],
368:   embeddings: Float32Array[],
369: ): void {
370:   const writeBatch = db.transaction(() => {
371:     const del = db.prepare('DELETE FROM vec_memories WHERE rowid = ?');
372:     const ins = db.prepare('INSERT INTO vec_memories (rowid, embedding) VALUES (?, ?)');
373:     for (let i = 0; i < rows.length; i += 1) {
374:       const { row, embedding } = getBatchPair(rows, embeddings, i);
375:       del.run(BigInt(row.id));
376:       ins.run(BigInt(row.id), to_embedding_buffer(embedding));
377:     }
378:   });
379: 
380:   writeBatch();
381: }
382: 
383: function ensureShardSchema(shard: Database.Database, profile: EmbeddingProfile, tableName: string): void {
384:   shard.exec(`
385:     CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS vec_metadata (
386:       key TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
387:       value TEXT NOT NULL,
388:       created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
389:     );
390:     CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS ${tableName} (
391:       id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
392:       vec BLOB NOT NULL
393:     );
394:   `);
395: 
396:   const writeMetadata = shard.transaction(() => {
397:     const stmt = shard.prepare('INSERT OR REPLACE INTO vec_metadata (key, value) VALUES (?, ?)');
398:     stmt.run('provider', profile.provider);
399:     stmt.run('model', profile.model);
400:     stmt.run('dim', String(profile.dim));
401:     stmt.run('embedding_dim', String(profile.dim));
402:   });
403:   writeMetadata();
404: }
405: 
406: function writeVectorsToShard(
407:   db: Database.Database,
408:   profile: EmbeddingProfile,
409:   tableName: string,
410:   rows: MemoryRow[],
411:   embeddings: Float32Array[],
412:   shardPath?: string,
413: ): void {
414:   const databaseDir = requireDatabaseDir(db, 'vector shard write');
415: 
416:   // Writes target a caller-supplied STAGING shard path (default: the live
417:   // active shard for backward compatibility). runJob writes every batch to the staging
418:   // path and atomically renames staging -> active on success, so a mid-loop failure can
419:   // never leave the live (active) shard half-written.
420:   const shard = new Database(shardPath ?? profile.getVectorShardPath(databaseDir));
421:   try {
422:     shard.pragma('journal_mode = WAL');
423: 
424:     // Load sqlite-vec so the runtime KNN virtual table can be created
425:     // and populated alongside the canonical vec_<dim> blob table. When the extension
426:     // is unavailable the runtime falls back to vec_<dim> only and search degrades to
427:     // lexical signals; we surface that through the existing console.warn in vector-index-store.
428:     let vecAvailable = true;
429:     try {
430:       sqliteVec.load(shard);
431:     } catch (_err) {
432:       vecAvailable = false;
433:     }
434: 
435:     ensureShardSchema(shard, profile, tableName);
436: 
437:     if (vecAvailable) {
438:       shard.exec(`
439:         CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE IF NOT EXISTS vec_memories USING vec0(
440:           embedding FLOAT[${profile.dim}]
441:         )
442:       `);
443:     }
444: 
445:     writeVectors(shard, tableName, rows, embeddings);
446:     if (vecAvailable) {
447:       writeVectorsToKnn(shard, rows, embeddings);
448:     }
449:   } finally {
450:     // WAL-durability: flush + TRUNCATE the shard WAL before close so the shard file is
451:     // consistent at rest with an empty WAL — mirrors close_db's corruption-prevention.
452:     // better-sqlite3 .close() only does a passive checkpoint; an abrupt later kill could otherwise
453:     // corrupt un-checkpointed shard frames (vec0 / FTS segment writes). Best-effort; never block close.
454:     try { shard.pragma('wal_checkpoint(TRUNCATE)'); } catch (_err: unknown) { /* best-effort */ }
455:     shard.close();
456:   }
457: }
458: 
459: function enqueueJob(db: Database.Database, jobId: string): void {
460:   if (runningJobs.has(jobId)) {
461:     return;
462:   }
463: 
464:   runningJobs.add(jobId);
465:   void runJob(db, jobId).finally(() => {
466:     runningJobs.delete(jobId);
467:   });
468: }
469: 
470: async function runJob(db: Database.Database, jobId: string): Promise<void> {
471:   const dbPath = getDatabasePath(db);
472:   let jobDb = db;
473:   if (getRestoreBarrierStatus()) {
474:     scheduleJobAfterRestore(jobId, dbPath);
475:     return;
476:   }
477:   const resolveJobDb = (): Database.Database => {
478:     const barrierStatus = getRestoreBarrierStatus();
479:     if (barrierStatus) {
480:       const error = new Error(barrierStatus.message);
481:       error.name = barrierStatus.code;
482:       throw error;
483:     }
484:     return jobDb;
485:   };
486:   jobDb = resolveJobDb();
487:   const initialJob = selectJob(jobDb, jobId);
488:   if (!initialJob || initialJob.status === 'completed' || initialJob.status === 'failed' || initialJob.status === 'cancelled') {
489:     return;
490:   }
491: 
492:   const manifest = getManifest(initialJob.toName);
493:   if (!manifest) {
494:     setJobStatus(jobDb, jobId, 'failed', undefined, `UNKNOWN_EMBEDDER: ${initialJob.toName}`);
495:     return;
496:   }
497:   const adapter = getEmbedderAdapter(manifest.backend, initialJob.toName, initialJob.toDim);
498:   const targetProfile = new EmbeddingProfile({
499:     provider: manifest.backend,
500:     model: manifest.name,
501:     dim: manifest.dim,
502:     dtype: null,
503:     baseUrl: null,
504:   });
505: 
506:   ensureVecTableForDim(jobDb, initialJob.toDim);
507:   const tableName = vecTableNameForDim(initialJob.toDim);
508:   const batchSize = getBatchSize();
509:   let processed = initialJob.processed;
510: 
511:   // Stage every shard write into a per-job staging file, then atomically
512:   // rename it over the live active shard ONLY after the completion transaction commits.
513:   // A mid-loop throw (or cancel) leaves the live (active) shard untouched; the partial
514:   // staging artifact is unlinked in the catch/cancel paths. databaseDir is guaranteed
515:   // file-backed here (startReindexInternal already called requireDatabaseDir).
516:   const databaseDir = requireDatabaseDir(jobDb, 'reindex staging');
517:   const activeShardPath = targetProfile.getVectorShardPath(databaseDir);
518:   const stagingShardPath = `${activeShardPath}.reindex-${jobId}.staging`;
519: 
520:   const cleanupStaging = (): void => {
521:     for (const suffix of ['', '-wal', '-shm']) {
522:       try {
523:         fs.unlinkSync(`${stagingShardPath}${suffix}`);
524:       } catch (_err: unknown) {
525:         /* best-effort: staging may not exist */
526:       }
527:     }
528:   };
529: 
530:   try {
531:     setJobStatus(jobDb, jobId, 'running');
532:     // Start each run from a clean staging slate (a prior crashed run may have left one).
533:     cleanupStaging();
534: 
535:     while (processed < initialJob.total) {
536:       jobDb = resolveJobDb();
537:       // Re-read the live cancel/status before each batch so cancelJob()
538:       // during a run stops the worker before the next write. Abort cleanly: drop the
539:       // staging artifact and leave the live shard + active pointer untouched.
540:       if (readJobStatus(jobDb, jobId) === 'cancelled') {
541:         cleanupStaging();
542:         return;
543:       }
544: 
545:       const rows = selectMemoryBatch(jobDb, processed, batchSize);
546:       if (rows.length === 0) {
547:         break;
548:       }
549: 
550:       const embeddings = await adapter.embed(rows.map(memoryText));
551:       jobDb = resolveJobDb();
552:       if (embeddings.length !== rows.length) {
553:         throw new Error(`Embedder returned ${embeddings.length} embeddings for ${rows.length} memories`);
554:       }
555: 
556:       writeVectors(jobDb, tableName, rows, embeddings);
557:       writeVectorsToShard(jobDb, targetProfile, tableName, rows, embeddings, stagingShardPath);
558:       processed += rows.length;
559:       // Clobber-safe: only advances while status is still 'running' (never resurrects
560:       // a concurrently-set 'cancelled').
561:       setJobStatus(jobDb, jobId, 'running', processed);
562:       await new Promise<void>((resolve) => {
563:         setImmediate(resolve);
564:       });
565:     }
566: 
567:     // Final cancel checkpoint before the irreversible swap/commit.
568:     jobDb = resolveJobDb();
569:     if (readJobStatus(jobDb, jobId) === 'cancelled') {
570:       cleanupStaging();
571:       return;
572:     }
573: 
574:     // Atomically swap the staged shard over the live active shard BEFORE the
575:     // completion transaction flips the active-embedder pointer. rename(2) is atomic on
576:     // the same filesystem; the staging file always lives beside the active shard, so the
577:     // active shard either is the old file (failure) or the fully-staged new file (success),
578:     // never a half-written mix. If no batch produced a staging file (e.g. zero rows), skip.
579:     if (fs.existsSync(stagingShardPath)) {
580:       // Drop any stale sidecars next to the active shard so the renamed WAL/SHM (already
581:       // TRUNCATE-checkpointed at staging close) is the authoritative pair.
582:       for (const suffix of ['-wal', '-shm']) {
583:         try {
584:           fs.unlinkSync(`${activeShardPath}${suffix}`);
585:         } catch (_err: unknown) {
586:           /* best-effort */
587:         }
588:       }
589:       fs.renameSync(stagingShardPath, activeShardPath);
590:     }
591: 
592:     const complete = jobDb.transaction(() => {
593:       // Persist the active-embedder provider pointer too; omitting it leaves the pointer empty,
594:       // which readActiveEmbedderIfValid coerces to undefined and the next boot loses provider
595:       // identity. 'api' backends are ambiguous (openai vs voyage) from the manifest alone, so
596:       // leave the provider unset for those rather than guess.
597:       const activeProvider = manifest.backend === 'ollama'
598:         ? 'ollama'
599:         : manifest.backend === 'sentence-transformers'
600:           ? 'hf-local'
601:           : undefined;
602:       setActiveEmbedder(jobDb, initialJob.toName, initialJob.toDim, activeProvider);
603:       // Commit embedding_status for rows now backed by an active-profile vector.
604:       // A vector-only reindex previously wrote vectors but left memory_index.embedding_status
605:       // stale, so a "completed" bulk re-embed never raised the success count. Reconcile the
606:       // status for every row present in the just-written vec_<dim> table, inside the same
607:       // atomic completion transaction so vectors and status flip together.
608:       const completedAt = nowIso();
609:       jobDb.prepare(`
610:         UPDATE memory_index
611:         SET embedding_status = 'success',
612:             embedding_generated_at = COALESCE(embedding_generated_at, @ts),
613:             updated_at = @ts,
614:             failure_reason = NULL
615:         WHERE embedding_status != 'success'
616:           AND id IN (SELECT id FROM ${tableName})
617:       `).run({ ts: completedAt });
618:       setJobStatus(jobDb, jobId, 'completed', initialJob.total);
619:     });
620:     complete();
621:     // The active-embedder pointer just flipped; drop the cached provider singleton so the
622:     // next embedding re-resolves against the new pointer instead of the stale model/dim.
623:     invalidateProviderSingleton();
624:     void teardownEmbedderAfterSwap(manifest.backend, initialJob.toName).catch((error: unknown) => {
625:       console.warn(`[embedder-reindex] embedder teardown after swap failed: ${error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error)}`);
626:     });
627:     if (getDatabaseDir(jobDb)) {
628:       attachActiveVectorShard(jobDb, targetProfile);
629:     }
630:   } catch (error: unknown) {
631:     if (getRestoreBarrierStatus() || isClosedDatabaseHandleError(error)) {
632:       cleanupStaging();
633:       scheduleJobAfterRestore(jobId, dbPath);
634:       logger.warn('embedder reindex paused for checkpoint restore', {
635:         event: 'embedder_reindex_restore_paused',
636:         jobId,
637:         processed,
638:       });
639:       return;
640:     }
641:     // A failed run must not leave a half-written shard anywhere. The live active
642:     // shard was never mutated (all writes went to staging); discard the partial staging
643:     // artifact so the next attempt starts clean and the active shard stays the last-good one.
644:     cleanupStaging();
645:     const message = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
646:     setJobStatus(jobDb, jobId, 'failed', processed, message);
647:     logger.error('embedder reindex job failed', {
648:       event: 'embedder_reindex_failed',
649:       jobId,
650:       toName: initialJob.toName,
651:       processed,
652:       total: initialJob.total,
653:       error: message,
654:     });
655:   }
656: }
657: 
658: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
659: // 4. CORE LOGIC
660: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
661: 
662: export function startReindex(
663:   options: StartReindexOptions,
664:   runtimeOptions: ReindexRuntimeOptions = {},
665: ): string {
666:   return startReindexInternal(options, runtimeOptions);
667: }
668: 
669: function startReindexInternal(
670:   options: StartReindexOptions,
671:   runtimeOptions: ReindexInternalRuntimeOptions = {},
672: ): string {
673:   const db = resolveDb(runtimeOptions.db);
674:   requireDatabaseDir(db, 'startup');
675:   ensureJobTable(db);
676: 
677:   const manifest = getManifest(options.toName);
678:   if (!manifest) {
679:     const error = new Error(`UNKNOWN_EMBEDDER: ${options.toName}`);
680:     error.name = 'UNKNOWN_EMBEDDER';
681:     throw error;
682:   }
683: 
684:   ensureVecTableForDim(db, manifest.dim);
685:   const active = getActiveEmbedder(db);
686:   const totalRow = db.prepare('SELECT COUNT(*) AS count FROM memory_index').get() as { count?: number } | undefined;
687:   const total = Number(totalRow?.count ?? 0);
688:   const timestamp = nowIso();
689:   const id = `emb-swap-${timestamp.replace(/[:.]/g, '-')}-${randomUUID().slice(0, 8)}`;
690: 
691:   db.prepare(`
692:     INSERT INTO embedder_jobs (
693:       id, from_name, to_name, to_dim, total, processed, status, started_at, updated_at, error
694:     ) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, 0, 'queued', ?, ?, NULL)
695:   `).run(id, active.name, manifest.name, manifest.dim, total, timestamp, timestamp);
696: 
697:   if (runtimeOptions.autoStart !== false) {
698:     enqueueJob(db, id);
699:   }
700: 
701:   return id;
702: }
703: 
704: export function getJobStatus(jobId: string, db?: Database.Database): ReindexJob | null {
705:   return selectJob(resolveDb(db), jobId);
706: }
707: 
708: export function getActiveJob(db?: Database.Database): ReindexJob | null {
709:   return selectActiveJob(resolveDb(db));
710: }
711: 
712: export function cancelJob(jobId: string, db?: Database.Database): ReindexJob | null {
713:   const resolvedDb = resolveDb(db);
714:   ensureJobTable(resolvedDb);
715:   const job = selectJob(resolvedDb, jobId);
716:   if (!job) {
717:     return null;
718:   }
719: 
720:   if (job.status === 'completed' || job.status === 'failed' || job.status === 'cancelled') {
721:     return job;
722:   }
723: 
724:   setJobStatus(resolvedDb, jobId, 'cancelled');
725:   return selectJob(resolvedDb, jobId);
726: }
727: 
728: export function resumeReindexJobs(db?: Database.Database): ReindexJob[] {
729:   const resolvedDb = resolveDb(db);
730:   requireDatabaseDir(resolvedDb, 'resume');
731:   ensureJobTable(resolvedDb);
732:   const rows = resolvedDb.prepare(`
733:     SELECT ${JOB_SELECT_COLUMNS}
734:     FROM embedder_jobs
735:     WHERE status IN ('queued', 'running')
736:     ORDER BY started_at ASC, id ASC
737:   `).all() as JobRow[];
738: 
739:   const jobs = rows.map(jobFromRow);
740:   for (const job of jobs) {
741:     enqueueJob(resolvedDb, job.id);
742:   }
743:   return jobs;
744: }
745: 
746: export function startReindexForTest(
747:   options: StartReindexOptions,
748:   runtimeOptions: ReindexInternalRuntimeOptions = {},
749: ): string {
750:   return startReindexInternal(options, runtimeOptions);
751: }
752: 
753: export function estimateEta(job: ReindexJob | null): number | null {
754:   if (!job || job.status === 'completed') {
755:     return 0;
756:   }
757:   if (job.processed <= 0 || job.total <= 0) {
758:     return null;
759:   }
760: 
761:   const elapsedMs = Date.now() - Date.parse(job.startedAt);
762:   if (!Number.isFinite(elapsedMs) || elapsedMs <= 0) {
763:     return null;
764:   }
765: 
766:   const ratePerMs = job.processed / elapsedMs;
767:   if (ratePerMs <= 0) {
768:     return null;
769:   }
770: 
771:   return Math.ceil((job.total - job.processed) / ratePerMs / 1000);
772: }

(End of file - total 772 lines)

1: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
2: // MODULE: Bm25 Index
3: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
4: // Feature catalog: BM25 trigger phrase re-index gate
5: import type Database from 'better-sqlite3';
6: import { normalizeContentForBM25 } from '../parsing/content-normalizer.js';
7: import {
8:   normalizeLexicalQueryTokens,
9:   sanitizeFTS5Query,
10:   sanitizeQueryTokens,
11:   simpleStem,
12:   tokenize,
13: } from './lexical-normalizer.js';
14: import type { NormalizedLexicalQueryTokens } from './lexical-normalizer.js';
15: import { clearRegisteredTimer, registerTimeout } from '../runtime/timer-registry.js';
16: 
17: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
18: // 1. INTERFACES
19: 
20: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
21: interface BM25SearchResult {
22:   id: string;
23:   /**
24:    * BM25 term-frequency relevance score (unbounded, typically 0-25+).
25:    * Higher = better lexical match. Not directly comparable to vector similarity
26:    * scores; use min-max normalization or RRF when combining with other methods.
27:    */
28:   score: number;
29: }
30: 
31: interface BM25Stats {
32:   documentCount: number;
33:   termCount: number;
34:   avgDocLength: number;
35: }
36: 
37: interface BM25DocumentSource {
38:   title?: string | null;
39:   content_text?: string | null;
40:   trigger_phrases?: string | string[] | null;
41:   file_path?: string | null;
42: }
43: 
44: /* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
45:    1B. CONSTANTS & FEATURE FLAG
46:    ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
47: 
48: const DEFAULT_K1 = 1.2;
49: const DEFAULT_B = 0.75;
50: const BM25_WARMUP_BATCH_SIZE = 250;
51: const BM25_ENABLED_VALUES = new Set(['1', 'true', 'yes', 'on', 'experimental', 'fallback']);
52: const BM25_DISABLED_VALUES = new Set(['0', 'false', 'no', 'off']);
53: const BM25_ENGINE_VALUES = ['auto', 'sqlite', 'packed-inmemory', 'legacy-inmemory'] as const;
54: type Bm25Engine = typeof BM25_ENGINE_VALUES[number];
55: let packedInmemoryWarningEmitted = false;
56: 
57: /**
58:  * Field weight multipliers for weighted BM25 scoring.
59:  * When indexing a document composed of multiple fields, multiply
60:  * each field's token scores by the appropriate weight before
61:  * accumulating the total document score.
62:  *
63:  * These weights are consumed by the FTS5 path in sqlite-fts.ts,
64:  * not the in-memory BM25 engine in this file. Exported for shared access.
65:  *
66:  * title:           10.0 — exact title matches are the strongest signal
67:  * trigger_phrases:  5.0 — curated keywords next most important
68:  * content_generic:  2.0 — generic textual content (file_path, tags, etc.)
69:  * body:             1.0 — baseline weight for full body / content_text
70:  */
71: const BM25_FTS5_WEIGHTS = [10.0, 5.0, 2.0, 1.0] as const;
72: 
73: const BM25_FIELD_WEIGHTS: Record<string, number> = {
74:   title: BM25_FTS5_WEIGHTS[0],
75:   trigger_phrases: BM25_FTS5_WEIGHTS[1],
76:   content_generic: BM25_FTS5_WEIGHTS[2],
77:   body: BM25_FTS5_WEIGHTS[3],
78: };
79: 
80: /**
81:  * Check whether the in-memory BM25 index is enabled.
82:  *
83:  * @returns `true` when BM25 indexing/search is enabled for the current process.
84:  * @example
85:  * ```ts
86:  * if (isBm25Enabled()) {
87:  *   getIndex();
88:  * }
89:  * ```
90:  */
91: function isBm25Enabled(): boolean {
92:   const value = process.env.ENABLE_BM25?.trim().toLowerCase();
93:   if (!value) return true; // enabled by default
94:   if (BM25_DISABLED_VALUES.has(value)) return false;
95:   return BM25_ENABLED_VALUES.has(value);
96: }
97: 
98: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
99: // 2. HELPERS
100: 
101: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
102: /**
103:  * Count token frequency occurrences for BM25 scoring.
104:  *
105:  * @param tokens - Normalized tokens produced by {@link tokenize}.
106:  * @returns A frequency map keyed by token text.
107:  * @example
108:  * ```ts
109:  * getTermFrequencies(['memory', 'memory', 'search']).get('memory');
110:  * // 2
111:  * ```
112:  */
113: function getTermFrequencies(tokens: string[]): Map<string, number> {
114:   const freq = new Map<string, number>();
115:   for (const token of tokens) {
116:     freq.set(token, (freq.get(token) || 0) + 1);
117:   }
118:   return freq;
119: }
120: 
121: function normalizeTriggerPhrasesForBM25(triggerPhrases: string | string[] | null | undefined): string {
122:   if (Array.isArray(triggerPhrases)) {
123:     return triggerPhrases
124:       .filter((phrase): phrase is string => typeof phrase === 'string' && phrase.trim().length > 0)
125:       .join(' ');
126:   }
127: 
128:   if (typeof triggerPhrases !== 'string') {
129:     return '';
130:   }
131: 
132:   const trimmed = triggerPhrases.trim();
133:   if (!trimmed) {
134:     return '';
135:   }
136: 
137:   try {
138:     const parsed = JSON.parse(trimmed) as unknown;
139:     if (Array.isArray(parsed)) {
140:       return parsed
141:         .filter((phrase): phrase is string => typeof phrase === 'string' && phrase.trim().length > 0)
142:         .join(' ');
143:     }
144:   } catch {
145:     // Fall back to raw string when the database value is not JSON.
146:   }
147: 
148:   return trimmed;
149: }
150: 
151: function buildBm25DocumentText(row: BM25DocumentSource): string {
152:   const textParts: string[] = [];
153: 
154:   if (typeof row.title === 'string' && row.title.trim()) {
155:     textParts.push(row.title.trim());
156:   }
157: 
158:   if (typeof row.content_text === 'string' && row.content_text.trim()) {
159:     textParts.push(normalizeContentForBM25(row.content_text));
160:   }
161: 
162:   const triggerPhrases = normalizeTriggerPhrasesForBM25(row.trigger_phrases);
163:   if (triggerPhrases) {
164:     textParts.push(triggerPhrases);
165:   }
166: 
167:   if (typeof row.file_path === 'string' && row.file_path.trim()) {
168:     textParts.push(row.file_path.trim());
169:   }
170: 
171:   return textParts.join(' ').trim();
172: }
173: 
174: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
175: // 3. BM25 INDEX CLASS
176: 
177: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
178: class BM25Index {
179:   private k1: number;
180:   private b: number;
181:   private documents: Map<string, { tokens: string[]; termFreq: Map<string, number> }>;
182:   private documentFreq: Map<string, number>;
183:   private totalDocLength: number;
184:   private warmupHandle: ReturnType<typeof setTimeout> | null;
185:   private warmupGeneration: number;
186: 
187:   constructor(k1: number = DEFAULT_K1, b: number = DEFAULT_B) {
188:     this.k1 = k1;
189:     this.b = b;
190:     this.documents = new Map();
191:     this.documentFreq = new Map();
192:     this.totalDocLength = 0;
193:     this.warmupHandle = null;
194:     this.warmupGeneration = 0;
195:   }
196: 
197:   addDocument(id: string, text: string): void {
198:     const tokens = tokenize(text);
199:     const termFreq = getTermFrequencies(tokens);
200: 
201:     // Remove old document if exists
202:     if (this.documents.has(id)) {
203:       this.removeDocument(id);
204:     }
205: 
206:     this.documents.set(id, { tokens, termFreq });
207:     this.totalDocLength += tokens.length;
208: 
209:     // Update document frequency
210:     for (const term of termFreq.keys()) {
211:       this.documentFreq.set(term, (this.documentFreq.get(term) || 0) + 1);
212:     }
213:   }
214: 
215:   removeDocument(id: string): boolean {
216:     const doc = this.documents.get(id);
217:     if (!doc) return false;
218: 
219:     this.totalDocLength -= doc.tokens.length;
220: 
221:     for (const term of doc.termFreq.keys()) {
222:       const count = this.documentFreq.get(term) || 0;
223:       if (count <= 1) {
224:         this.documentFreq.delete(term);
225:       } else {
226:         this.documentFreq.set(term, count - 1);
227:       }
228:     }
229: 
230:     this.documents.delete(id);
231:     return true;
232:   }
233: 
234:   calculateIdf(term: string): number {
235:     const n = this.documents.size;
236:     const df = this.documentFreq.get(term) || 0;
237:     if (df === 0 || n === 0) return 0;
238:     return Math.log((n - df + 0.5) / (df + 0.5) + 1);
239:   }
240: 
241:   calculateScore(queryTokens: string[], docId: string): number {
242:     const doc = this.documents.get(docId);
243:     if (!doc) return 0;
244: 
245:     const avgDl = this.documents.size > 0 ? this.totalDocLength / this.documents.size : 0;
246:     const dl = doc.tokens.length;
247: 
248:     let score = 0;
249:     for (const term of queryTokens) {
250:       const tf = doc.termFreq.get(term) || 0;
251:       if (tf === 0) continue;
252: 
253:       const idf = this.calculateIdf(term);
254:       const numerator = tf * (this.k1 + 1);
255:       const denominator = tf + this.k1 * (1 - this.b + this.b * (dl / Math.max(avgDl, 1)));
256:       score += idf * (numerator / denominator);
257:     }
258: 
259:     return score;
260:   }
261: 
262:   search(query: string, limit: number = 10): BM25SearchResult[] {
263:     const queryTokens = normalizeLexicalQueryTokens(query).bm25;
264:     if (queryTokens.length === 0) return [];
265: 
266:     const results: BM25SearchResult[] = [];
267: 
268:     for (const [id] of this.documents) {
269:       const score = this.calculateScore(queryTokens, id);
270:       if (score > 0) {
271:         results.push({ id, score });
272:       }
273:     }
274: 
275:     return results
276:       .sort((a, b) => b.score - a.score)
277:       .slice(0, limit);
278:   }
279: 
280:   getStats(): BM25Stats {
281:     return {
282:       documentCount: this.documents.size,
283:       termCount: this.documentFreq.size,
284:       avgDocLength: this.documents.size > 0 ? this.totalDocLength / this.documents.size : 0,
285:     };
286:   }
287: 
288:   clear(): void {
289:     this.cancelWarmup();
290:     this.documents.clear();
291:     this.documentFreq.clear();
292:     this.totalDocLength = 0;
293:   }
294: 
295:   addDocuments(docs: Array<{ id: string; text: string }>): void {
296:     for (const doc of docs) {
297:       this.addDocument(doc.id, doc.text);
298:     }
299:   }
300: 
301:   /**
302:    * Incrementally synchronize changed rows from the database into the in-memory index.
303:    */
304:   syncChangedRows(database: Database.Database, rowIds: Array<number | string>): number {
305:     const normalizedIds = Array.from(
306:       new Set(
307:         rowIds
308:           .map((rowId) => Number(rowId))
309:           .filter((rowId) => Number.isInteger(rowId) && rowId > 0)
310:       )
311:     );
312: 
313:     if (normalizedIds.length === 0) {
314:       return 0;
315:     }
316: 
317:     try {
318:       const placeholders = normalizedIds.map(() => '?').join(', ');
319:       const rows = (database.prepare(
320:         `SELECT id, title, content_text, trigger_phrases, file_path
321:          FROM memory_index
322:          WHERE id IN (${placeholders})`
323:       ) as Database.Statement).all(...normalizedIds) as Array<{
324:         id: number;
325:         title: string | null;
326:         content_text: string | null;
327:         trigger_phrases: string | null;
328:         file_path: string | null;
329:       }>;
330: 
331:       const activeRowMap = new Map<number, typeof rows[number]>();
332:       for (const row of rows) {
333:         activeRowMap.set(row.id, row);
334:       }
335: 
336:       for (const rowId of normalizedIds) {
337:         const row = activeRowMap.get(rowId);
338:         if (!row) {
339:           this.removeDocument(String(rowId));
340:           continue;
341:         }
342: 
343:         const text = buildBm25DocumentText(row);
344:         if (text.trim()) {
345:           this.addDocument(String(row.id), text);
346:         } else {
347:           this.removeDocument(String(row.id));
348:         }
349:       }
350: 
351:       return normalizedIds.length;
352:     } catch (error: unknown) {
353:       const msg = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
354:       console.warn(`[bm25-index] Failed to sync BM25 rows: ${msg}`);
355:       return 0;
356:     }
357:   }
358: 
359:   /**
360:    * Defer full startup warmup into batched row-ID syncs so process
361:    * initialization is not blocked by a monolithic in-memory rebuild.
362:    */
363:   rebuildFromDatabase(database: Database.Database): number {
364:     this.clear();
365: 
366:     try {
367:       const rows = (database.prepare(
368:         `SELECT id
369:          FROM memory_index
370:          ORDER BY id`
371:       ) as Database.Statement).all() as Array<{ id: number }>;
372: 
373:       const pendingIds = rows.map((row) => row.id);
374:       if (pendingIds.length === 0) {
375:         return 0;
376:       }
377: 
378:       const warmupGeneration = ++this.warmupGeneration;
379:       const processBatch = () => {
380:         if (warmupGeneration !== this.warmupGeneration) {
381:           return;
382:         }
383: 
384:         const batchIds = pendingIds.splice(0, BM25_WARMUP_BATCH_SIZE);
385:         if (batchIds.length === 0) {
386:           this.warmupHandle = null;
387:           return;
388:         }
389: 
390:         this.syncChangedRows(database, batchIds);
391: 
392:         if (pendingIds.length > 0) {
393:           this.warmupHandle = registerTimeout(processBatch, 0, { unref: true });
394:         } else {
395:           this.warmupHandle = null;
396:         }
397:       };
398: 
399:       this.warmupHandle = registerTimeout(processBatch, 0, { unref: true });
400:       return pendingIds.length;
401:     } catch (error: unknown) {
402:       const msg = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
403:       console.warn(`[bm25-index] Failed to schedule BM25 warmup: ${msg}`);
404:       return 0;
405:     }
406:   }
407: 
408:   cancelWarmup(): void {
409:     this.warmupGeneration += 1;
410:     if (this.warmupHandle) {
411:       clearRegisteredTimer(this.warmupHandle);
412:       this.warmupHandle = null;
413:     }
414:   }
415: }
416: 
417: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
418: // 4. SINGLETON
419: 
420: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
421: let indexInstance: BM25Index | null = null;
422: 
423: /**
424:  * Retrieve the shared in-memory BM25 index singleton.
425:  *
426:  * @returns The process-wide {@link BM25Index} instance.
427:  * @example
428:  * ```ts
429:  * const index = getIndex();
430:  * index.addDocument('123', 'memory search');
431:  * ```
432:  */
433: function getIndex(): BM25Index {
434:   if (!indexInstance) {
435:     indexInstance = new BM25Index();
436:   }
437:   return indexInstance;
438: }
439: 
440: /**
441:  * Reset the shared BM25 index singleton.
442:  *
443:  * @returns Nothing.
444:  * @example
445:  * ```ts
446:  * resetIndex();
447:  * ```
448:  */
449: function resetIndex(): void {
450:   indexInstance?.cancelWarmup();
451:   indexInstance = null;
452: }
453: 
454: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
455: // 5. LEXICAL ENGINE ROUTING
456: 
457: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
458: function resolveBm25Engine(): Bm25Engine {
459:   const value = process.env.SPECKIT_BM25_ENGINE?.trim().toLowerCase();
460:   if (!value) {
461:     return 'auto';
462:   }
463: 
464:   if ((BM25_ENGINE_VALUES as readonly string[]).includes(value)) {
465:     return value as Bm25Engine;
466:   }
467: 
468:   console.warn(`[bm25-index] Unsupported SPECKIT_BM25_ENGINE=${value}; falling back to auto`);
469:   return 'auto';
470: }
471: 
472: function isMemoryFtsAvailable(database: Database.Database | null | undefined): boolean {
473:   if (!database) {
474:     return false;
475:   }
476: 
477:   try {
478:     const result = (database.prepare(
479:       `SELECT name FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name='memory_fts'`
480:     ) as Database.Statement).get() as { name: string } | undefined;
481:     return !!result;
482:   } catch {
483:     return false;
484:   }
485: }
486: 
487: function emitPackedInmemoryWarning(): void {
488:   if (packedInmemoryWarningEmitted) {
489:     return;
490:   }
491:   packedInmemoryWarningEmitted = true;
492:   console.warn('[bm25-index] SPECKIT_BM25_ENGINE=packed-inmemory is reserved and not yet implemented; using legacy-inmemory');
493: }
494: 
495: function shouldWarmInMemoryBm25(database: Database.Database | null | undefined): boolean {
496:   if (!isBm25Enabled()) {
497:     return false;
498:   }
499: 
500:   const engine = resolveBm25Engine();
501:   if (engine === 'sqlite') {
502:     if (!isMemoryFtsAvailable(database)) {
503:       throw new Error('SPECKIT_BM25_ENGINE=sqlite requires the memory_fts table; JS BM25 warmup is disabled in sqlite mode');
504:     }
505:     return false;
506:   }
507: 
508:   if (engine === 'auto') {
509:     return !isMemoryFtsAvailable(database);
510:   }
511: 
512:   if (engine === 'packed-inmemory') {
513:     emitPackedInmemoryWarning();
514:     return true;
515:   }
516: 
517:   return true;
518: }
519: 
520: function shouldUseSqliteLexicalEngine(database: Database.Database | null | undefined): boolean {
521:   const engine = resolveBm25Engine();
522:   if (engine === 'sqlite') {
523:     if (!isMemoryFtsAvailable(database)) {
524:       throw new Error('SPECKIT_BM25_ENGINE=sqlite requires the memory_fts table for lexical BM25 search');
525:     }
526:     return true;
527:   }
528: 
529:   if (engine === 'auto') {
530:     return isMemoryFtsAvailable(database);
531:   }
532: 
533:   if (engine === 'packed-inmemory') {
534:     emitPackedInmemoryWarning();
535:   }
536: 
537:   return false;
538: }
539: 
540: function getBm25EngineStatus(database: Database.Database | null | undefined = null): {
541:   lexical_engine: Bm25Engine;
542:   fts5_available: boolean;
543:   warms_in_memory_bm25: boolean;
544:   bm25_enabled: boolean;
545: } {
546:   const lexical_engine = resolveBm25Engine();
547:   const fts5_available = isMemoryFtsAvailable(database);
548:   let warms_in_memory_bm25 = false;
549: 
550:   try {
551:     warms_in_memory_bm25 = shouldWarmInMemoryBm25(database);
552:   } catch {
553:     warms_in_memory_bm25 = false;
554:   }
555: 
556:   return {
557:     lexical_engine,
558:     fts5_available,
559:     warms_in_memory_bm25,
560:     bm25_enabled: isBm25Enabled(),
561:   };
562: }

<content>
1: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
2: // MODULE: DB Shard Migration
3: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
4: 
5: import * as fs from 'fs';
6: import * as path from 'path';
7: 
8: import Database from 'better-sqlite3';
9: import * as sqliteVec from 'sqlite-vec';
10: 
11: import type { EmbeddingProfile } from '@spec-kit/shared/embeddings/profile';
12: 
13: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
14: // 1. CONSTANTS
15: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
16: 
17: const SHARD_EXACT_TABLES = new Set(['vec_memories', 'embedding_cache']);
18: const CANONICAL_SKIP_PREFIXES = ['vec_memories'];
19: 
20: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
21: // 2. HELPERS
22: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
23: 
24: function quoteSqlString(value: string): string {
25:   return `'${value.replace(/'/g, "''")}'`;
26: }
27: 
28: function quoteIdent(value: string): string {
29:   return `"${value.replace(/"/g, '""')}"`;
30: }
31: 
32: function listUserTables(db: Database.Database, schema = 'main'): string[] {
33:   const tableRows = db.prepare(`
34:     SELECT name
35:     FROM ${quoteIdent(schema)}.sqlite_master
36:     WHERE type IN ('table', 'view')
37:       AND name NOT LIKE 'sqlite_%'
38:     ORDER BY name
39:   `).all() as Array<{ name: string }>;
40:   return tableRows.map((row) => row.name);
41: }
42: 
43: function isShardTable(tableName: string): boolean {
44:   return SHARD_EXACT_TABLES.has(tableName)
45:     || tableName.startsWith('vec_')
46:     || CANONICAL_SKIP_PREFIXES.some((prefix) => tableName.startsWith(`${prefix}_`));
47: }
48: 
49: function copyTable(db: Database.Database, fromSchema: string, toSchema: string, tableName: string): void {
50:   const source = `${quoteIdent(fromSchema)}.${quoteIdent(tableName)}`;
51:   const target = `${quoteIdent(toSchema)}.${quoteIdent(tableName)}`;
52:   db.exec(`DROP TABLE IF EXISTS ${target}`);
53:   db.exec(`CREATE TABLE ${target} AS SELECT * FROM ${source}`);
54: }
55: 
56: function getTableColumns(db: Database.Database, schema: string, tableName: string): Set<string> {
57:   const columns = db.prepare(`PRAGMA ${quoteIdent(schema)}.table_info(${quoteIdent(tableName)})`).all() as Array<{ name: string }>;
58:   return new Set(columns.map((column) => column.name));
59: }
60: 
61: function copyShardPayloadTable(
62:   db: Database.Database,
63:   tableName: string,
64:   profile: EmbeddingProfile,
65: ): void {
66:   if (tableName.startsWith('vec_memories_')) {
67:     return;
68:   }
69:   if (tableName === 'vec_memories') {
70:     db.exec('DROP TABLE IF EXISTS shard.vec_memories');
71:     db.exec(`
72:       CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE shard.vec_memories USING vec0(
73:         embedding FLOAT[${profile.dim}]
74:       )
75:     `);
76:     const rows = db.prepare('SELECT rowid, embedding FROM legacy.vec_memories').all() as Array<{ rowid: number; embedding: Buffer }>;
77:     const insert = db.prepare('INSERT INTO shard.vec_memories(rowid, embedding) VALUES (?, ?)');
78:     for (const row of rows) {
79:       insert.run(BigInt(row.rowid), row.embedding);
80:     }
81:     return;
82:   }
83:   if (tableName === 'embedding_cache') {
84:     const columns = getTableColumns(db, 'legacy', 'embedding_cache');
85:     const profileKeyExpr = columns.has('profile_key') ? "COALESCE(profile_key, '')" : "''";
86:     const inputKindExpr = columns.has('input_kind') ? "COALESCE(input_kind, 'document')" : "'document'";
87:     db.exec('DROP TABLE IF EXISTS shard.embedding_cache');
88:     db.exec(`
89:       CREATE TABLE shard.embedding_cache (
90:         content_hash TEXT NOT NULL,
91:         profile_key TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
92:         input_kind TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'document' CHECK (input_kind IN ('document', 'query')),
93:         model_id TEXT NOT NULL,
94:         embedding BLOB NOT NULL,
95:         dimensions INTEGER NOT NULL,
96:         created_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now')),
97:         last_used_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now')),
98:         PRIMARY KEY (content_hash, profile_key, input_kind, model_id, dimensions)
99:       )
100:     `);
101:     db.exec(`
102:       INSERT OR REPLACE INTO shard.embedding_cache (
103:         content_hash, profile_key, input_kind, model_id, embedding, dimensions, created_at, last_used_at
104:       )
105:       SELECT
106:         content_hash,
107:         ${profileKeyExpr},
108:         ${inputKindExpr},
109:         model_id,
110:         embedding,
111:         dimensions,
112:         created_at,
113:         last_used_at
114:       FROM legacy.embedding_cache
115:     `);
116:     return;
117:   }
118:   copyTable(db, 'legacy', 'shard', tableName);
119: }
120: 
121: function dropCanonicalShardPayloadTables(db: Database.Database): void {
122:   const tables = listUserTables(db, 'main');
123:   for (const tableName of tables) {
124:     if (!isShardTable(tableName) || tableName === 'vec_metadata') {
125:       continue;
126:     }
127:     try {
128:       db.exec(`DROP TABLE IF EXISTS main.${quoteIdent(tableName)}`);
129:     } catch (_error: unknown) {
130:       // Best-effort canonical slimming; shard copy above is authoritative.
131:     }
132:   }
133: }
134: 
135: function moveWalCompanions(sourcePath: string, backupPath: string): void {
136:   for (const suffix of ['-wal', '-shm']) {
137:     const sourceCompanion = `${sourcePath}${suffix}`;
138:     if (!fs.existsSync(sourceCompanion)) {
139:       continue;
140:     }
141:     fs.renameSync(sourceCompanion, `${backupPath}${suffix}`);
142:   }
143: }
144: 
145: function legacyHasVectorTables(legacyPath: string): boolean {
146:   if (!fs.existsSync(legacyPath)) {
147:     return false;
148:   }
149: 
150:   const legacy = new Database(legacyPath, { readonly: true, fileMustExist: true });
151:   try {
152:     const row = legacy.prepare(`
153:       SELECT 1 AS found
154:       FROM sqlite_master
155:       WHERE type IN ('table', 'view')
156:         AND (name = 'vec_memories' OR name LIKE 'vec\\_%' ESCAPE '\\')
157:       LIMIT 1
158:     `).get() as { found?: number } | undefined;
159:     return row?.found === 1;
160:   } finally {
161:     legacy.close();
162:   }
163: }
164: 
165: function readShardMetadata(shardPath: string): Record<string, string> | null {
166:   if (!fs.existsSync(shardPath)) {
167:     return null;
168:   }
169: 
170:   const shard = new Database(shardPath, { readonly: true, fileMustExist: true });
171:   try {
172:     const hasMetadata = shard.prepare(`
173:       SELECT 1 AS found
174:       FROM sqlite_master
175:       WHERE type='table' AND name='vec_metadata'
176:       LIMIT 1
177:     `).get() as { found?: number } | undefined;
178:     if (hasMetadata?.found !== 1) {
179:       return null;
180:     }
181:     const rows = shard.prepare('SELECT key, value FROM vec_metadata').all() as Array<{ key: string; value: string }>;
182:     return Object.fromEntries(rows.map((row) => [row.key, row.value]));
183:   } finally {
184:     shard.close();
185:   }
186: }
187: 
188: function verifyExistingShard(profile: EmbeddingProfile, shardPath: string): void {
189:   const metadata = readShardMetadata(shardPath);
190:   if (!metadata) {
191:     return;
192:   }
193:   const mismatches = [
194:     ['provider', profile.provider],
195:     ['model', profile.model],
196:     ['dim', String(profile.dim)],
197:   ].filter(([key, expected]) => metadata[key] && metadata[key] !== expected);
198: 
199:   if (mismatches.length > 0) {
200:     const detail = mismatches.map(([key, expected]) => `${key}: expected ${expected}, got ${metadata[key]}`).join('; ');
201:     throw new Error(`Vector shard metadata mismatch for ${shardPath}: ${detail}`);
202:   }
203: }
204: 
205: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
206: // 3. CORE LOGIC
207: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
208: 
209: export function migrateLegacySingleDbToShardSync(
210:   legacyPath: string,
211:   canonicalPath: string,
212:   shardPath: string,
213:   profile: EmbeddingProfile,
214: ): void {
215:   if (!legacyHasVectorTables(legacyPath)) {
216:     return;
217:   }
218: 
219:   if (fs.existsSync(canonicalPath) && fs.existsSync(shardPath)) {
220:     verifyExistingShard(profile, shardPath);
221:     console.error(`[db-shard-migration] canonical and shard already exist for ${profile.slug}; skipping migration`);
222:     return;
223:   }
224: 
225:   fs.mkdirSync(path.dirname(canonicalPath), { recursive: true, mode: 0o700 });
226:   fs.mkdirSync(path.dirname(shardPath), { recursive: true, mode: 0o700 });
227: 
228:   console.error(`[db-shard-migration] migrating legacy profile DB: ${legacyPath}`);
229:   if (!fs.existsSync(canonicalPath)) {
230:     fs.copyFileSync(legacyPath, canonicalPath);
231:     for (const suffix of ['-wal', '-shm']) {
232:       if (fs.existsSync(`${legacyPath}${suffix}`)) {
233:         fs.copyFileSync(`${legacyPath}${suffix}`, `${canonicalPath}${suffix}`);
234:       }
235:     }
236:   }
237: 
238:   const canonical = new Database(canonicalPath);
239:   try {
240:     canonical.pragma('journal_mode = WAL');
241:     canonical.pragma('busy_timeout = 10000');
242:     sqliteVec.load(canonical);
243:     canonical.exec(`ATTACH DATABASE ${quoteSqlString(legacyPath)} AS legacy`);
244:     canonical.exec(`ATTACH DATABASE ${quoteSqlString(shardPath)} AS shard`);
245:     canonical.exec('PRAGMA shard.journal_mode = WAL');
246: 
247:     const legacyTables = listUserTables(canonical, 'legacy');
248:     for (const tableName of legacyTables) {
249:       if (tableName === 'vec_metadata') {
250:         continue;
251:       }
252:       if (isShardTable(tableName)) {
253:         copyShardPayloadTable(canonical, tableName, profile);
254:       }
255:     }
256: 
257:     canonical.exec(`
258:       DROP TABLE IF EXISTS shard.vec_metadata;
259:       CREATE TABLE shard.vec_metadata (
260:         key TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
261:         value TEXT NOT NULL,
262:         created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
263:       );
264:       INSERT OR REPLACE INTO shard.vec_metadata (key, value) VALUES
265:         ('provider', ${quoteSqlString(profile.provider)}),
266:         ('model', ${quoteSqlString(profile.model)}),
267:         ('dim', ${quoteSqlString(String(profile.dim))}),
268:         ('embedding_dim', ${quoteSqlString(String(profile.dim))});
269:     `);
270:     dropCanonicalShardPayloadTables(canonical);
271: 
272:     canonical.exec('DETACH DATABASE shard');
273:     canonical.exec('DETACH DATABASE legacy');
274:     canonical.exec('VACUUM');
275:   } finally {
276:     canonical.close();
277:   }
278: 
279:   const shard = new Database(shardPath);
280:   try {
281:     shard.pragma('journal_mode = WAL');
282:     shard.exec('VACUUM');
283:   } finally {
284:     shard.close();
285:   }
286: 
287:   const migrationDir = path.join(path.dirname(canonicalPath), 'migrations');
288:   fs.mkdirSync(migrationDir, { recursive: true, mode: 0o700 });
289:   const timestamp = new Date().toISOString().replace(/[-:.TZ]/g, '').slice(0, 14);
290:   const backupPath = path.join(migrationDir, `legacy_${profile.slug}_${timestamp}.sqlite.bak`);
291:   fs.renameSync(legacyPath, backupPath);
292:   moveWalCompanions(legacyPath, backupPath);
293:   console.error(`[db-shard-migration] legacy DB moved to rollback backup: ${backupPath}`);
294: }
295: 
296: export async function migrateLegacySingleDbToShard(
297:   legacyPath: string,
298:   canonicalPath: string,
299:   shardPath: string,
300:   profile: EmbeddingProfile,
301: ): Promise<void> {
302:   migrateLegacySingleDbToShardSync(legacyPath, canonicalPath, shardPath, profile);
303: }

(End of file - total 303 lines)
</content>

1: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
2: // MODULE: Checkpoints

4: // Feature catalog: Checkpoint creation (checkpoint_create)
5: // Feature catalog: Checkpoint restore (checkpoint_restore)
6: // Gzip-compressed database checkpoints with embedding preservation

18: import { rebuildAutoEntities } from '../extraction/entity-extractor.js';

23:   hasScopeConstraints,
24:   normalizeScopeContext,

27:   type ScopeContext,
28:   type GovernanceAuditEntry,
29:   type TierDowngradeAuditParams,

31: import { detectCommunities, storeCommunityAssignments } from '../graph/community-detection.js';
32: import { generateCommunitySummaries } from '../graph/community-summaries.js';
33: import { storeCommunities } from '../graph/community-storage.js';
34: import { snapshotDegrees } from '../graph/graph-signals.js';

36: import { isIndexableConstitutionalMemoryPath, shouldIndexForMemory } from '../utils/index-scope.js';

40: 
41: function batchedInQuery<T>(db: Database.Database, sql: string, ids: (number | string)[], batchSize = 500): T[] {
42:   const results: T[] = [];
43:   for (let i = 0; i < ids.length; i += batchSize) {
44:     const batch = ids.slice(i, i + batchSize);
45:     const placeholders = batch.map(() => '?').join(', ');
46:     const batchSql = sql.replace('__PLACEHOLDERS__', placeholders);
47:     results.push(...(db.prepare(batchSql).all(...batch) as T[]));
48:   }
49:   return results;
50: }
51: 
52: function dedupeRowsById<T extends Record<string, unknown>>(rows: T[]): T[] {
53:   const results: T[] = [];
54:   const seenIds = new Set<number | string>();
55: 
56:   for (const row of rows) {
57:     const id = row.id;
58:     if (typeof id === 'number' || typeof id === 'string') {
59:       if (seenIds.has(id)) {
60:         continue;
61:       }
62:       seenIds.add(id);
63:     }
64:     results.push(row);
65:   }
66: 
67:   return results;
68: }
69: 
70: function getSnapshotColumnsFromRows(rows: Array<Record<string, unknown>>): string[] {
71:   const columns = new Set<string>();
72: 
73:   for (const row of rows) {
74:     for (const key of Object.keys(row ?? {})) {
75:       if (typeof key === 'string' && key.length > 0) {
76:         columns.add(key);
77:       }
78:     }
79:   }
80: 
81:   return Array.from(columns);
82: }
83: 
84: function flushGovernanceAudits(
85:   database: Database.Database,
86:   entries: GovernanceAuditEntry[],
87: ): void {
88:   for (const entry of entries) {
89:     try {
90:       recordGovernanceAudit(database, entry);
91:     } catch (error: unknown) {
92:       console.warn(`[checkpoints] governance_audit insert failed: ${toErrorMessage(error)}`);
93:     }
94:   }
95: }
96: 
97: function flushTierDowngradeAudits(
98:   database: Database.Database,
99:   entries: TierDowngradeAuditParams[],
100: ): void {
101:   for (const entry of entries) {
102:     try {
103:       recordTierDowngradeAudit(database, entry);
104:     } catch (error: unknown) {
105:       console.warn(`[checkpoints] governance_audit insert failed: ${toErrorMessage(error)}`);
106:     }
107:   }
108: }
109: 
110: /* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
111:    1. CONSTANTS
112: ----------------------------------------------------------------*/
113: 
114: const MAX_CHECKPOINTS = 10;
115: const CHECKPOINT_CREATE_MAX_ATTEMPTS = 3;
116: const CHECKPOINT_CREATE_RETRY_MIN_DELAY_MS = 50;
117: const CHECKPOINT_CREATE_RETRY_MAX_DELAY_MS = 200;
118: const RESTORE_JOURNAL_NAME = '.restore-journal.json';

120: 
121: const CHECKPOINT_MANIFEST = Object.freeze({

139:     'session_sent_memories',
140:     'memory_summaries',
141:   ],

143:     'memory_lineage',

145:     'memory_entities',

147:     'degree_snapshots',

159: });

231:   | 'CHECKPOINT_CREATE_INVALID_NAME'
232:   | 'CHECKPOINT_CREATE_PERMISSION_DENIED'
233:   | 'CHECKPOINT_CREATE_FAILED';
234: 
235: type CheckpointRestoreErrorCode =
236:   | 'CHECKPOINT_RESTORE_MANIFEST_INVALID'
237:   | 'CHECKPOINT_RESTORE_DOWNGRADE_UNSAFE'
238:   | 'CHECKPOINT_RESTORE_EMBEDDER_MISMATCH'
239:   | 'CHECKPOINT_RESTORE_FILE_MISSING'
240:   | 'CHECKPOINT_RESTORE_PATH_UNRESOLVED'
241:   | 'CHECKPOINT_RESTORE_JOURNAL_FAILED'
242:   | 'CHECKPOINT_RESTORE_SWAP_FAILED';
243: 
244: class CheckpointCreateError extends Error {
245:   public readonly code: CheckpointCreateErrorCode;
246:   public readonly originalCode: string | null;
247:   public readonly details: Record<string, unknown>;
248: 
249:   constructor(
250:     code: CheckpointCreateErrorCode,
251:     message: string,
252:     details: Record<string, unknown> = {},
253:     cause?: unknown,
254:   ) {
255:     super(message, { cause });
256:     Object.setPrototypeOf(this, CheckpointCreateError.prototype);
257:     this.name = 'CheckpointCreateError';
258:     this.code = code;
259:     this.originalCode = typeof details.originalCode === 'string' ? details.originalCode : null;
260:     this.details = details;
261:   }
262: }
263: 
264: class CheckpointRestoreError extends Error {
265:   public readonly code: CheckpointRestoreErrorCode;
266: 
267:   constructor(code: CheckpointRestoreErrorCode, message: string, cause?: unknown) {
268:     super(message, { cause });
269:     Object.setPrototypeOf(this, CheckpointRestoreError.prototype);
270:     this.name = 'CheckpointRestoreError';
271:     this.code = code;
272:   }
273: }
