403: // Validate the decompressed checkpoint snapshot before
404: // restore. Previously the gzipped blob was JSON.parsed and cast directly
405: // to CheckpointSnapshot — malformed memory_index rows propagated through
406: // `getMemoryIds()` and the INSERT loops. This schema validates the
407: // outer envelope and the array shapes; individual row shapes are still
408: // permissive (sqlite columns evolve over time) but each row must be a
409: // non-null object so downstream column extraction doesn't NPE.
410: const CheckpointTableSnapshotSchema = z.object({
411:   columns: z.array(z.string()),
412:   rows: z.array(z.record(z.string(), z.unknown())),
413: });
414: 
415: const CheckpointVectorRowSchema = z.object({
416:   rowid: z.number(),
417:   embedding: z.unknown(), // Buffer or null — preserved as-is
418: });
419: 
420: const CheckpointSnapshotSchema = z.object({
421:   manifest: z.object({
422:     snapshot: z.array(z.string()),
423:     rebuild: z.array(z.string()),
424:     skip: z.array(z.string()),
425:   }).optional(),
426:   tables: z.record(z.string(), CheckpointTableSnapshotSchema).optional(),
427:   memories: z.array(z.record(z.string(), z.unknown())),
428:   workingMemory: z.array(z.record(z.string(), z.unknown())),
429:   vectors: z.array(CheckpointVectorRowSchema).optional(),
430:   causalEdges: z.array(z.record(z.string(), z.unknown())).optional(),
431:   timestamp: z.string(),
432: }).passthrough();
433: 
434: interface RestoreBarrierStatus {
435:   code: string;
436:   message: string;
437: }
438: 
439: interface RestoreBarrierHooks {
440:   afterAcquire?: (() => void) | null;
441:   beforeRelease?: (() => void) | null;
442: }
443: 
444: /* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
445:    3. MODULE STATE
446: ----------------------------------------------------------------*/
447: 
448: let db: Database.Database | null = null;
449: let restoreInProgress = false;
450: let restoreBarrierHooks: RestoreBarrierHooks = {};
451: 
452: const RESTORE_IN_PROGRESS_ERROR_CODE = 'E_RESTORE_IN_PROGRESS';
453: const RESTORE_IN_PROGRESS_ERROR_MESSAGE = 'Checkpoint restore maintenance is in progress. Retry after the restore lifecycle completes.';
454: 
455: /* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
456:    4. INITIALIZATION
457: ----------------------------------------------------------------*/
458: 
459: function init(database: Database.Database): void {
460:   db = database;
461: }
462: 
463: function getDatabase(): Database.Database {
464:   if (!db) throw new Error('Database not initialized. The checkpoints module requires the MCP server to be running. Restart the MCP server and retry.');
465:   return db;
466: }
467: 
468: function getErrorCode(error: unknown): string | null {
469:   if (typeof error === 'object' && error !== null && 'code' in error) {
470:     const code = (error as { code?: unknown }).code;
471:     return typeof code === 'string' ? code : null;
472:   }
473:   return null;
474: }
475: 
476: function isCheckpointBusyError(error: unknown): boolean {
477:   const code = getErrorCode(error);
478:   const message = toErrorMessage(error);
479:   return code === 'SQLITE_BUSY'
480:     || code === 'SQLITE_LOCKED'
481:     || /\bSQLITE_BUSY\b|\bSQLITE_LOCKED\b|database is locked/i.test(message);
482: }
483: 
484: function classifyCheckpointCreateError(
485:   error: unknown,
486:   context: Record<string, unknown> = {},
487: ): CheckpointCreateError {
488:   if (error instanceof CheckpointCreateError) {
489:     return error;
490:   }
491: 
492:   const originalCode = getErrorCode(error);
493:   const message = toErrorMessage(error);
494:   const details = {
495:     ...context,
496:     ...(originalCode ? { originalCode } : {}),
497:     originalMessage: message,
498:   };
499: 
500:   if (originalCode === 'SQLITE_BUSY' || /\bSQLITE_BUSY\b|database is locked/i.test(message)) {
501:     return new CheckpointCreateError(
502:       'CHECKPOINT_CREATE_SQLITE_BUSY',
503:       'Checkpoint creation failed because the SQLite database was busy. Retry after current writes finish.',
504:       details,
505:       error,
506:     );
507:   }
508: 
509:   if (originalCode === 'SQLITE_LOCKED' || /\bSQLITE_LOCKED\b/i.test(message)) {
510:     return new CheckpointCreateError(
511:       'CHECKPOINT_CREATE_SQLITE_LOCKED',
512:       'Checkpoint creation failed because the SQLite database was locked by another operation.',
513:       details,
514:       error,
515:     );
516:   }
517: 
518:   if (/UNIQUE constraint failed: checkpoints\.name|constraint failed/i.test(message)) {
519:     return new CheckpointCreateError(
520:       'CHECKPOINT_CREATE_DUPLICATE_NAME',
521:       'Checkpoint creation failed because a checkpoint with this name already exists.',
522:       details,
523:       error,
524:     );
525:   }
526: 
527:   if (
528:     originalCode === 'EACCES'
529:     || originalCode === 'EPERM'
530:     || originalCode === 'SQLITE_READONLY'
531:     || /permission denied|readonly|read-only/i.test(message)
532:   ) {
533:     return new CheckpointCreateError(
534:       'CHECKPOINT_CREATE_PERMISSION_DENIED',
535:       'Checkpoint creation failed because the database path is not writable.',
536:       details,
537:       error,
538:     );
539:   }
540: 
541:   return new CheckpointCreateError(
542:     'CHECKPOINT_CREATE_FAILED',
543:     `Checkpoint creation failed: ${message}`,
544:     details,
545:     error,
546:   );
547: }
548: 
549: function checkpointRetryDelayMs(): number {
550:   return CHECKPOINT_CREATE_RETRY_MIN_DELAY_MS
551:     + Math.floor(Math.random() * (CHECKPOINT_CREATE_RETRY_MAX_DELAY_MS - CHECKPOINT_CREATE_RETRY_MIN_DELAY_MS + 1));
552: }
553: 
554: function sleepSync(ms: number): void {
555:   Atomics.wait(new Int32Array(new SharedArrayBuffer(4)), 0, 0, ms);
556: }
557: 
558: function isRestoreInProgress(): boolean {
559:   return restoreInProgress;
560: }
561: 
562: function getRestoreBarrierStatus(): RestoreBarrierStatus | null {
563:   if (!restoreInProgress) {
564:     return null;
565:   }
566: 
567:   return {
568:     code: RESTORE_IN_PROGRESS_ERROR_CODE,
569:     message: RESTORE_IN_PROGRESS_ERROR_MESSAGE,
570:   };
571: }
572: 
573: function setRestoreBarrierHooks(hooks: RestoreBarrierHooks | null): void {
574:   restoreBarrierHooks = hooks ?? {};
575: }
576: 
577: function acquireRestoreBarrier(): void {
578:   restoreInProgress = true;
579:   restoreBarrierHooks.afterAcquire?.();
580: }
581: 
582: function releaseRestoreBarrier(): void {
583:   try {
584:     restoreBarrierHooks.beforeRelease?.();
585:   } finally {
586:     restoreInProgress = false;
587:   }
588: }
589: 
590: /* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
591:    5. HELPERS
592: ----------------------------------------------------------------*/
593: 
594: function getGitBranch(): string | null {
595:   const candidates = [
596:     process.env.GIT_BRANCH,
597:     process.env.BRANCH_NAME,
598:     process.env.CI_COMMIT_REF_NAME,
599:     process.env.VERCEL_GIT_COMMIT_REF,
600:   ];
601: 
602:   for (const candidate of candidates) {
603:     if (typeof candidate === 'string' && candidate.trim().length > 0) {
604:       return candidate.trim();
605:     }
606:   }
607: 
608:   return null;
609: }
610: 
611: function quoteSqlString(value: string): string {
612:   return `'${value.replace(/'/g, "''")}'`;
613: }
614: 
615: function getDatabaseList(database: Database.Database): DatabaseListRow[] {
616:   return database.prepare('PRAGMA database_list').all() as DatabaseListRow[];
617: }
618: 
619: function sanitizeCheckpointName(name: string): string {
620:   const sanitized = name.trim();
621:   if (
622:     sanitized.length === 0
623:     || sanitized.length > 128
624:     || sanitized.includes('/')
625:     || sanitized.includes('\\')
626:     || sanitized.includes('..')
627:     || sanitized.includes('\0')
628:   ) {
629:     throw new CheckpointCreateError(
630:       'CHECKPOINT_CREATE_INVALID_NAME',
631:       'Checkpoint creation failed because the checkpoint name is invalid.',
632:       { name },
633:     );
634:   }
635:   return sanitized;
636: }
637: 
638: function resolveCheckpointDir(database: Database.Database, name: string): string {
639:   const sanitizedName = sanitizeCheckpointName(name);
640:   const mainRow = getDatabaseList(database).find((row) => row.name === 'main');
641:   const mainFile = mainRow?.file;
642:   if (!mainFile || mainFile === ':memory:') {
643:     throw new CheckpointCreateError(
644:       'CHECKPOINT_CREATE_FAILED',
645:       'Checkpoint creation failed because the main database file path could not be resolved.',
646:       { name: sanitizedName },
647:     );
648:   }
649:   return path.join(path.dirname(mainFile), 'checkpoints', sanitizedName);
650: }
651: 
652: function getActiveVectorShardPath(database: Database.Database): string | null {
653:   const activeVec = getDatabaseList(database).find((row) => row.name === 'active_vec');
654:   return activeVec?.file && activeVec.file.length > 0 ? activeVec.file : null;
655: }
656: 
657: function deriveEmbedderSlug(shardPath: string | null): string | null {
658:   if (!shardPath) {
659:     return null;
660:   }
661:   const match = /^context-vectors__(.+)\.sqlite$/.exec(path.basename(shardPath));
662:   return match?.[1] ?? null;
663: }
664: 
665: function sweepStaleCheckpointTmpDirs(checkpointsDir: string): void {
666:   if (!fs.existsSync(checkpointsDir)) {
667:     return;
668:   }
669:   for (const entry of fs.readdirSync(checkpointsDir, { withFileTypes: true })) {
670:     if (entry.isDirectory() && entry.name.includes('.tmp-')) {
671:       fs.rmSync(path.join(checkpointsDir, entry.name), { recursive: true, force: true });
672:     }
673:   }
674: }
675: 
676: function resolvePathSafe(targetPath: string): string {
677:   try {
678:     return fs.realpathSync(targetPath);
679:   } catch (_error: unknown) {
680:     return path.resolve(targetPath);
681:   }
682: }
683: 
684: function sweepOrphanCheckpointSnapshotDirs(database: Database.Database, checkpointsDir: string): void {
685:   // Checkpoint pruning removes the catalog row inside the create transaction but deletes
686:   // the snapshot dir AFTER commit. A crash in that gap leaves a snapshot dir with no
687:   // catalog row — never swept by the .tmp-* sweep (it has a published name) and never
688:   // restorable. Reconcile on-disk v2 snapshot dirs against the live catalog and remove
689:   // any that no catalog row references. Best-effort: never throw out of the create path.
690:   try {
691:     if (!fs.existsSync(checkpointsDir) || !tableExists(database, 'checkpoints')) {
692:       return;
693:     }
694: 
695:     const checkpointColumns = new Set(getTableColumns(database, 'checkpoints'));
696:     if (!checkpointColumns.has('snapshot_path')) {
697:       return;
698:     }
699:     const referenced = new Set<string>();
700:     const rows = database.prepare(
701:       "SELECT snapshot_path FROM checkpoints WHERE snapshot_path IS NOT NULL AND snapshot_path <> ''",
702:     ).all() as Array<{ snapshot_path?: string | null }>;
703:     for (const row of rows) {
704:       if (typeof row.snapshot_path === 'string' && row.snapshot_path.length > 0) {
705:         referenced.add(resolvePathSafe(row.snapshot_path));
706:       }
707:     }
708: 
709:     for (const entry of fs.readdirSync(checkpointsDir, { withFileTypes: true })) {
710:       if (!entry.isDirectory() || entry.name.includes('.tmp-')) {
711:         continue;
712:       }
713:       const entryPath = path.join(checkpointsDir, entry.name);
714:       if (referenced.has(resolvePathSafe(entryPath))) {
715:         continue;
716:       }
717:       try {
718:         fs.rmSync(entryPath, { recursive: true, force: true });
719:         console.warn(`[checkpoints] Swept orphan checkpoint snapshot dir with no catalog row: ${entry.name}`);
720:       } catch (_removeError: unknown) {
721:         // Best-effort: a single un-removable dir must not block checkpoint creation.
722:       }
723:     }
724:   } catch (error: unknown) {
725:     console.warn(`[checkpoints] Orphan checkpoint snapshot sweep skipped (non-fatal): ${toErrorMessage(error)}`);
726:   }
727: }
728: 
729: function readSchemaVersion(database: Database.Database): number | null {
730:   try {
731:     const row = database.prepare('SELECT version FROM schema_version WHERE id = 1').get() as { version?: number } | undefined;
732:     return typeof row?.version === 'number' ? row.version : null;
733:   } catch (_error: unknown) {
734:     return null;
735:   }
736: }
737: 
738: function countTableRows(database: Database.Database, tableName: string): number {
739:   if (!tableExists(database, tableName)) {
740:     return 0;
741:   }
742:   const row = database.prepare(`SELECT COUNT(*) AS count FROM ${tableName}`).get() as { count?: number } | undefined;
743:   return typeof row?.count === 'number' ? row.count : 0;
744: }
745: 
746: function countActiveVectorRows(database: Database.Database): number {
747:   try {
748:     const row = database.prepare(`
749:       SELECT 1 AS found
750:       FROM active_vec.sqlite_master
751:       WHERE type IN ('table', 'view') AND name = 'vec_memories'
752:       LIMIT 1
753:     `).get() as { found?: number } | undefined;
754:     if (row?.found !== 1) {
755:       return 0;
756:     }
757:     const countRow = database.prepare('SELECT COUNT(*) AS count FROM active_vec.vec_memories').get() as { count?: number } | undefined;
758:     return typeof countRow?.count === 'number' ? countRow.count : 0;
759:   } catch (_error: unknown) {
760:     return 0;
761:   }
762: }
763: 
764: function readCheckpointV2Manifest(snapshotPath: string | null): CheckpointV2Manifest | null {
765:   if (!snapshotPath) {
766:     return null;
767:   }
768:   try {
769:     const raw = fs.readFileSync(path.join(snapshotPath, 'manifest.json'), 'utf-8');
770:     const parsed = JSON.parse(raw) as Partial<CheckpointV2Manifest>;
771:     return parsed.formatVersion === 2 ? parsed as CheckpointV2Manifest : null;
772:   } catch (_error: unknown) {
773:     return null;
774:   }
775: }
776: 
777: function loadCheckpointV2Manifest(snapshotPath: string | null): CheckpointV2Manifest {
778:   if (!snapshotPath) {
779:     throw new CheckpointRestoreError(
780:       'CHECKPOINT_RESTORE_MANIFEST_INVALID',
781:       'Checkpoint v2 restore failed because snapshot_path is missing.',
782:     );
783:   }
784: 
785:   const manifestPath = path.join(snapshotPath, 'manifest.json');
786:   try {
787:     const raw = fs.readFileSync(manifestPath, 'utf-8');
788:     const parsed = JSON.parse(raw) as Partial<CheckpointV2Manifest>;
789:     if (
790:       parsed.formatVersion !== 2
791:       || typeof parsed.includeEmbeddings !== 'boolean'
792:       || !Array.isArray(parsed.mainTables)
793:       || !Array.isArray(parsed.vecTables)
794:       || typeof parsed.schemaVersion !== 'number'
795:       || typeof parsed.memoryCount !== 'number'
796:       || typeof parsed.vectorCount !== 'number'
797:     ) {
798:       throw new Error('manifest shape is invalid');
799:     }
800:     return parsed as CheckpointV2Manifest;
801:   } catch (error: unknown) {
802:     throw new CheckpointRestoreError(
803:       'CHECKPOINT_RESTORE_MANIFEST_INVALID',
804:       `Checkpoint v2 restore failed because manifest.json could not be read: ${toErrorMessage(error)}`,
805:       error,
806:     );
807:   }
808: }
809: 
810: function resolveMainDatabasePath(database: Database.Database): string {
811:   const mainRow = getDatabaseList(database).find((row) => row.name === 'main');
812:   const mainFile = mainRow?.file;
813:   if (!mainFile || mainFile === ':memory:') {
814:     throw new CheckpointRestoreError(
815:       'CHECKPOINT_RESTORE_PATH_UNRESOLVED',
816:       'Checkpoint v2 restore failed because the live main database path could not be resolved.',
817:     );
818:   }
819:   return mainFile;
820: }
821: 
822: function removeSqliteSidecars(databasePath: string): void {
823:   for (const sidecarPath of [`${databasePath}-wal`, `${databasePath}-shm`]) {
824:     fs.rmSync(sidecarPath, { force: true });
825:   }
826: }
827: 
828: function getRestoreJournalPath(liveMainPath: string): string {
829:   return path.join(path.dirname(liveMainPath), 'checkpoints', RESTORE_JOURNAL_NAME);
830: }
831: 
832: function getNeedsRebuildSentinelPath(liveMainPath: string): string {
833:   return path.join(path.dirname(liveMainPath), 'checkpoints', NEEDS_REBUILD_SENTINEL_NAME);
834: }
835: 
836: function fsyncFileIfPossible(filePath: string): void {
837:   let fd: number | null = null;
838:   try {
839:     fd = fs.openSync(filePath, 'r');
840:     fs.fsyncSync(fd);
841:   } catch (_error: unknown) {
842:     // Best-effort durability; the next boot still treats any visible journal as authoritative.
843:   } finally {
844:     if (fd !== null) {
845:       try { fs.closeSync(fd); } catch (_error: unknown) { /* best-effort */ }
846:     }
847:   }
848: }
849: 
850: function fsyncDirectoryIfPossible(dirPath: string): void {
851:   let fd: number | null = null;
852:   try {
853:     fd = fs.openSync(dirPath, 'r');
854:     fs.fsyncSync(fd);
855:   } catch (_error: unknown) {
856:     // Directory fsync is unavailable on some platforms/filesystems.
857:   } finally {
858:     if (fd !== null) {
859:       try { fs.closeSync(fd); } catch (_error: unknown) { /* best-effort */ }
860:     }
861:   }
862: }
863: 
864: function writeRestoreJournal(journalPath: string, journal: RestoreJournalFile): void {
865:   const journalDir = path.dirname(journalPath);
866:   const tempJournalPath = `${journalPath}.tmp`;
867:   fs.mkdirSync(journalDir, { recursive: true, mode: 0o700 });
868:   fs.writeFileSync(tempJournalPath, `${JSON.stringify(journal, null, 2)}\n`, { mode: 0o600 });
869:   fsyncFileIfPossible(tempJournalPath);
870:   fs.renameSync(tempJournalPath, journalPath);
871:   fsyncDirectoryIfPossible(journalDir);
872: }
873: 
874: function clearRestoreJournal(journalPath: string): void {
875:   fs.rmSync(journalPath, { force: true });
876:   fsyncDirectoryIfPossible(path.dirname(journalPath));
877: }
878: 
879: function writeNeedsRebuildSentinelAtPath(
880:   sentinelPath: string,
881:   options: NeedsRebuildSentinelWriteOptions,
882: ): void {
883:   const sentinelDir = path.dirname(sentinelPath);
884:   const tempSentinelPath = `${sentinelPath}.tmp`;
885:   const sentinel: NeedsRebuildSentinelFile = {
886:     formatVersion: 1,
887:     createdAt: new Date().toISOString(),
888:     source: options.source,
889:     reason: options.reason,
890:     ...(options.summary ? { rebuildSummary: options.summary } : {}),
891:   };
892: 
893:   fs.mkdirSync(sentinelDir, { recursive: true, mode: 0o700 });
894:   fs.writeFileSync(tempSentinelPath, `${JSON.stringify(sentinel, null, 2)}\n`, { mode: 0o600 });
895:   fsyncFileIfPossible(tempSentinelPath);
896:   fs.renameSync(tempSentinelPath, sentinelPath);
897:   fsyncDirectoryIfPossible(sentinelDir);
898: }
899: 
900: function clearNeedsRebuildSentinelAtPath(sentinelPath: string): boolean {
901:   const existed = fs.existsSync(sentinelPath);
902:   fs.rmSync(sentinelPath, { force: true });
903:   if (existed) {
904:     fsyncDirectoryIfPossible(path.dirname(sentinelPath));
905:   }
906:   return existed;
907: }

959: function readCheckpointCatalogRows(database: Database.Database): Array<Record<string, unknown>> {
960:   if (!tableExists(database, 'checkpoints')) {
961:     return [];
962:   }
963:   return database.prepare('SELECT * FROM checkpoints ORDER BY id ASC').all() as Array<Record<string, unknown>>;
964: }
965: 
966: function mergeCheckpointCatalogRows(database: Database.Database, rows: Array<Record<string, unknown>>): void {
967:   if (rows.length === 0 || !tableExists(database, 'checkpoints')) {
968:     return;
969:   }
970: 
971:   const targetColumns = new Set(getTableColumns(database, 'checkpoints'));
972:   for (const row of rows) {
973:     const name = row.name;
974:     if (typeof name !== 'string' || name.length === 0) {
975:       continue;
976:     }
977:     const existingByName = database.prepare('SELECT id FROM checkpoints WHERE name = ?').get(name) as { id?: number } | undefined;
978:     if (existingByName?.id) {
979:       continue;
980:     }
981: 
982:     let columns = Object.keys(row).filter((column) => targetColumns.has(column));
983:     const id = row.id;
984:     if (typeof id === 'number') {
985:       const existingById = database.prepare('SELECT name FROM checkpoints WHERE id = ?').get(id) as { name?: string } | undefined;
986:       if (existingById?.name && existingById.name !== name) {
987:         columns = columns.filter((column) => column !== 'id');
988:       }
989:     }
990:     if (columns.length === 0) {
991:       continue;
992:     }
993: 
994:     const placeholders = columns.map(() => '?').join(', ');
995:     database.prepare(`
996:       INSERT OR IGNORE INTO checkpoints (${columns.join(', ')})
997:       VALUES (${placeholders})
998:     `).run(...columns.map((column) => row[column]));
999:   }
1000: }
1001: 
1002: function statPathBytes(targetPath: string): number {
1003:   try {
1004:     const stats = fs.statSync(targetPath);
1005:     if (stats.isFile()) {
1006:       return stats.size;
1007:     }
1008:     if (!stats.isDirectory()) {
1009:       return 0;
1010:     }
1011:     return fs.readdirSync(targetPath).reduce((total, entry) => total + statPathBytes(path.join(targetPath, entry)), 0);
1012:   } catch (_error: unknown) {
1013:     return 0;
1014:   }
1015: }
1016: 
1017: function getCheckpointV2SnapshotSize(snapshotPath: string | null): number {
1018:   const manifest = readCheckpointV2Manifest(snapshotPath);
1019:   if (manifest) {
1020:     return manifest.mainBytes + manifest.vecBytes;
1021:   }
1022:   return snapshotPath ? statPathBytes(snapshotPath) : 0;
1023: }

1025: function removeCheckpointSnapshotDirs(snapshotPaths: string[]): void {
1026:   for (const snapshotPath of snapshotPaths) {
1027:     try {
1028:       fs.rmSync(snapshotPath, { recursive: true, force: true });
1029:     } catch (_error: unknown) {
1030:       // Best-effort cleanup; the checkpoint row is already gone.
1031:     }
1032:   }
1033: }

1035: function collectPrunedCheckpointSnapshotPaths(database: Database.Database, pruneCount: number): string[] {
1036:   if (pruneCount <= 0) {
1037:     return [];
1038:   }
1039:   const checkpointColumns = new Set(getTableColumns(database, 'checkpoints'));
1040:   const snapshotPathSelect = checkpointColumns.has('snapshot_path') ? 'snapshot_path' : 'NULL AS snapshot_path';
1041:   const snapshotFormatSelect = checkpointColumns.has('snapshot_format') ? 'snapshot_format' : "'v1' AS snapshot_format";
1042:   const rows = database.prepare(`
1043:     SELECT id, ${snapshotPathSelect}, ${snapshotFormatSelect}
1044:     FROM checkpoints
1045:     ORDER BY created_at ASC, id ASC
1046:     LIMIT ?
1047:   `).all(pruneCount) as Array<{ id: number; snapshot_path?: string | null; snapshot_format?: string | null }>;
1048: 
1049:   if (rows.length === 0) {
1050:     return [];
1051:   }
1052:   const ids = rows.map((row) => row.id);
1053:   const placeholders = ids.map(() => '?').join(', ');
1054:   database.prepare(`DELETE FROM checkpoints WHERE id IN (${placeholders})`).run(...ids);
1055:   return rows
1056:     .filter((row) => row.snapshot_format === 'v2' && typeof row.snapshot_path === 'string' && row.snapshot_path.length > 0)
1057:     .map((row) => row.snapshot_path as string);
1058: }
1059: 
1060: function enforceMaxCheckpointsInTransaction(database: Database.Database): string[] {
1061:   const checkpointCount = (database.prepare(
1062:     'SELECT COUNT(*) as count FROM checkpoints'
1063:   ) as Database.Statement).get() as { count: number };
1064: 
1065:   if (checkpointCount.count <= MAX_CHECKPOINTS) {
1066:     return [];
1067:   }
1068: 
1069:   return collectPrunedCheckpointSnapshotPaths(database, checkpointCount.count - MAX_CHECKPOINTS);
1070: }
1071: 
1072: function supportsCheckpointV2(database: Database.Database): boolean {
1073:   const checkpointColumns = new Set(getTableColumns(database, 'checkpoints'));
1074:   return checkpointColumns.has('snapshot_format') && checkpointColumns.has('snapshot_path');
1075: }

1077: function hasMainVectorPayloadTables(database: Database.Database): boolean {
1078:   try {
1079:     // Gate v2 selection on vec_memories ONLY — the actual vector payload that the
1080:     // shard-attach slimming removes from main. vec_metadata is a tiny key/value
1081:     // config table that the same slimming intentionally RETAINS in main as a
1082:     // dimension fallback, so its presence does NOT mean vector payload lives in
1083:     // main. Including it here made full-DB create always observe "payload in main"
1084:     // on a sharded runtime and silently fall back to the v1 whole-snapshot path
1085:     // that overflows V8's single-string ceiling — the exact failure v2 prevents.
1086:     const row = database.prepare(`
1087:       SELECT 1 AS found
1088:       FROM sqlite_master
1089:       WHERE type IN ('table', 'view')
1090:         AND name = 'vec_memories'
1091:       LIMIT 1
1092:     `).get() as { found?: number } | undefined;
1093:     return row?.found === 1;
1094:   } catch (_error: unknown) {
1095:     return false;
1096:   }
1097: }
1098: 
1099: function tableExists(database: Database.Database, tableName: string): boolean {
1100:   try {
1101:     if (tableName === 'vec_memories') {
1102:       const mainOrTempRow = database.prepare(`
1103:         SELECT 1 AS found
1104:         FROM temp.sqlite_master
1105:         WHERE type IN ('table','view') AND name = 'vec_memories'
1106:         UNION ALL
1107:         SELECT 1 AS found
1108:         FROM sqlite_master
1109:         WHERE type IN ('table','view') AND name = 'vec_memories'
1110:         LIMIT 1
1111:       `).get() as { found?: number } | undefined;
1112:       if (mainOrTempRow?.found === 1) {
1113:         return true;
1114:       }
1115: 
1116:       const hasActiveVectorShard = (database.prepare('PRAGMA database_list').all() as Array<{ name?: string }>)
1117:         .some((entry) => entry.name === 'active_vec');
1118:       if (!hasActiveVectorShard) {
1119:         return false;
1120:       }
1121: 
1122:       const vectorRow = database.prepare(`
1123:         SELECT 1 AS found
1124:         FROM active_vec.sqlite_master
1125:         WHERE type IN ('table','view') AND name = 'vec_memories'
1126:         LIMIT 1
1127:       `).get() as { found?: number } | undefined;
1128:       return vectorRow?.found === 1;
1129:     }
1130: 
1131:     const row = database.prepare(
1132:       "SELECT name FROM sqlite_master WHERE type IN ('table','view') AND name = ?"
1133:     ).get(tableName) as { name?: string } | undefined;
1134:     return !!row?.name;
1135:   } catch (_error: unknown) {
1136:     return false;
1137:   }
1138: }

1143:   'memory_index', 'memory_fts', 'vec_memories', 'vec_metadata', 'causal_edges',
1144:   'memory_conflicts', 'memory_corrections', 'memory_lineage', 'memory_history',
1145:   'memory_summaries', 'mutation_ledger',
1146:   // Working memory & session
1147:   'working_memory', 'session_state', 'session_sent_memories', 'session_learning',
1148:   // Graph & signals
1149:   'weight_history', 'adaptive_signal_events', 'negative_feedback_events',
1150:   'active_memory_projection', 'auto_entities', 'community_assignments', 'degree_snapshots',
1151:   // Feedback & learning
1152:   'learned_feedback_audit', 'learned_trigger_scores', 'learned_trigger_feedback',
1153:   // Governance
1154:   'governance_audit',
1155:   // Checkpoints & system
1156:   'checkpoints', 'checkpoint_items', 'deletion_log', 'consumption_log',
1157:   'eval_shadow_comparisons',
1158: ]);

1160: function validateTableName(tableName: string): void {

1163:   }

1166: function getTableColumns(database: Database.Database, tableName: string): string[] {

1168:   try {
1169:     return (database.prepare(`PRAGMA table_info("${tableName}")`).all() as Array<{ name: string }>)
1170:       .map((column) => column.name)
1171:       .filter((name) => typeof name === 'string' && name.length > 0);
1172:   } catch (_error: unknown) {

1185: function toBuffer(value: unknown): Buffer | null {
1186:   if (Buffer.isBuffer(value)) return value;
1187:   if (value && typeof value === 'object') {
1188:     const maybeSerialized = value as { type?: unknown; data?: unknown };
1189:     if (
1190:       maybeSerialized.type === 'Buffer' &&
1191:       Array.isArray(maybeSerialized.data) &&
1192:       maybeSerialized.data.every((entry) => typeof entry === 'number')
1193:     ) {
1194:       return Buffer.from(maybeSerialized.data);
1195:     }
1196:   }
1197:   return null;
1198: }
1199: 
1200: function deserializeSnapshotValue(value: unknown): unknown {
1201:   if (value === undefined) {
1202:     return null;
1203:   }
1204:   return toBuffer(value) ?? value;
1205: }

1207: function getMemoryIds(memories: Array<Record<string, unknown>>): number[] {
1208:   const ids = new Set<number>();
1209:   for (const memory of memories) {
1210:     if (!memory || typeof memory !== 'object') {
1211:       continue;
1212:     }
1213:     const rawId = memory.id;
1214:     if (typeof rawId === 'number' && Number.isSafeInteger(rawId)) {
1215:       ids.add(rawId);
1216:       continue;
1217:     }
1218:     if (typeof rawId === 'string' && /^\d+$/.test(rawId)) {
1219:       ids.add(Number.parseInt(rawId, 10));
1220:     }
1221:   }
1222:   return Array.from(ids);
1223: }
1224: 
1225: function parseCheckpointMetadata(value: unknown): Record<string, unknown> {
1226:   if (!value) {
1227:     return {};
1228:   }
1229:   if (typeof value === 'string') {
1230:     try {
1231:       const parsed = JSON.parse(value) as unknown;
1232:       return parsed && typeof parsed === 'object' && !Array.isArray(parsed)
1233:         ? parsed as Record<string, unknown>
1234:         : {};
1235:     } catch (_error: unknown) {
1236:       return {};
1237:     }
1238:   }
1239:   return typeof value === 'object' && !Array.isArray(value)
1240:     ? value as Record<string, unknown>
1241:     : {};
1242: }
1243: 
1244: function checkpointMetadataMatchesScope(rawMetadata: unknown, scope: ScopeContext): boolean {
1245:   const normalizedScope = normalizeScopeContext(scope);
1246:   if (!hasScopeConstraints(normalizedScope)) {
1247:     return true;
1248:   }
1249: 
1250:   const metadata = parseCheckpointMetadata(rawMetadata);
1251:   return (
1252:     (normalizedScope.tenantId === undefined || metadata.tenantId === normalizedScope.tenantId)
1253:     && (normalizedScope.userId === undefined || metadata.userId === normalizedScope.userId)
1254:     && (normalizedScope.agentId === undefined || metadata.agentId === normalizedScope.agentId)
1255:   );
1256: }
1257: 
1258: function hasDirectScopeColumns(columns: ReadonlySet<string>): boolean {
1259:   return (
1260:     columns.has('tenant_id')
1261:     || columns.has('user_id')
1262:     || columns.has('agent_id')
1263:     || columns.has('session_id')
1264:   );
1265: }

1267: function getScopeFilterContext(
1268:   _database: Database.Database,
1269:   scope: ScopeContext = {},
1270: ): {
1271:   normalizedScope: ScopeContext;
1272:   predicate: ((row: Record<string, unknown>) => boolean) | null;
1273: } {
1274:   const normalizedScope = normalizeScopeContext(scope);
1275:   if (!hasScopeConstraints(normalizedScope)) {
1276:     return {
1277:       normalizedScope,
1278:       predicate: null,
1279:     };
1280:   }
1281: 
1282:   return {
1283:     normalizedScope,
1284:     predicate: createScopeFilterPredicate<Record<string, unknown>>(normalizedScope),
1285:   };
1286: }

1288: function getScopedMemories(
1289:   database: Database.Database,
1290:   specFolder: string | null,
1291:   scope: ScopeContext = {},
1292: ): {
1293:   memories: Array<Record<string, unknown>>;
1294:   memoryIds: number[];
1295:   normalizedScope: ScopeContext;
1296: } {
1297:   const { normalizedScope, predicate } = getScopeFilterContext(database, scope);
1298:   const baseMemories = specFolder
1299:     ? database.prepare('SELECT * FROM memory_index WHERE spec_folder = ?').all(specFolder) as Array<Record<string, unknown>>
1300:     : database.prepare('SELECT * FROM memory_index').all() as Array<Record<string, unknown>>;
1301:   const memories = predicate ? baseMemories.filter((row) => predicate(row)) : baseMemories;
1302: 
1303:   return {
1304:     memories,
1305:     memoryIds: getMemoryIds(memories),
1306:     normalizedScope,
1307:   };
1308: }

1311:   database: Database.Database,

1318: function buildRestoreScopeDeleteWhere(
1319:   columns: ReadonlySet<string>,
1320:   checkpointSpecFolder: string | null,
1321:   scope: ScopeContext = {},
1322: ): {
1323:   clauses: string[];
1324:   params: string[];
1325: } {
1326:   const normalizedScope = normalizeScopeContext(scope);
1327:   const clauses: string[] = [];
1328:   const params: string[] = [];
1329: 
1330:   if (checkpointSpecFolder && columns.has('spec_folder')) {
1331:     clauses.push('spec_folder = ?');
1332:     params.push(checkpointSpecFolder);
1333:   }
1334:   if (normalizedScope.tenantId && columns.has('tenant_id')) {
1335:     clauses.push('tenant_id = ?');
1336:     params.push(normalizedScope.tenantId);
1337:   }
1338:   if (normalizedScope.userId && columns.has('user_id')) {
1339:     clauses.push('user_id = ?');
1340:     params.push(normalizedScope.userId);
1341:   }
1342:   if (normalizedScope.agentId && columns.has('agent_id')) {
1343:     clauses.push('agent_id = ?');
1344:     params.push(normalizedScope.agentId);
1345:   }
1346: 
1347:   return { clauses, params };
1348: }

1350: function getEdgeIds(edges: Array<Record<string, unknown>>): number[] {
1351:   const ids = new Set<number>();
1352:   for (const edge of edges) {
1353:     const rawId = edge?.id;
1354:     if (typeof rawId === 'number' && Number.isSafeInteger(rawId)) {
1355:       ids.add(rawId);
1356:       continue;
1357:     }
1358:     if (typeof rawId === 'string' && /^\d+$/.test(rawId)) {
1359:       ids.add(Number.parseInt(rawId, 10));
1360:     }
1361:   }
1362:   return Array.from(ids);
1363: }

1365: function deleteRowsByIds(
1366:   database: Database.Database,
1367:   tableName: string,
1368:   columnName: string,
1369:   ids: number[],
1370: ): void {
1371:   if (ids.length === 0) {
1372:     return;
1373:   }
1374: 
1375:   const sql = `DELETE FROM ${tableName} WHERE ${columnName} IN (__PLACEHOLDERS__)`;
1376:   for (let i = 0; i < ids.length; i += 500) {
1377:     const batch = ids.slice(i, i + 500);
1378:     const placeholders = batch.map(() => '?').join(', ');
1379:     database.prepare(sql.replace('__PLACEHOLDERS__', placeholders)).run(...batch);
1380:   }
1381: }

1383: function snapshotCausalEdgesForMemoryIds(
1384:   database: Database.Database,

1391:   try {
1392:     const edgeIds = memoryIds.map((id) => String(id));
1393:     return dedupeRowsById([
1394:       ...batchedInQuery<Record<string, unknown>>(
1395:         database,
1396:         `
1397:           SELECT * FROM causal_edges
1398:           WHERE source_id IN (__PLACEHOLDERS__)
1399:         `,
1400:         edgeIds,
1401:       ),
1402:       ...batchedInQuery<Record<string, unknown>>(
1403:         database,
1404:         `
1405:           SELECT * FROM causal_edges
1406:           WHERE target_id IN (__PLACEHOLDERS__)
1407:         `,
1408:         edgeIds,
1409:       ),
1410:     ]);
1411:   } catch (_error: unknown) {

1413:   }

1416: function getTableSnapshotColumns(database: Database.Database, tableName: string): string[] {

1423: function selectTableRows(
1424:   database: Database.Database,
1425:   tableName: string,
1426:   options: {
1427:     specFolder: string | null;
1428:     memoryIds: number[];
1429:     scope?: ScopeContext;
1430:   },
1431: ): Array<Record<string, unknown>> {
1432:   const { specFolder, memoryIds, scope = {} } = options;
1433:   const normalizedScope = normalizeScopeContext(scope);
1434:   const hasScope = hasScopeConstraints(normalizedScope);
1435:   const scopePredicate = hasScope
1436:     ? createScopeFilterPredicate<Record<string, unknown>>(normalizedScope)
1437:     : null;
1438: 
1439:   if (tableName === 'memory_index') {
1440:     const rows = specFolder
1441:       ? database.prepare(
1442:         'SELECT * FROM memory_index WHERE spec_folder = ?'
1443:       ).all(specFolder) as Array<Record<string, unknown>>
1444:       : database.prepare('SELECT * FROM memory_index').all() as Array<Record<string, unknown>>;
1445:     return scopePredicate ? rows.filter((row) => scopePredicate(row)) : rows;
1446:   }
1447: 
1448:   if (tableName === 'vec_memories') {
1449:     if (memoryIds.length > 0) {
1450:       return batchedInQuery<Record<string, unknown>>(
1451:         database,
1452:         'SELECT rowid AS rowid, embedding FROM vec_memories WHERE rowid IN (__PLACEHOLDERS__)',
1453:         memoryIds,
1454:       );
1455:     }
1456:     if (specFolder) {
1457:       return database.prepare(`
1458:       SELECT v.rowid AS rowid, v.embedding AS embedding
1459:       FROM vec_memories v
1460:       JOIN memory_index m ON m.id = v.rowid
1461:       WHERE m.spec_folder = ?
1462:     `).all(specFolder) as Array<Record<string, unknown>>;
1463:     }
1464:     return hasScope ? [] : database.prepare(
1465:       'SELECT rowid AS rowid, embedding FROM vec_memories'
1466:     ).all() as Array<Record<string, unknown>>;
1467:   }
1468: 
1469:   if (tableName === 'causal_edges') {
1470:     if (memoryIds.length > 0) {
1471:       return snapshotCausalEdgesForMemoryIds(database, memoryIds);
1472:     }
1473:     return hasScope ? [] : database.prepare('SELECT * FROM causal_edges').all() as Array<Record<string, unknown>>;
1474:   }
1475: 
1476:   const columns = new Set(getTableColumns(database, tableName));
1477: 
1478:   if (specFolder && columns.has('spec_folder')) {
1479:     return database.prepare(
1480:       `SELECT * FROM ${tableName} WHERE spec_folder = ?`
1481:     ).all(specFolder) as Array<Record<string, unknown>>;
1482:   }
1483: 
1484:   if (memoryIds.length > 0 && columns.has('memory_id')) {
1485:     return batchedInQuery<Record<string, unknown>>(
1486:       database,

1488:       memoryIds,
1489:     );

1491:

1497:           SELECT * FROM memory_corrections
1498:           WHERE original_memory_id IN (__PLACEHOLDERS__)
1499:         `,
1500:         memoryIds,

1502:       ...batchedInQuery<Record<string, unknown>>(
1503:         database,
1504:         `
1505:           SELECT * FROM memory_corrections
1506:           WHERE correction_memory_id IN (__PLACEHOLDERS__)
1507:         `,
1508:         memoryIds,
1509:       ),
1510:     ]);
1511:   }
1512: 
1513:   const allRows = database.prepare(`SELECT * FROM ${tableName}`).all() as Array<Record<string, unknown>>;
1514:   if (!scopePredicate) {

1517: 
1518:   if (hasDirectScopeColumns(columns)) {
1519:     return allRows.filter((row) => scopePredicate(row));

1521: 
1522:   return [];

1524: 
1525: function createTableSnapshot(
1526:   database: Database.Database,

1531:     scope?: ScopeContext;

1535:     return null;
1536:   }
1537: 
1538:   const columns = getTableSnapshotColumns(database, tableName);
1539:   if (columns.length === 0) {
1540:     return null;
1541:   }
1542: 
1543:   const rows = selectTableRows(database, tableName, options);
1544:   return { columns, rows };
1545: }
1546: 
1547: function buildCheckpointManifest(): CheckpointManifest {
1548:   return {
1549:     snapshot: [...CHECKPOINT_MANIFEST.snapshot],
1550:     rebuild: [...CHECKPOINT_MANIFEST.rebuild],

1554: 
1555: function buildLegacyTableSnapshots(snapshot: CheckpointSnapshot): Record<string, TableSnapshot> {
1556:   const tableSnapshots: Record<string, TableSnapshot> = {};

1558:   if (Array.isArray(snapshot.memories)) {

1: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
2: // MODULE: Learned Triggers Schema Migration
3: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
4: // Feature catalog: Learned relevance feedback
5: // Schema migration for the learned_triggers column.
6: //
7: // ALTER TABLE memory_index ADD COLUMN learned_triggers TEXT DEFAULT '[]';
8: //
9: // CRITICAL: This column MUST NOT be added to the FTS5 index.
10: // The column stores JSON array of { term, addedAt, source, expiresAt }
11: // Objects representing learned relevance feedback terms.
12: //
13: // Rollback: ALTER TABLE memory_index DROP COLUMN learned_triggers;
14: // (SQLite 3.35.0+)
15: import type { DatabaseExtended as Database } from '@spec-kit/shared/types';
16: 
17: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
18: // 1. TYPES
19: 
20: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
21: /**
22:  * A single learned trigger entry stored in the learned_triggers JSON array.
23:  */
24: export interface LearnedTriggerEntry {
25:   /** The learned term */
26:   term: string;
27:   /** Unix timestamp (seconds) when this term was added */
28:   addedAt: number;
29:   /** Source identifier (e.g., query ID that led to this learning) */
30:   source: string;
31:   /** Unix timestamp (seconds) when this term expires (30-day TTL) */
32:   expiresAt: number;
33: }
34: 
35: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
36: // 2. CONSTANTS
37: 
38: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
39: /** Column name for learned triggers (NOT in FTS5 index) */
40: export const LEARNED_TRIGGERS_COLUMN = 'learned_triggers';
41: 
42: /** Default value for the learned_triggers column */
43: export const LEARNED_TRIGGERS_DEFAULT = '[]';
44: 
45: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
46: // 3. MIGRATION
47: 
48: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
49: /**
50:  * Add the learned_triggers column to memory_index if it does not already exist.
51:  * This migration is idempotent -- safe to run multiple times.
52:  *
53:  * CRITICAL: This column is stored on memory_index only. It is NOT added to
54:  * the memory_fts FTS5 virtual table. The FTS5 index columns are:
55:  *   title, trigger_phrases, file_path, content_text
56:  * learned_triggers is deliberately excluded to keep user-generated content
57:  * isolated from the organic search index.
58:  *
59:  * @param db - SQLite database connection
60:  * @returns true if migration ran (column was added), false if already present
61:  */
62: export function migrateLearnedTriggers(db: Database): boolean {
63:   try {
64:     // Check if column already exists using PRAGMA table_info
65:     const columns = db.prepare('PRAGMA table_info(memory_index)').all() as Array<{ name: string }>;
66:     const hasColumn = columns.some((col) => col.name === LEARNED_TRIGGERS_COLUMN);
67: 
68:     if (hasColumn) {
69:       return false; // Already migrated
70:     }
71: 
72:     db.exec(
73:       `ALTER TABLE memory_index ADD COLUMN ${LEARNED_TRIGGERS_COLUMN} TEXT DEFAULT '${LEARNED_TRIGGERS_DEFAULT}'`
74:     );
75: 
76:     console.warn('[learned-triggers-schema] Migration complete: learned_triggers column added');
77:     return true;
78:   } catch (error: unknown) {
79:     const msg = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
80:     // SQLite may report "duplicate column name" if run concurrently
81:     if (msg.includes('duplicate column')) {
82:       return false;
83:     }
84:     console.error(`[learned-triggers-schema] Migration failed: ${msg}`);
85:     throw error;
86:   }
87: }
88: 
89: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
90: // 4. FTS5 ISOLATION VERIFICATION
91: 
92: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
93: /**
94:  * CRITICAL test: Verify that learned_triggers is NOT present in the
95:  * FTS5 index (memory_fts). This is a safety check that should be run
96:  * after migration and during tests.
97:  *
98:  * The FTS5 virtual table memory_fts should only contain:
99:  *   title, trigger_phrases, file_path, content_text
100:  *
101:  * If learned_triggers appears in FTS5, it means user-generated feedback
102:  * has polluted the organic search index -- a data integrity violation.
103:  *
104:  * @param db - SQLite database connection
105:  * @returns true if isolation is verified (learned_triggers NOT in FTS5)
106:  * @throws Error if learned_triggers IS found in FTS5 (critical violation)
107:  */
108: export function verifyFts5Isolation(db: Database): boolean {
109:   try {
110:     // Check if FTS5 table exists
111:     const ftsTable = db.prepare(
112:       `SELECT sql FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name='memory_fts'`
113:     ).get() as { sql: string } | undefined;
114: 
115:     if (!ftsTable) {
116:       // FTS5 table does not exist; isolation trivially holds
117:       return true;
118:     }
119: 
120:     const createSql = ftsTable.sql.toLowerCase();
121: 
122:     // Check that learned_triggers does NOT appear in the FTS5 CREATE statement
123:     if (createSql.includes('learned_triggers')) {
124:       throw new Error(
125:         'CRITICAL: learned_triggers found in FTS5 index (memory_fts). ' +
126:         'This violates data isolation requirements. Learned triggers must ' +
127:         'ONLY exist in the memory_index.learned_triggers column, never in FTS5.'
128:       );
129:     }
130: 
131:     return true;
132:   } catch (error: unknown) {
133:     if (error instanceof Error && error.message.startsWith('CRITICAL:')) {
134:       throw error; // Re-throw critical violations
135:     }
136:     const msg = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
137:     console.error(`[learned-triggers-schema] FTS5 isolation check failed: ${msg}`);
138:     return false;
139:   }
140: }
141: 
142: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
143: // 5. ROLLBACK
144: 
145: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
146: /**
147:  * Drop the learned_triggers column from memory_index.
148:  * Requires SQLite 3.35.0+ which supports ALTER TABLE DROP COLUMN.
149:  *
150:  * @param db - SQLite database connection
151:  * @returns true if column was dropped, false if it did not exist
152:  */
153: export function rollbackLearnedTriggers(db: Database): boolean {
154:   try {
155:     // Check if column exists first
156:     const columns = db.prepare('PRAGMA table_info(memory_index)').all() as Array<{ name: string }>;
157:     const hasColumn = columns.some((col) => col.name === LEARNED_TRIGGERS_COLUMN);
158: 
159:     if (!hasColumn) {
160:       return false; // Nothing to rollback
161:     }
162: 
163:     db.exec(`ALTER TABLE memory_index DROP COLUMN ${LEARNED_TRIGGERS_COLUMN}`);
164:     console.warn('[learned-triggers-schema] Rollback complete: learned_triggers column removed');
165:     return true;
166:   } catch (error: unknown) {
167:     const msg = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
168:     console.error(`[learned-triggers-schema] Rollback failed: ${msg}`);
169:     throw error;
170:   }
171: }
172: 
173: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
174: // 6. HELPERS
175: 
176: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
177: /**
178:  * Parse the learned_triggers JSON column value into typed entries.
179:  *
180:  * @param raw - Raw JSON string from the database column
181:  * @returns Parsed array of learned trigger entries
182:  */
183: export function parseLearnedTriggers(raw: string | null | undefined): LearnedTriggerEntry[] {
184:   if (!raw || raw === LEARNED_TRIGGERS_DEFAULT) {
185:     return [];
186:   }
187:   try {
188:     const parsed = JSON.parse(raw);
189:     if (!Array.isArray(parsed)) return [];
190:     return parsed.filter(
191:       (e: unknown): e is LearnedTriggerEntry =>
192:         typeof e === 'object' && e !== null &&
193:         typeof (e as LearnedTriggerEntry).term === 'string' &&
194:         typeof (e as LearnedTriggerEntry).addedAt === 'number' &&
195:         typeof (e as LearnedTriggerEntry).expiresAt === 'number'
196:     );
197:   } catch {
198:     return [];
199:   }
200: }
201: 
202: /**
203:  * Serialize learned trigger entries to JSON for storage.
204:  *
205:  * @param entries - Array of learned trigger entries
206:  * @returns JSON string for database storage
207:  */
208: export function serializeLearnedTriggers(entries: LearnedTriggerEntry[]): string {
209:   return JSON.stringify(entries);
210: }

(End of file - total 210 lines)

251:       ?,
252:       MAX(1, error_count),
253:       'seed'
254:     FROM parse_diagnostics
255:     WHERE error_message LIKE '%resolved is not a function%'
256:   `).run(now, now);
257: }
258: 
259: /** Initialize (or get) the code graph database */
260: export function initDb(dbDir: string): Database.Database {
261:   if (db) return db;
262: 
263:   try {
264:     dbPath = join(dbDir, 'code-graph.sqlite');
265:     db = new Database(dbPath);
266:     // Explicit busy_timeout so concurrent writers wait up to 5s
267:     // for the writer lock instead of throwing SQLITE_BUSY immediately. Set
268:     // BEFORE journal_mode/foreign_keys so any incidental contention during
269:     // PRAGMA setup also benefits from the wait.
270:     db.pragma('busy_timeout = 5000');
271:     db.pragma('journal_mode = WAL'); // WAL enables concurrent readers without locks
272:     db.pragma('foreign_keys = ON');
273:     db.exec(SCHEMA_SQL);
274:     ensureSchemaMigrations(db);
275: 
276:     const versionRow = db.prepare('SELECT version FROM schema_version LIMIT 1').get() as { version: number } | undefined;
277:     if (!versionRow) {
278:       db.prepare('INSERT INTO schema_version (version) VALUES (?)').run(SCHEMA_VERSION);
279:     } else if (versionRow.version < SCHEMA_VERSION) {
280:       db.prepare('UPDATE schema_version SET version = ?').run(SCHEMA_VERSION);
281:     }
282: 
283:     return db;
284:   } catch (err) {
285:     if (db) {
286:       try { db.close(); } catch { /* best effort cleanup for failed init */ }
287:     }
288:     db = null;
289:     dbPath = null;
290:     throw err;
291:   }
292: }
293: 
294: /** Get the current database instance (lazy-initializes if needed) */
295: export function getDb(): Database.Database {
296:   if (!db) initDb(DATABASE_DIR);
297:   // initDb either assigns the singleton database or throws before this return.
298:   return db!;
299: }
300: 
301: /** Close the database connection */
302: export function closeDb(): void {
303:   if (db) {
304:     db.close();
305:     db = null;
306:     dbPath = null;
307:   }
308: }
309: 
310: export function closeDbWithAssertion(): void {
311:   const handle = db;
312:   closeDb();
313:   assertDbHandleClosed(handle);
314: }

390: export function getLastDetectorProvenanceSummary(): DetectorProvenanceSummary | null {
391:   const result = getLastDetectorProvenanceSummaryWithDiagnostics();
392:   // Backward-compat: caller-visible API still returns null on
393:   // any non-resolved state; corrupt/invalid state is observable via the
394:   // *WithDiagnostics() companion below.
395:   return result.kind === 'resolved' ? result.value : null;
396: }

398: /**
399:  * Discriminated metadata read result so callers can distinguish
400:  * - 'absent': the row does not exist (first-write hasn't happened yet)
401:  * - 'resolved': the row exists, parsed cleanly, and matches the expected shape
402:  * - 'corrupt': the row exists but is not valid JSON (write-side bug or
403:  *   storage corruption)
404:  * - 'invalid-shape': the row parsed as JSON but does not match the expected
405:  *   shape (schema-version drift or write-side type bug)
406:  *
407:  * The original API (returns `null` on absent / corrupt / invalid-shape) is
408:  * preserved; new callers use these companions to react differently to corrupt
409:  * state vs absent state (e.g. quarantine the row instead of silently rebuilding).
410:  */
411: export type MetadataReadResult<T> =
412:   | { kind: 'absent' }
413:   | { kind: 'resolved'; value: T }
414:   | { kind: 'corrupt'; raw: string }
415:   | { kind: 'invalid-shape'; raw: string };
416: 
417: export function getLastDetectorProvenanceSummaryWithDiagnostics(): MetadataReadResult<DetectorProvenanceSummary> {
418:   // Typed read: distinguish absent vs corrupt vs invalid.
419:   const value = getMetadata('last_detector_provenance_summary');
420:   if (!value) {
421:     return { kind: 'absent' };
422:   }
423:   let parsed: unknown;
424:   try {
425:     parsed = JSON.parse(value);
426:   } catch {
427:     return { kind: 'corrupt', raw: value };
428:   }
429:   // Shape guard: must be an object with `dominant` and `counts` properties.
430:   if (
431:     typeof parsed !== 'object'
432:     || parsed === null
433:     || !('dominant' in parsed)
434:     || !('counts' in parsed)
435:   ) {
436:     return { kind: 'invalid-shape', raw: value };
437:   }
438:   return { kind: 'resolved', value: parsed as DetectorProvenanceSummary };
439: }
440: 
441: export function setLastDetectorProvenanceSummary(summary: DetectorProvenanceSummary): void {
442:   setMetadata('last_detector_provenance_summary', JSON.stringify(summary));
443: }

485: function isFailedScanRecord(value: unknown): value is FailedScanRecord {
486:   if (typeof value !== 'object' || value === null) {
487:     return false;
488:   }
489:   const record = value as Record<string, unknown>;
490:   return typeof record.reason === 'string'
491:     && typeof record.totalFiles === 'number'
492:     && typeof record.totalNodes === 'number'
493:     && typeof record.parseErrorCount === 'number'
494:     && typeof record.parseErrorRatio === 'number'
495:     && typeof record.recordedAt === 'string';
496: }
497: 
498: export function recordFailedScan(
499:   record: Omit<FailedScanRecord, 'recordedAt'>,
500: ): FailedScanRecord {
501:   const failedScan: FailedScanRecord = {
502:     ...record,
503:     recordedAt: new Date().toISOString(),
504:   };
505:   setMetadata('last_failed_scan', JSON.stringify(failedScan));
506:   return failedScan;
507: }
508: 
509: export function getLastFailedScan(): FailedScanRecord | null {
510:   const raw = getMetadata('last_failed_scan');
511:   if (!raw) {
512:     return null;
513:   }
514: 
515:   try {
516:     const parsed = JSON.parse(raw) as unknown;
517:     return isFailedScanRecord(parsed) ? parsed : null;
518:   } catch {
519:     return null;
520:   }
521: }

529: export function getLastGoldVerificationWithDiagnostics(): MetadataReadResult<object> {
530:   // Typed read: distinguish absent vs corrupt vs invalid.
531:   const value = getCodeGraphMetadata('last_gold_verification');
532:   if (!value) {
533:     return { kind: 'absent' };
534:   }
535:   let parsed: unknown;
536:   try {
537:     parsed = JSON.parse(value);
538:   } catch {
539:     return { kind: 'corrupt', raw: value };
540:   }
541:   // Shape guard: gold verification must be an object (it's a free-form
542:   // record but never a primitive). The detailed shape check stays at the
543:   // call sites that already use isRecord() etc.
544:   if (typeof parsed !== 'object' || parsed === null) {
545:     return { kind: 'invalid-shape', raw: value };
546:   }
547:   return { kind: 'resolved', value: parsed as object };
548: }

564: /**
565:  * Insert or update a file record, returning the file ID.
566:  *
567:  * `options.fileMtimeMs` lets callers stage a placeholder mtime (e.g. `0`)
568:  * during multi-step structural persistence so `isFileStale()` continues
569:  * flagging the file as stale until nodes + edges have landed.
570:  * When omitted, the current on-disk mtime is used (original behavior).
571:  */
572: export function upsertFile(
573:   filePath: string,
574:   language: string,
575:   contentHash: string,
576:   nodeCount: number,
577:   edgeCount: number,
578:   parseHealth: string,
579:   parseDurationMs: number,
580:   options?: { fileMtimeMs?: number },
581: ): number {
582:   const d = getDb();
583:   const now = new Date().toISOString();
584:   const fileMtimeMs = options?.fileMtimeMs !== undefined
585:     ? options.fileMtimeMs
586:     : (getCurrentFileMtimeMs(filePath) ?? 0);
587: 
588:   const existing = d.prepare('SELECT id FROM code_files WHERE file_path = ?').get(filePath) as { id: number } | undefined;
589:   if (existing) {
590:     d.prepare(`
591:       UPDATE code_files SET language = ?, content_hash = ?, node_count = ?, edge_count = ?,
592:         file_mtime_ms = ?, parse_health = ?, indexed_at = ?, parse_duration_ms = ?
593:       WHERE id = ?
594:     `).run(language, contentHash, nodeCount, edgeCount, fileMtimeMs, parseHealth, now, parseDurationMs, existing.id);
595:     return existing.id;
596:   }
597: 
598:   const result = d.prepare(`
599:     INSERT INTO code_files (
600:       file_path, language, content_hash, file_mtime_ms, node_count, edge_count, parse_health, indexed_at, parse_duration_ms
601:     )
602:     VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)
603:   `).run(filePath, language, contentHash, fileMtimeMs, nodeCount, edgeCount, parseHealth, now, parseDurationMs);
604:   return Number(result.lastInsertRowid);
605: }
606: 
607: export function recordParseDiagnostic(filePath: string, errorMessage: string): ParseDiagnostic {
608:   const d = getDb();
609:   const now = new Date().toISOString();
610:   const message = errorMessage.length > 0
611:     ? errorMessage
612:     : 'Parser returned parseHealth="error" without diagnostics';
613:   d.prepare(`
614:     INSERT INTO parse_diagnostics (file_path, error_message, error_count, last_seen_at)
615:     VALUES (?, ?, 1, ?)
616:     ON CONFLICT(file_path) DO UPDATE SET
617:       error_message = excluded.error_message,
618:       error_count = parse_diagnostics.error_count + 1,
619:       last_seen_at = excluded.last_seen_at
620:   `).run(filePath, message, now);
621: 
622:   const row = d.prepare(`
623:     SELECT file_path, error_message, error_count, last_seen_at
624:     FROM parse_diagnostics
625:     WHERE file_path = ?
626:   `).get(filePath) as {
627:     file_path: string;
628:     error_message: string;
629:     error_count: number;
630:     last_seen_at: string;
631:   };
632: 
633:   return {
634:     filePath: row.file_path,
635:     errorMessage: row.error_message,
636:     errorCount: row.error_count,
637:     lastSeenAt: row.last_seen_at,
638:   };
639: }
640: 
641: export function clearParseDiagnostic(filePath: string): void {
642:   const d = getDb();
643:   d.prepare('DELETE FROM parse_diagnostics WHERE file_path = ?').run(filePath);
644: }

646: export function getParseDiagnosticsSummary(limit: number = 5): ParseDiagnosticsSummary {
647:   const d = getDb();
648:   const safeLimit = Number.isFinite(limit) ? Math.max(1, Math.min(Math.trunc(limit), 20)) : 5;
649:   const affected = d.prepare('SELECT COUNT(*) AS count FROM parse_diagnostics').get() as { count: number };
650:   const rows = d.prepare(`
651:     SELECT file_path, error_message, error_count, last_seen_at
652:     FROM parse_diagnostics
653:     ORDER BY last_seen_at DESC, file_path ASC
654:     LIMIT ?
655:   `).all(safeLimit) as Array<{
656:     file_path: string;
657:     error_message: string;
658:     error_count: number;
659:     last_seen_at: string;
660:   }>;
661: 
662:   return {
663:     affectedFiles: affected.count,
664:     recentErrors: rows.map((row) => ({
665:       filePath: row.file_path,
666:       errorMessage: row.error_message,
667:       errorCount: row.error_count,
668:       lastSeenAt: row.last_seen_at,
669:     })),
670:   };
671: }

673: export function countStaleButValidParseDiagnostics(): number {
674:   const d = getDb();
675:   const row = d.prepare(`
676:     SELECT COUNT(*) AS count
677:     FROM parse_diagnostics diagnostic
678:     INNER JOIN code_files file ON file.file_path = diagnostic.file_path
679:   `).get() as { count: number };
680:   return row.count;
681: }

683: /** Batch insert nodes for a file (deletes existing first) */
684: export function replaceNodes(fileId: number, nodes: CodeNode[]): void {
685:   const d = getDb();
686:   const insert = d.prepare(`
687:     INSERT OR IGNORE INTO code_nodes (symbol_id, file_id, file_path, fq_name, kind, name,
688:       start_line, end_line, start_column, end_column, language, signature, docstring, content_hash)
689:     VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)
690:   `);
691: 
692:   const tx = d.transaction(() => {
693:     const oldSymbolRows = d.prepare('SELECT symbol_id FROM code_nodes WHERE file_id = ?').all(fileId) as { symbol_id: string }[];
694:     const oldSymbolIds = oldSymbolRows.map(row => row.symbol_id);
695: 
696:     d.prepare('DELETE FROM code_nodes WHERE file_id = ?').run(fileId);
697: 
698:     if (oldSymbolIds.length > 0) {
699:       const placeholders = oldSymbolIds.map(() => '?').join(',');
700:       d.prepare(`
701:         DELETE FROM code_edges
702:         WHERE source_id IN (${placeholders}) OR target_id IN (${placeholders})
703:       `).run(...oldSymbolIds, ...oldSymbolIds);
704:     }
705: 
706:     for (const n of nodes) {
707:       insert.run(
708:         n.symbolId, fileId, n.filePath, n.fqName, n.kind, n.name,
709:         n.startLine, n.endLine, n.startColumn, n.endColumn,
710:         n.language, n.signature ?? null, n.docstring ?? null, n.contentHash,
711:       );
712:     }
713:   });
714:   tx();
715: }
716: 
717: export interface ReplaceEdgesOptions {
718:   /**
719:    * BUG-03: when true, skip the inline dangling-target prune. During a full
720:    * scan, files are persisted one at a time; a cross-file IMPORTS edge whose
721:    * target lives in a not-yet-persisted file would otherwise be deleted here
722:    * (its `target_id` is not yet in `code_nodes`) and is never restored, because
723:    * the post-persist resolver only reconciles CALLS edges. Callers that persist
724:    * many files in sequence pass `true` and run `pruneDanglingEdges()` ONCE after
725:    * all nodes + cross-file resolution land. Selective/single-file callers leave
726:    * it false so genuinely-dangling edges are cleaned inline.
727:    */
728:   deferDanglingTargetPrune?: boolean;
729: }
730: 
731: /** Batch insert edges (deletes edges from the source nodes first) */
732: export function replaceEdges(sourceIds: string[], edges: CodeEdge[], opts: ReplaceEdgesOptions = {}): void {
733:   const d = getDb();
734: 
735:   const insert = d.prepare(`
736:     INSERT INTO code_edges (source_id, target_id, edge_type, weight, metadata)
737:     VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?)
738:   `);
739: 
740:   const tx = d.transaction(() => {
741:     if (sourceIds.length > 0) {
742:       const placeholders = sourceIds.map(() => '?').join(',');
743:       d.prepare(`DELETE FROM code_edges WHERE source_id IN (${placeholders})`).run(...sourceIds);
744:     }
745: 
746:     const candidateSourceIds = [...new Set(edges.map((edge) => edge.sourceId))];
747:     const retainedSourceIds = new Set<string>();
748:     if (candidateSourceIds.length > 0) {
749:       const placeholders = candidateSourceIds.map(() => '?').join(',');
750:       const rows = d.prepare(`
751:         SELECT symbol_id
752:         FROM code_nodes
753:         WHERE symbol_id IN (${placeholders})
754:       `).all(...candidateSourceIds) as Array<{ symbol_id: string }>;
755:       for (const row of rows) {
756:         retainedSourceIds.add(row.symbol_id);
757:       }
758:     }
759: 
760:     for (const e of edges) {
761:       if (!retainedSourceIds.has(e.sourceId)) {
762:         continue;
763:       }
764:       insert.run(e.sourceId, e.targetId, e.edgeType, e.weight, e.metadata ? JSON.stringify(e.metadata) : null);
765:     }
766: 
767:     if (!opts.deferDanglingTargetPrune) {
768:       d.prepare(`
769:         DELETE FROM code_edges WHERE
770:           source_id NOT IN (SELECT symbol_id FROM code_nodes) OR
771:           target_id NOT IN (SELECT symbol_id FROM code_nodes)
772:       `).run();
773:     }
774:   });
775:   tx();
776: }
777: 
778: /**
779:  * BUG-03: remove edges whose source or target symbol no longer exists in
780:  * `code_nodes`. Run ONCE after a multi-file scan (all nodes persisted +
781:  * cross-file resolution complete) when per-file `replaceEdges` deferred its
782:  * inline prune. Returns the number of edges removed.
783:  */
784: export function pruneDanglingEdges(): number {
785:   const d = getDb();
786:   const result = d.prepare(`
787:     DELETE FROM code_edges WHERE
788:       source_id NOT IN (SELECT symbol_id FROM code_nodes) OR
789:       target_id NOT IN (SELECT symbol_id FROM code_nodes)
790:   `).run();
791:   return result.changes;
792: }
793: 
794: /** Check if a file needs re-indexing based on stored mtime and content hash */
795: export function isFileStale(filePath: string, options?: { currentContentHash?: string }): boolean {
796:   const d = getDb();
797:   const row = d.prepare('SELECT file_mtime_ms, content_hash FROM code_files WHERE file_path = ?').get(filePath) as {
798:     file_mtime_ms: number;
799:     content_hash: string | null;
800:   } | undefined;
801:   if (!row) return true;
802:   const currentMtimeMs = getCurrentFileMtimeMs(filePath);
803:   if (currentMtimeMs === null) return true;
804:   if (!row.content_hash) return true;
805: 
806:   // Hash content on mtime drift before declaring stale. A touch
807:   // (mtime drift, content unchanged) used to force reindex; now it stays
808:   // fresh as long as the content hash matches. Avoids gratuitous full-scans
809:   // on `git checkout` of unchanged files.
810:   const currentContentHash = options?.currentContentHash ?? getCurrentFileContentHash(filePath);
811:   if (currentContentHash === null) return true;
812:   return row.content_hash !== currentContentHash;
813: }
814: 
815: /** Batch stale check for a set of file paths */
816: export interface FreshFilesResult {
817:   readonly stale: string[];
818:   readonly fresh: string[];
819: }
820: 
821: export function ensureFreshFiles(filePaths: string[]): FreshFilesResult {
822:   const uniquePaths = [...new Set(filePaths)];
823:   if (uniquePaths.length === 0) {
824:     return { stale: [], fresh: [] };
825:   }
826: 
827:   const d = getDb();
828:   const placeholders = uniquePaths.map(() => '?').join(',');
829:   const rows = d.prepare(`
830:     SELECT file_path, file_mtime_ms, content_hash
831:     FROM code_files
832:     WHERE file_path IN (${placeholders})
833:   `).all(...uniquePaths) as Array<{ file_path: string; file_mtime_ms: number; content_hash: string | null }>;
834:   const storedFiles = new Map(rows.map((row) => [row.file_path, row]));
835: 
836:   const stale: string[] = [];
837:   const fresh: string[] = [];
838: 
839:   for (const filePath of uniquePaths) {
840:     const storedFile = storedFiles.get(filePath);
841:     const currentMtimeMs = getCurrentFileMtimeMs(filePath);
842:     if (storedFile === undefined || currentMtimeMs === null) {
843:       stale.push(filePath);
844:       continue;
845:     }
846:     if (!storedFile.content_hash) {
847:       stale.push(filePath);
848:       continue;
849:     }
850:     // Hash on mtime drift before declaring stale. Touch-only
851:     // changes (mtime drift, content unchanged) stay fresh; only real content
852:     // changes flip the file to stale.
853:     const currentContentHash = getCurrentFileContentHash(filePath);
854:     if (currentContentHash === null || storedFile.content_hash !== currentContentHash) {
855:       stale.push(filePath);
856:       continue;
857:     }
858:     fresh.push(filePath);
859:   }
860: 
861:   return { stale, fresh };
862: }

889: /** Remove a file and its nodes/edges from the graph */
890: export function removeFile(filePath: string): void {
891:   const d = getDb();
892:   const file = d.prepare('SELECT id FROM code_files WHERE file_path = ?').get(filePath) as { id: number } | undefined;
893:   if (!file) return;
894:   // CG-003: wrap the edge-delete + file-delete (CASCADE drops nodes) in a single
895:   // transaction so a crash mid-call cannot leave orphaned edges / partial graph state.
896:   const tx = d.transaction(() => {
897:     const nodeIds = d.prepare('SELECT symbol_id FROM code_nodes WHERE file_id = ?').all(file.id) as { symbol_id: string }[];
898:     if (nodeIds.length > 0) {
899:       const placeholders = nodeIds.map(() => '?').join(',');
900:       const ids = nodeIds.map(n => n.symbol_id);
901:       d.prepare(`DELETE FROM code_edges WHERE source_id IN (${placeholders}) OR target_id IN (${placeholders})`).run(...ids, ...ids);
902:     }
903:     d.prepare('DELETE FROM code_files WHERE id = ?').run(file.id);
904:   });
905:   tx();
906: }

908: /** Query: get file outline (nodes sorted by line) */
909: export function queryOutline(filePath: string): CodeNode[] {
910:   const d = getDb();

912:     SELECT * FROM code_nodes WHERE file_path = ? ORDER BY start_line
913:   `).all(filePath) as Record<string, unknown>[];

918: /** Query: startup-friendly highlights — most-called project symbols (incoming calls). */
919: export function queryStartupHighlights(limit: number = 5): StartupHighlight[] {
920:   const d = getDb();
921:   const safeLimit = Number.isFinite(limit) ? Math.max(1, Math.min(Math.trunc(limit), 20)) : 5;
922:   const rows = d.prepare(`
923:     WITH filtered_nodes AS (
924:       SELECT n.symbol_id, n.name, n.kind, n.file_path, n.start_line
925:       FROM code_nodes n
926:       WHERE n.kind IN ('class', 'function', 'method', 'interface', 'type_alias', 'module')
927:         AND n.file_path NOT LIKE '%/site-packages/%'
928:         AND n.file_path NOT LIKE '%/node_modules/%'
929:         AND n.file_path NOT LIKE '%/.venv/%'
930:         AND n.file_path NOT LIKE '%/vendor/%'
931:         AND n.file_path NOT LIKE '%/dist/%'
932:         AND n.file_path NOT LIKE '%/build/%'
933:         AND n.file_path NOT LIKE '%/__pycache__/%'
934:         AND n.file_path NOT LIKE '%/tests/%'
935:         AND n.file_path NOT LIKE '%/test_%'
936:         AND n.file_path NOT LIKE '%__tests__%'
937:     ),
938:     aggregated AS (
939:       SELECT
940:         fn.name as name,
941:         fn.kind as kind,
942:         fn.file_path as file_path,
943:         MIN(fn.start_line) as start_line,
944:         COALESCE(SUM(CASE WHEN UPPER(e.edge_type) = 'CALLS' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END), 0) as call_count
945:       FROM filtered_nodes fn
946:       LEFT JOIN code_edges e
947:         ON e.target_id = fn.symbol_id
948:       GROUP BY fn.name, fn.kind, fn.file_path
949:     ),
950:     ranked AS (
951:       SELECT
952:         name,
953:         kind,
954:         file_path,
955:         call_count,
956:         start_line,
957:         ROW_NUMBER() OVER (
958:           PARTITION BY file_path
959:           ORDER BY call_count DESC, start_line ASC
960:         ) as file_rank
961:       FROM aggregated
962:     )
963:     SELECT
964:       name,
965:       kind,
966:       file_path,
967:       call_count
968:     FROM ranked
969:     WHERE file_rank <= 2
970:     ORDER BY call_count DESC, start_line ASC
971:     LIMIT ?
972:   `).all(safeLimit) as Array<{
973:     name: string;
974:     kind: string;
975:     file_path: string;
976:     call_count: number;
977:   }>;
978: 
979:   return rows.map((row) => ({
980:     name: row.name,
981:     kind: row.kind,
982:     filePath: row.file_path,
983:     callCount: row.call_count,
984:   }));
985: }

987: /** Query: get edges from a symbol */
988: export interface CodeEdgeTargetResult {
989:   readonly edge: CodeEdge;
990:   readonly targetNode: CodeNode | null;

993: export function queryEdgesFrom(symbolId: string, edgeType?: string): CodeEdgeTargetResult[] {
994:   const d = getDb();
995:   let sql = 'SELECT * FROM code_edges WHERE source_id = ?';
996:   const params: unknown[] = [symbolId];
997:   if (edgeType) {
998:     sql += ' AND edge_type = ?';
999:     params.push(edgeType);
1000:   }
1001: 
1002:   const edges = d.prepare(sql).all(...params) as Record<string, unknown>[];
1003:   return edges.map(e => {
1004:     const edge = rowToEdge(e);
1005:     const targetRow = d.prepare('SELECT * FROM code_nodes WHERE symbol_id = ?').get(edge.targetId) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;
1006:     return { edge, targetNode: targetRow ? rowToNode(targetRow) : null };
1007:   });

1016: export function queryEdgesTo(symbolId: string, edgeType?: string): CodeEdgeSourceResult[] {
1017:   const d = getDb();

1020:   if (edgeType) {

1028:     const sourceRow = d.prepare('SELECT * FROM code_nodes WHERE symbol_id = ?').get(edge.sourceId) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;
1029:     return { edge, sourceNode: sourceRow ? rowToNode(sourceRow) : null };

1033: export function resolveSubjectFilePath(subject: string): string | null {
1034:   const d = getDb();
1035: 
1036:   const candidates = [subject];
1037:   if (!isAbsolute(subject)) {
1038:     candidates.push(resolvePath(process.cwd(), subject));
1039:   }
1040: 
1041:   for (const candidate of candidates) {
1042:     const directFile = d.prepare('SELECT file_path FROM code_files WHERE file_path = ? LIMIT 1').get(candidate) as { file_path: string } | undefined;
1043:     if (directFile) return directFile.file_path;
1044:   }
1045: 
1046:   const byId = d.prepare('SELECT file_path FROM code_nodes WHERE symbol_id = ? LIMIT 1').get(subject) as { file_path: string } | undefined;
1047:   if (byId) return byId.file_path;
1048: 
1049:   const byFq = d.prepare('SELECT file_path FROM code_nodes WHERE fq_name = ? LIMIT 1').get(subject) as { file_path: string } | undefined;
1050:   if (byFq) return byFq.file_path;
1051: 
1052:   const byName = d.prepare('SELECT file_path FROM code_nodes WHERE name = ? LIMIT 1').get(subject) as { file_path: string } | undefined;
1053:   if (byName) return byName.file_path;
1054: 
1055:   const escapedSubject = subject.replace(/[%_]/g, '\\$&');
1056:   const like = d.prepare('SELECT file_path FROM code_files WHERE file_path LIKE ? LIMIT 1').get(`%${escapedSubject}`) as { file_path: string } | undefined;
1057:   if (like) return like.file_path;
1058: 
1059:   return null;
1060: }

1062: export function queryFileImportDependents(): FileImportDependent[] {
1063:   const d = getDb();
1064:   const rows = d.prepare(`
1065:     SELECT DISTINCT
1066:       target.file_path AS imported_file_path,
1067:       source.file_path AS importer_file_path
1068:     FROM code_edges edge
1069:     INNER JOIN code_nodes source ON source.symbol_id = edge.source_id
1070:     INNER JOIN code_nodes target ON target.symbol_id = edge.target_id
1071:     WHERE UPPER(edge.edge_type) = 'IMPORTS'
1072:       AND source.file_path != target.file_path
1073:   `).all() as Array<{
1074:     imported_file_path: string;
1075:     importer_file_path: string;
1076:   }>;
1077: 
1078:   return rows.map((row) => ({
1079:     importedFilePath: row.imported_file_path,
1080:     importerFilePath: row.importer_file_path,
1081:   }));
1082: }
