221:       VALUES ('delete', old.id, old.title, old.trigger_phrases, old.file_path, old.content_text);
222:       INSERT INTO memory_fts(rowid, title, trigger_phrases, file_path, content_text)
223:       VALUES (new.id, new.title, new.trigger_phrases, new.file_path, new.content_text);
224:     END
225:   `);
226: 
227:   database.exec(`
228:     CREATE TRIGGER IF NOT EXISTS memory_fts_delete AFTER DELETE ON memory_index BEGIN
229:       INSERT INTO memory_fts(memory_fts, rowid, title, trigger_phrases, file_path, content_text)
230:       VALUES ('delete', old.id, old.title, old.trigger_phrases, old.file_path, old.content_text);
231:     END
232:   `);
233: 
234:   database.exec(`INSERT INTO memory_fts(memory_fts) VALUES('rebuild')`);
235: }
236: 
237: /* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
238:    4. MAIN API
239: ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
240: 
241: function downgradeSchemaV16ToV15(
242:   database: Database.Database,
243:   options: SchemaDowngradeOptions = {}
244: ): SchemaDowngradeResult {
245:   const currentVersion = getCurrentSchemaVersion(database);
246:   if (currentVersion !== TARGET_FROM_VERSION) {
247:     throw new Error(`Downgrade supports only v${TARGET_FROM_VERSION} -> v${TARGET_TO_VERSION}. Current schema is v${currentVersion}.`);
248:   }
249: 
250:   ensureColumnsExist(database, OLLAMA);
251: 
252:   const checkpointName = options.checkpointName || `pre-schema-downgrade-v16-to-v15-${nowTimestampForName()}`;
253:   const checkpoint = checkpoints.createCheckpoint({
254:     name: checkpointName,
255:     specFolder: options.specFolder || null,
256:     metadata: {
257:       reason: 'schema downgrade v16->v15',
258:       fromVersion: TARGET_FROM_VERSION,
259:       toVersion: TARGET_TO_VERSION,
260:       removedColumns: [...OLLAMA],
261:     },
262:   });
263: 
264:   if (!checkpoint) {
265:     throw new Error('Failed to create pre-downgrade checkpoint');
266:   }
267: 
268:   database.pragma('foreign_keys = OFF');
269:   try {
270:     const run = database.transaction(() => {
271:       database.exec(`
272:         DROP TRIGGER IF EXISTS memory_fts_insert;
273:         DROP TRIGGER IF EXISTS memory_fts_update;
274:         DROP TRIGGER IF EXISTS memory_fts_delete;
275:         DROP TABLE IF EXISTS memory_fts;
276:       `);
277: 
278:       database.exec(`ALTER TABLE memory_index RENAME TO memory_index_v16_backup`);
279: 
280:       createMemoryIndexV15(database);
281: 
282:       const columnsCsv = V15_COLUMNS.join(', ');
283:       database.exec(`
284:         INSERT INTO memory_index (${columnsCsv})
285:         SELECT ${columnsCsv}
286:         FROM memory_index_v16_backup
287:       `);
288: 
289:       // Drop backup table to release index names before re-creating v15 indexes.
290:       database.exec(`DROP TABLE memory_index_v16_backup`);
291: 
292:       createV15Indexes(database);
293:       createFtsArtifacts(database);
294: 
295:       database.prepare(`
296:         INSERT OR REPLACE INTO schema_version (id, version, updated_at)
297:         VALUES (1, ?, datetime('now'))
298:       `).run(TARGET_TO_VERSION);
299:     });
300: 
301:     run();
302:   } finally {
303:     database.pragma('foreign_keys = ON');
304:   }
305: 
306:   const row = database.prepare('SELECT COUNT(*) as count FROM memory_index').get() as { count: number };
307:   return {
308:     fromVersion: TARGET_FROM_VERSION,
309:     toVersion: TARGET_TO_VERSION,
310:     checkpointName: checkpointName,
311:     preservedRows: row.count,
312:     removedColumns: [...OLLAMA],
313:   };
314: }
315: 
316: export {
317:   downgradeSchemaV16ToV15,
318: };
319: 
320: /**
321:  * Re-exports related public types.
322:  */
323: export type {
324:   SchemaDowngradeOptions,
325:   SchemaDowngradeResult,
326: };

(End of file - total 326 lines)

81: /**
82:  * Retry-specific memory row with required id and file_path.
83:  * Uses MemoryDbRow as the base type with Record<string, unknown>
84:  * for camelCase fallback fields (e.g. retryCount, triggerPhrases).
85:  */
86: type RetryMemoryRow = Partial<MemoryDbRow> & Record<string, unknown> & {
87:   id: number;
88:   file_path: string;
89: };
90: 
91: type ContentLoader = (memory: RetryMemoryRow) => Promise<string | null>;
92: type RetryQueueStatus = 'pending' | 'retry';
93: 
94: type EmbeddingFailureCode =
95:   | 'EMBEDDING_PROVIDER_ERROR'
96:   | 'EMBEDDING_TIMEOUT'
97:   | 'EMBEDDING_RATE_LIMIT';
98: 
99: interface SanitizeEmbeddingFailureOptions {
100:   provider?: string | null;
101:   force?: boolean;

103: 
104: interface SanitizedEmbeddingFailure {
105:   code: EmbeddingFailureCode | null;
106:   provider: string | null;
107:   errorType: string | null;
108:   publicMessage: string;
109:   sanitized: boolean;

112: interface RetryClaimResult {
113:   claimed: boolean;
114:   previousStatus: RetryQueueStatus | null;
115: }
116: 
117: function getRetryStatus(memory: RetryMemoryRow): RetryQueueStatus | null {
118:   const status = typeof memory.embedding_status === 'string'
119:     ? memory.embedding_status
120:     : typeof memory.embeddingStatus === 'string'
121:       ? memory.embeddingStatus
122:       : null;
123: 
124:   return status === 'pending' || status === 'retry' ? status : null;
125: }
126: 
127: function getRetryCountValue(memory: RetryMemoryRow): number {
128:   return Number(memory.retry_count ?? memory.retryCount ?? 0) || 0;
129: }
130: 
131: function getLastRetryAtValue(memory: RetryMemoryRow): string | null {
132:   const value = typeof memory.last_retry_at === 'string'
133:     ? memory.last_retry_at
134:     : typeof memory.lastRetryAt === 'string'
135:       ? memory.lastRetryAt
136:       : null;
137:   return value && value.trim().length > 0 ? value : null;
138: }
139: 
140: function detectEmbeddingProviderName(source: string | null | undefined): string | null {
141:   if (typeof source !== 'string' || source.trim().length === 0) {
142:     return null;
143:   }
144: 
145:   const explicitProvider = source.match(/provider=([a-z0-9._-]+)/i);
146:   if (explicitProvider?.[1]) {
147:     return explicitProvider[1].toLowerCase();
148:   }
149: 
150:   const lower = source.toLowerCase();
151:   if (lower.includes('voyage')) return 'voyage';
152:   if (lower.includes('azure')) return 'azure-openai';
153:   if (lower.includes('openai') || lower.includes('text-embedding')) return 'openai';
154:   if (lower.includes('system')) return 'system';
155: 
156:   return null;
157: }
158: 
159: function shouldSanitizeEmbeddingFailure(message: string): boolean {
160:   const lower = message.toLowerCase();
161:   return (
162:     /^embedding_[a-z_]+/.test(lower) ||
163:     lower.includes('openai') ||
164:     lower.includes('voyage') ||
165:     lower.includes('azure') ||
166:     lower.includes('text-embedding') ||
167:     lower.includes('rate limit') ||
168:     lower.includes('too many requests') ||
169:     lower.includes('429') ||
170:     lower.includes('timeout') ||
171:     lower.includes('timed out') ||
172:     lower.includes('api key') ||
173:     lower.includes('unauthorized') ||
174:     lower.includes('forbidden') ||
175:     lower.includes('quota') ||
176:     lower.includes('embedding provider')
177:   );
178: }
179: 
180: function inferEmbeddingFailure(message: string): { code: EmbeddingFailureCode; errorType: string } {
181:   const explicitType = message.match(/type=([a-z0-9._-]+)/i)?.[1]?.toLowerCase();
182:   const lower = message.toLowerCase();
183: 
184:   if (lower.includes('embedding_timeout') || lower.includes('timeout') || lower.includes('timed out')) {
185:     return {
186:       code: 'EMBEDDING_TIMEOUT',
187:       errorType: explicitType ?? 'timeout',
188:     };
189:   }
190: 
191:   if (
192:     lower.includes('embedding_rate_limit') ||
193:     lower.includes('rate limit') ||
194:     lower.includes('too many requests') ||
195:     lower.includes('429')
196:   ) {
197:     return {
198:       code: 'EMBEDDING_RATE_LIMIT',
199:       errorType: explicitType ?? 'rate_limit',
200:     };
201:   }
202: 
203:   if (
204:     explicitType === 'auth' ||
205:     lower.includes('api key') ||
206:     lower.includes('unauthorized') ||
207:     lower.includes('forbidden')
208:   ) {
209:     return {
210:       code: 'EMBEDDING_PROVIDER_ERROR',
211:       errorType: 'auth',
212:     };
213:   }
214: 
215:   return {
216:     code: 'EMBEDDING_PROVIDER_ERROR',
217:     errorType: explicitType ?? 'provider_error',
218:   };
219: }
220: 
221: function sanitizeEmbeddingFailure(
222:   raw: unknown,
223:   options: SanitizeEmbeddingFailureOptions = {},
224: ): SanitizedEmbeddingFailure {
225:   const publicMessage = raw instanceof Error
226:     ? raw.message.trim()
227:     : String(raw ?? '').trim();
228: 
229:   if (publicMessage.length === 0) {
230:     return {
231:       code: null,
232:       provider: null,
233:       errorType: null,
234:       publicMessage,
235:       sanitized: false,
236:     };
237:   }
238: 
239:   if (!options.force && !shouldSanitizeEmbeddingFailure(publicMessage)) {
240:     return {
241:       code: null,
242:       provider: null,
243:       errorType: null,
244:       publicMessage,
245:       sanitized: false,
246:     };
247:   }
248: 
249:   const provider = detectEmbeddingProviderName(options.provider ?? publicMessage) ?? 'unknown';
250:   const { code, errorType } = inferEmbeddingFailure(publicMessage);
251: 
252:   return {
253:     code,
254:     provider,
255:     errorType,
256:     publicMessage: `${code} (provider=${provider}, type=${errorType})`,
257:     sanitized: true,
258:   };
259: }
260: 
261: export function sanitizeEmbeddingFailureMessage(
262:   raw: unknown,
263:   options: SanitizeEmbeddingFailureOptions = {},
264: ): string | null {
265:   const failure = sanitizeEmbeddingFailure(raw, options);
266:   return failure.publicMessage.length > 0 ? failure.publicMessage : null;
267: }
268: 
269: export function sanitizeAndLogEmbeddingFailure(
270:   context: string,
271:   raw: unknown,
272:   options: SanitizeEmbeddingFailureOptions = {},
273: ): string | null {
274:   const failure = sanitizeEmbeddingFailure(raw, options);
275:   if (failure.publicMessage.length === 0) {
276:     return null;
277:   }
278: 
279:   if (failure.sanitized) {
280:     console.error(context, {
281:       sanitized: {
282:         code: failure.code,
283:         provider: failure.provider,
284:         errorType: failure.errorType,
285:       },
286:       redacted: true,

288:   }
289:

292: 
293: function claimRetryCandidate(memory: RetryMemoryRow): RetryClaimResult {
294:   const db = vectorIndex.getDb();
295:   const previousStatus = getRetryStatus(memory);
296:   if (!db || previousStatus === null) {
297:     return { claimed: false, previousStatus };
298:   }
299: 
300:   const now = new Date().toISOString();
301:   const retryCount = getRetryCountValue(memory);
302:   const lastRetryAt = getLastRetryAtValue(memory);
303:   // Concurrency: atomic claim prevents double-processing of retry jobs
304:   const result = previousStatus === 'pending'
305:     ? db.prepare(`
306:         UPDATE memory_index
307:         SET embedding_status = 'retry',
308:             last_retry_at = ?,
309:             updated_at = ?
310:         WHERE id = ?
311:           AND embedding_status = 'pending'
312:           AND retry_count = ?
313:           AND ((? IS NULL AND last_retry_at IS NULL) OR last_retry_at = ?)
314:       `).run(now, now, memory.id, retryCount, lastRetryAt, lastRetryAt)
315:     : db.prepare(`
316:         UPDATE memory_index
317:         SET last_retry_at = ?,
318:             updated_at = ?
319:         WHERE id = ?
320:           AND embedding_status = 'retry'
321:           AND retry_count = ?
322:           AND ((? IS NULL AND last_retry_at IS NULL) OR last_retry_at = ?)
323:       `).run(now, now, memory.id, retryCount, lastRetryAt, lastRetryAt);
324: 
325:   return {
326:     claimed: result.changes > 0,
327:     previousStatus,
328:   };
329: }
330: 
331: /* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
332:    2. CONFIGURATION
333: ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
334: 
335: // Backoff delays in milliseconds (1min, 5min, 15min)
336: const BACKOFF_DELAYS: number[] = [
337:   60 * 1000,
338:   5 * 60 * 1000,
339:   15 * 60 * 1000,
340: ];
341: 
342: const MAX_RETRIES = 3;
343: const MAX_RETRY_QUEUE_PENDING = parsePositiveIntEnv('SPECKIT_RETRY_QUEUE_MAX_PENDING', 1_000);
344: const MAX_RETRY_QUEUE_AGE_MS = parsePositiveIntEnv('SPECKIT_RETRY_QUEUE_MAX_AGE_MS', 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000);
345: 
346: // 005-003: Read retry-retention limits at call-time so tuned env (SPECKIT_RETRY_QUEUE_MAX_*)
347: // applies to a long-lived daemon without depending on the exact restart sequence. The
348: // module-load constants above remain the boot-time snapshot exported for diagnostics/tests.
349: function getMaxRetryQueuePending(): number {
350:   return parsePositiveIntEnv('SPECKIT_RETRY_QUEUE_MAX_PENDING', 1_000);
351: }
352: 
353: function getMaxRetryQueueAgeMs(): number {
354:   return parsePositiveIntEnv('SPECKIT_RETRY_QUEUE_MAX_AGE_MS', 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000);
355: }
356: 
357: // Background retry job configuration
358: // Env-overrideable
359: //   SPECKIT_RETRY_INTERVAL_MS — interval between background batches (default 300000 = 5 min)
360: //   SPECKIT_RETRY_BATCH_SIZE  — items processed per batch (default 5)
361: //   SPECKIT_RETRY_ENABLED     — set to "false" to disable the background loop entirely (default enabled)
362: const BACKGROUND_JOB_CONFIG: BackgroundJobConfig = {
363:   intervalMs: parseInt(process.env.SPECKIT_RETRY_INTERVAL_MS || String(5 * 60 * 1000), 10),
364:   batchSize: parseInt(process.env.SPECKIT_RETRY_BATCH_SIZE || '5', 10),
365:   enabled: (process.env.SPECKIT_RETRY_ENABLED ?? 'true') !== 'false',
366: };
367: 
368: // Background job state
369: let backgroundJobInterval: ReturnType<typeof setInterval> | null = null;
370: let backgroundJobRunning = false;
371: let shutdownRequested = false;
372: let retryAbortController: AbortController | null = null;
373: 
374: // In-memory retry telemetry (never persisted to DB — read by getEmbeddingRetryStats)
375: let lastBackgroundRunAt: string | null = null;
376: let totalRetryAttempts = 0;
377: const retryHealthSnapshot: EmbeddingRetryStats = {
378:   pending: 0,
379:   failed: 0,
380:   retryAttempts: 0,
381:   circuitBreakerOpen: false,
382:   lastRun: null,
383:   queueDepth: 0,
384: };
385: 
386: // circuit breaker — prevents the background retry job from
387: // Hammering the embedding API when the provider is entirely down. After
388: // PROVIDER_FAILURE_THRESHOLD consecutive failures across any items, the
389: // Circuit opens for PROVIDER_COOLDOWN_MS, causing retryEmbedding to skip
390: // The API call and return a transient error instead.
391: const PROVIDER_FAILURE_THRESHOLD = 5;
392: const PROVIDER_COOLDOWN_MS = 120_000; // 2 minutes
393: const CIRCUIT_TRANSITION_WINDOW_MS = 10 * 60 * 1000; // 10 minutes
394: const FLAPPING_THRESHOLD = 2;
395: const circuitTransitions: number[] = [];
396: 
397: let providerFailures = 0;
398: let providerCircuitOpenedAt: number | null = null;
399: 
400: function parsePositiveIntEnv(name: string, fallback: number): number {
401:   const raw = process.env[name];
402:   if (!raw) return fallback;
403: 
404:   const parsed = Number.parseInt(raw, 10);
405:   return Number.isFinite(parsed) && parsed > 0 ? parsed : fallback;
406: }
407: 
408: function recordCircuitTransition(): void {
409:   const now = Date.now();
410:   circuitTransitions.push(now);
411:   while (circuitTransitions.length > 0 && circuitTransitions[0] < now - CIRCUIT_TRANSITION_WINDOW_MS) {
412:     circuitTransitions.shift();
413:   }
414: }
415: 
416: function getCircuitFlapState(): { flapping: boolean; transitionsInLast10Min: number } {
417:   const now = Date.now();
418:   const recent = circuitTransitions.filter(t => t >= now - CIRCUIT_TRANSITION_WINDOW_MS);
419:   return { flapping: recent.length > FLAPPING_THRESHOLD, transitionsInLast10Min: recent.length };
420: }
421: 
422: function isProviderCircuitOpen(): boolean {
423:   if (providerCircuitOpenedAt === null) return false;
424:   if (Date.now() - providerCircuitOpenedAt >= PROVIDER_COOLDOWN_MS) {
425:     providerCircuitOpenedAt = null;
426:     providerFailures = 0;
427:     recordCircuitTransition();
428:     return false;
429:   }
430:   return true;
431: }
432: 
433: function recordProviderSuccess(): void {
434:   providerFailures = 0;
435:   providerCircuitOpenedAt = null;
436:   retryHealthSnapshot.circuitBreakerOpen = false;
437: }
438: 
439: function recordProviderFailure(): void {
440:   providerFailures++;
441:   if (providerFailures >= PROVIDER_FAILURE_THRESHOLD && providerCircuitOpenedAt === null) {
442:     providerCircuitOpenedAt = Date.now();
443:     recordCircuitTransition();
444:     console.warn(`[retry-manager] Embedding provider circuit breaker OPEN after ${providerFailures} consecutive failures. Cooldown: ${PROVIDER_COOLDOWN_MS}ms`);
445:   }
446:   retryHealthSnapshot.circuitBreakerOpen = isProviderCircuitOpen();
447: }
448: 
449: function applyRetryHealthTransition(previousStatus: string | null | undefined, nextStatus: string): void {
450:   if (previousStatus === 'pending') {
451:     retryHealthSnapshot.pending = Math.max(0, retryHealthSnapshot.pending - 1);
452:   }
453: 
454:   if (previousStatus === 'pending' || previousStatus === 'retry') {
455:     retryHealthSnapshot.queueDepth = Math.max(0, retryHealthSnapshot.queueDepth - 1);
456:   }
457: 
458:   if (nextStatus === 'pending') {
459:     retryHealthSnapshot.pending++;
460:     retryHealthSnapshot.queueDepth++;
461:   }
462: 
463:   if (nextStatus === 'retry') {
464:     retryHealthSnapshot.queueDepth++;
465:   }
466: 
467:   if (previousStatus === 'failed') {
468:     retryHealthSnapshot.failed = Math.max(0, retryHealthSnapshot.failed - 1);
469:   }
470: 
471:   if (nextStatus === 'failed') {
472:     retryHealthSnapshot.failed++;
473:   }
474: }
475: 
476: function refreshRetryHealthSnapshot(stats: RetryStats): void {
477:   retryHealthSnapshot.pending = stats.pending;
478:   retryHealthSnapshot.failed = stats.failed;
479:   retryHealthSnapshot.retryAttempts = totalRetryAttempts;
480:   retryHealthSnapshot.circuitBreakerOpen = isProviderCircuitOpen();
481:   retryHealthSnapshot.lastRun = lastBackgroundRunAt;
482:   retryHealthSnapshot.queueDepth = stats.queue_size;
483: }
484: 
485: function enforceRetryRetentionLimits(
486:   maxPending = getMaxRetryQueuePending(),
487:   maxAgeMs = getMaxRetryQueueAgeMs(),
488:   now: Date = new Date(),
489: ): { expired: number; overflow: number } {
490:   const db = vectorIndex.getDb();
491:   if (!db) return { expired: 0, overflow: 0 };
492: 
493:   const nowIso = now.toISOString();
494:   const oldestAllowedIso = new Date(now.getTime() - maxAgeMs).toISOString();
495:   // Retention is non-destructive for never-attempted clean `pending` rows.
496:   // Only rows that have actually been attempted (status='retry' OR retry_count>0) are
497:   // subject to max-age expiry and pending-cap overflow; clean `pending` work waits to be
498:   // embedded instead of being parked as `failed` before the drain can claim it.
499:   const expired = db.prepare(`
500:     UPDATE memory_index
501:     SET embedding_status = 'failed',
502:         failure_reason = 'Retry retention max age exceeded',
503:         updated_at = ?
504:     WHERE embedding_status IN ('pending', 'retry')
505:       AND (embedding_status = 'retry' OR COALESCE(retry_count, 0) > 0)
506:       AND COALESCE(last_retry_at, created_at, updated_at) < ?
507:   `).run(nowIso, oldestAllowedIso).changes;
508: 
509:   const rows = db.prepare(`
510:     SELECT id FROM memory_index
511:     WHERE embedding_status IN ('pending', 'retry')
512:       AND (embedding_status = 'retry' OR COALESCE(retry_count, 0) > 0)
513:     ORDER BY COALESCE(last_retry_at, created_at, updated_at) ASC, id ASC
514:     LIMIT -1 OFFSET ?
515:   `).all(maxPending) as Array<{ id: number }>;
516: 
517:   let overflow = 0;
518:   if (rows.length > 0) {
519:     const updateOverflow = db.prepare(`
520:       UPDATE memory_index
521:       SET embedding_status = 'failed',
522:           failure_reason = 'Retry retention pending cap exceeded',
523:           updated_at = ?
524:       WHERE id = ?
525:         AND embedding_status IN ('pending', 'retry')
526:         AND (embedding_status = 'retry' OR COALESCE(retry_count, 0) > 0)
527:     `);
528:     const txn = db.transaction((ids: number[]) => {
529:       for (const id of ids) {
530:         overflow += updateOverflow.run(nowIso, id).changes;
531:       }
532:     });
533:     txn(rows.map((row) => row.id));
534:   }
535: 
536:   if (expired > 0 || overflow > 0) {
537:     console.warn(`[retry-manager] Retention pruned expired=${expired}, overflow=${overflow}`);
538:   }
539: 
540:   return { expired, overflow };
541: }
542: 
543: /* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
544:    3. RETRY QUEUE
545: ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
546: 
547: function getRetryQueue(limit = 10): RetryMemoryRow[] {
548:   vectorIndex.initializeDb();
549:   const db = vectorIndex.getDb();
550:   if (!db) {
551:     console.warn('[retry-manager] Database not initialized. Server may still be starting up.');
552:     return [];
553:   }
554:   enforceRetryRetentionLimits();
555:   const now = Date.now();
556: 
557:   const rows = db.prepare(`
558:     SELECT * FROM memory_index
559:     WHERE embedding_status IN ('pending', 'retry')
560:       AND retry_count < ?
561:     ORDER BY
562:       CASE WHEN embedding_status = 'pending' THEN 0 ELSE 1 END,
563:       retry_count ASC,
564:       created_at ASC
565:     LIMIT ?
566:   `).all(MAX_RETRIES, limit * 2) as RetryMemoryRow[];
567: 
568:   const eligible: RetryMemoryRow[] = [];
569:   for (const row of rows) {
570:     if (isEligibleForRetry(row, now)) {
571:       eligible.push(parseRow(row));
572:       if (eligible.length >= limit) break;
573:     }
574:   }
575: 
576:   return eligible;
577: }
578: 
579: function isEligibleForRetry(row: RetryMemoryRow, now: number): boolean {
580:   // Note: better-sqlite3 returns snake_case column names from SELECT *
581:   if (row.embedding_status === 'pending') return true;
582: 
583:   if (row.embedding_status === 'retry' && row.last_retry_at) {
584:     const lastRetry = new Date(row.last_retry_at).getTime();
585:     const retryCount = (row.retry_count as number) ?? (row.retryCount as number) ?? 0;
586:     // Off-by-one fix: retryCount is already incremented after the failure that triggered
587:     // The retry status, so use (retryCount - 1) for the backoff index. First retry (retryCount=1)
588:     // Should use BACKOFF_DELAYS[0] (1 minute), not BACKOFF_DELAYS[1] (5 minutes).
589:     const backoffIndex = Math.max(0, retryCount - 1);
590:     const requiredDelay = BACKOFF_DELAYS[Math.min(backoffIndex, BACKOFF_DELAYS.length - 1)];
591:     return (now - lastRetry) >= requiredDelay;
592:   }
593: 
594:   return row.embedding_status === 'retry';
595: }
596: 
597: function getFailedEmbeddings(): RetryMemoryRow[] {
598:   const db = vectorIndex.getDb();
599:   if (!db) {
600:     console.warn('[retry-manager] Database not initialized. Server may still be starting up. Returning empty array.');
601:     return [];
602:   }
603: 
604:   const rows = db.prepare(`
605:     SELECT * FROM memory_index
606:     WHERE embedding_status = 'failed'
607:     ORDER BY updated_at DESC
608:   `).all() as RetryMemoryRow[];
609: 
610:   return rows.map(parseRow);
611: }
612: 
613: function getRetryStats(): RetryStats {
614:   const db = vectorIndex.getDb();
615:   if (!db) {
616:     console.warn('[retry-manager] Database not initialized. Server may still be starting up. Returning default stats.');
617:     return { pending: 0, retry: 0, failed: 0, success: 0, total: 0, queue_size: 0 };
618:   }
619: 
620:   const stats = db.prepare(`
621:     SELECT
622:       SUM(CASE WHEN embedding_status = 'pending' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as pending,
623:       SUM(CASE WHEN embedding_status = 'retry' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as retry,
624:       SUM(CASE WHEN embedding_status = 'failed' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as failed,
625:       SUM(CASE WHEN embedding_status = 'success' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as success,
626:       COUNT(*) as total
627:     FROM memory_index
628:   `).get() as Record<string, number>;
629: 
630:   const result = {
631:     pending: stats.pending || 0,
632:     retry: stats.retry || 0,
633:     failed: stats.failed || 0,
634:     success: stats.success || 0,
635:     total: stats.total || 0,
636:     queue_size: (stats.pending || 0) + (stats.retry || 0),
637:   };
638: 
639:   refreshRetryHealthSnapshot(result);
640:   return result;
641: }
642: 
643: /**
644:  * Return a lightweight in-memory snapshot of embedding retry health.
645:  * PULL accessor — synchronous, zero DB access, always safe to call.
646:  * Returns zero-state when the retry manager has not yet been initialized.
647:  */
648: function getEmbeddingRetryStats(): EmbeddingRetryStats {
649:   return {
650:     pending: retryHealthSnapshot.pending,
651:     failed: retryHealthSnapshot.failed,
652:     retryAttempts: totalRetryAttempts,
653:     circuitBreakerOpen: isProviderCircuitOpen(),
654:     lastRun: lastBackgroundRunAt,
655:     queueDepth: retryHealthSnapshot.queueDepth,
656:   };
657: }
658: 
659: /* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
660:    4. RETRY OPERATIONS
661: ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
662: 
663: async function retryEmbedding(
664:   id: number,
665:   content: string,
666:   claimedPreviousStatus: RetryQueueStatus | null = null,
667: ): Promise<RetryResult> {
668:   const db = vectorIndex.getDb();
669:   if (!db) return { success: false, error: 'Database not initialized. Server may still be starting up.' };
670: 
671:   const now = new Date().toISOString();
672: 
673:   try {
674:     const memory = vectorIndex.getMemory(id);
675:     if (!memory) return { success: false, error: 'Memory not found' };
676:     const previousStatus = claimedPreviousStatus ?? (
677:       typeof memory.embedding_status === 'string'
678:         ? memory.embedding_status
679:         : typeof (memory as { embeddingStatus?: unknown }).embeddingStatus === 'string'
680:           ? (memory as { embeddingStatus?: string }).embeddingStatus ?? null
681:           : null
682:     );
683: 
684:     if ((memory.retry_count as number) >= MAX_RETRIES) {
685:       markAsFailed(id, 'Maximum retry attempts exceeded');
686:       return { success: false, error: 'Maximum retries exceeded', permanent: true };
687:     }
688: 
689:     // BUG-1 fix: Normalize content before embedding to match sync save path (memory-save.ts:1119).
690:     // Without this, async-saved memories get embeddings from raw markdown (YAML frontmatter, HTML
691:     // Comments, code fences) while sync-saved memories get clean normalized embeddings.
692:     const normalizedContent = normalizeContentForEmbedding(content);
693:     const modelId = getModelName();
694:     const embeddingDim = getEmbeddingDimension();
695:     const contentHash = computeContentHash(normalizedContent);
696:     const cachedEmbedding = lookupEmbedding(db, contentHash, modelId, embeddingDim);
697: 
698:     let embedding: Float32Array | null = null;
699: 
700:     if (cachedEmbedding) {
701:       embedding = new Float32Array(
702:         cachedEmbedding.buffer,
703:         cachedEmbedding.byteOffset,
704:         Math.floor(cachedEmbedding.byteLength / Float32Array.BYTES_PER_ELEMENT),
705:       );
706:     } else {
707:       // Skip API call if provider circuit breaker is open
708:       if (isProviderCircuitOpen()) {
709:         return { success: false, error: 'Embedding provider circuit breaker open — skipping API call' };
710:       }
711: 
712:       try {
713:         embedding = await generateDocumentEmbedding(normalizedContent);
714:       } catch (providerError: unknown) {
715:         const sanitizedError = sanitizeAndLogEmbeddingFailure(
716:           `[retry-manager] Embedding retry failed for #${id}`,
717:           providerError,
718:           { provider: modelId, force: true },
719:         ) ?? 'EMBEDDING_PROVIDER_ERROR (provider=unknown, type=provider_error)';
720:         recordProviderFailure();
721:         // Security: raw provider errors sanitized before persistence/response
722:         incrementRetryCount(id, sanitizedError);
723:         return { success: false, error: sanitizedError };
724:       }
725: 
726:       if (embedding) {
727:         recordProviderSuccess();
728:         storeEmbedding(
729:           db,
730:           contentHash,
731:           modelId,
732:           Buffer.from(embedding.buffer, embedding.byteOffset, embedding.byteLength),
733:           embedding.length,
734:         );
735:       } else {
736:         recordProviderFailure();
737:       }
738:     }
739: 
740:     if (!embedding) {
741:       incrementRetryCount(id, 'Embedding generation returned null');
742:       return { success: false, error: 'Embedding returned null' };
743:     }
744: 
745:     const updateTx = db.transaction(() => {
746:       db.prepare(`
747:         UPDATE memory_index
748:         SET embedding_status = 'success',
749:             embedding_generated_at = ?,
750:             updated_at = ?,
751:             failure_reason = NULL
752:         WHERE id = ?
753:       `).run(now, now, id);
754: 
755:       try {
756:         db.prepare('DELETE FROM vec_memories WHERE rowid = ?').run(BigInt(id));
757:       } catch (_error: unknown) {
758:         // Ignore if doesn't exist
759:       }
760: 
761:       const embeddingBuffer = Buffer.from(embedding.buffer, embedding.byteOffset, embedding.byteLength);
762:       db.prepare('INSERT INTO vec_memories (rowid, embedding) VALUES (?, ?)').run(BigInt(id), embeddingBuffer);
763:     });
764: 
765:     try {
766:       updateTx();
767:       applyRetryHealthTransition(previousStatus, 'success');
768:       return { success: true, id, dimensions: embedding.length };
769:     } catch (tx_error: unknown) {
770:       // Security: raw provider errors sanitized before persistence/response
771:       console.error(`[retry-manager] Transaction error for #${id}:`, tx_error instanceof Error ? tx_error.message : String(tx_error));
772:       incrementRetryCount(id, 'EMBEDDING_PROVIDER_ERROR (provider=system, type=transaction_failed)');
773:       return { success: false, error: 'EMBEDDING_PROVIDER_ERROR (provider=system, type=transaction_failed)' };
774:     }
775:   } catch (error: unknown) {
776:     recordProviderFailure();
777:     // Security: raw provider errors sanitized before persistence/response
778:     const sanitizedMessage = sanitizeAndLogEmbeddingFailure(
779:       `[retry-manager] Unexpected retry error for #${id}`,
780:       error,
781:       { provider: null, force: true },
782:     ) ?? 'EMBEDDING_PROVIDER_ERROR (provider=unknown, type=provider_error)';
783:     incrementRetryCount(id, sanitizedMessage);
784:     return { success: false, error: sanitizedMessage };
785:   }
786: }
787: 
788: function incrementRetryCount(id: number, reason: string): void {
789:   totalRetryAttempts++;
790:   const db = vectorIndex.getDb();
791:   if (!db) return;
792: 
793:   const now = new Date().toISOString();
794:   const memory = vectorIndex.getMemory(id);
795:   if (!memory) {
796:     console.warn(`[retry-manager] Memory ${id} not found during retry count increment`);
797:     return;
798:   }
799: 
800:   const newRetryCount = (Number(memory.retry_count) || 0) + 1;
801:   // Security: raw provider errors sanitized before persistence/response
802:   const persistedReason = sanitizeEmbeddingFailureMessage(reason) ?? reason;
803:   const previousStatus = typeof memory.embedding_status === 'string'
804:     ? memory.embedding_status
805:     : typeof (memory as { embeddingStatus?: unknown }).embeddingStatus === 'string'
806:       ? (memory as { embeddingStatus?: string }).embeddingStatus ?? null
807:       : null;
808: 
809:   if (newRetryCount >= MAX_RETRIES) {
810:     markAsFailed(id, reason);
811:   } else {
812:     db.prepare(`
813:       UPDATE memory_index
814:       SET embedding_status = 'retry',
815:           retry_count = ?,
816:           last_retry_at = ?,
817:           failure_reason = ?,
818:           updated_at = ?
819:       WHERE id = ?
820:     `).run(newRetryCount, now, persistedReason, now, id);
821:     applyRetryHealthTransition(previousStatus, 'retry');
822:   }
823: }
824: 
825: function markAsFailed(id: number, reason: string): void {
826:   const db = vectorIndex.getDb();
827:   if (!db) {
828:     console.warn('[retry-manager] Database not initialized. Server may still be starting up. Cannot mark as failed.');
829:     return;
830:   }
831:   const now = new Date().toISOString();
832:   const memory = vectorIndex.getMemory(id);
833:   // Security: raw provider errors sanitized before persistence/response
834:   const persistedReason = sanitizeEmbeddingFailureMessage(reason) ?? reason;
835:   const previousStatus = memory && typeof memory.embedding_status === 'string'
836:     ? memory.embedding_status
837:     : memory && typeof (memory as { embeddingStatus?: unknown }).embeddingStatus === 'string'
838:       ? (memory as { embeddingStatus?: string }).embeddingStatus ?? null
839:       : null;
840: 
841:   db.prepare(`
842:     UPDATE memory_index
843:     SET embedding_status = 'failed',
844:         failure_reason = ?,
845:         updated_at = ?
846:     WHERE id = ?
847:   `).run(persistedReason, now, id);
848:   applyRetryHealthTransition(previousStatus, 'failed');
849: }
850: 
851: function resetForRetry(id: number): boolean {
852:   const db = vectorIndex.getDb();
853:   if (!db) return false;
854:   const now = new Date().toISOString();
855:   const memory = vectorIndex.getMemory(id);
856:   const previousStatus = memory && typeof memory.embedding_status === 'string'
857:     ? memory.embedding_status
858:     : memory && typeof (memory as { embeddingStatus?: unknown }).embeddingStatus === 'string'
859:       ? (memory as { embeddingStatus?: string }).embeddingStatus ?? null
860:       : null;
861: 
862:   const result = db.prepare(`
863:     UPDATE memory_index
864:     SET embedding_status = 'retry',
865:         retry_count = 0,
866:         last_retry_at = NULL,
867:         failure_reason = NULL,
868:         updated_at = ?
869:     WHERE id = ? AND embedding_status = 'failed'
870:   `).run(now, id);
871: 
872:   if (result.changes > 0) {
873:     applyRetryHealthTransition(previousStatus, 'retry');
874:   }
875: 
876:   return result.changes > 0;
877: }
878: 
879: /* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
880:    5. BATCH PROCESSING
881: ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
882: 
883: async function processRetryQueue(limit = 3, contentLoader: ContentLoader | null = null): Promise<BatchResult> {
884:   if (shutdownRequested || retryAbortController?.signal.aborted) {
885:     return { processed: 0, succeeded: 0, failed: 0, details: [] };
886:   }
887: 
888:   const queue = getRetryQueue(limit);
889: 
890:   if (queue.length === 0) {
891:     return { processed: 0, succeeded: 0, failed: 0 };
892:   }
893: 
894:   const results: BatchResult = {
895:     processed: 0,
896:     succeeded: 0,
897:     failed: 0,
898:     details: [],
899:   };
900:   const details = results.details ?? (results.details = []);
901: 
902:   for (const memory of queue) {
903:     if (shutdownRequested || retryAbortController?.signal.aborted) {
904:       break;
905:     }
906: 
907:     // For items that have never been attempted (pending), check provider state first.
908:     // Claiming a pending item promotes it to retry, which is subject to retention pruning.
909:     // During an outage, leave pending rows as-is so they are not pruned before the drain can embed them.
910:     if (memory.embedding_status === 'pending' && isProviderCircuitOpen()) {
911:       continue;
912:     }
913: 
914:     const claim = claimRetryCandidate(memory);
915:     if (!claim.claimed) {
916:       continue;
917:     }
918: 
919:     let content: string | null = null;
920: 
921:     if (contentLoader) {
922:       content = await contentLoader(memory);
923:     } else if (typeof memory.content_text === 'string' && memory.content_text.length > 0) {
924:       // Use stored content_text — critical for chunks which share file_path with parent
925:       content = memory.content_text;
926:     } else {
927:       content = await loadContentFromFile(memory.file_path);
928:     }
929: 
930:     if (shutdownRequested || retryAbortController?.signal.aborted) {
931:       break;
932:     }
933: 
934:     if (!content) {
935:       // Count content load failure as a retry attempt to prevent infinite retry loops
936:       incrementRetryCount(memory.id, 'Content load failed: file unreadable or missing');
937:       details.push({ id: memory.id, success: false, error: 'Could not load content (counted as retry)' });
938:       results.failed++;
939:       results.processed++;
940:       continue;
941:     }
942: 
943:     const result = await retryEmbedding(memory.id, content, claim.previousStatus);
944:     results.processed++;
945: 
946:     if (result.success) {
947:       results.succeeded++;
948:     } else {
949:       results.failed++;
950:     }
951: 
952:     details.push({ id: memory.id, ...result } as RetryDetailEntry);
953:   }
954: 
955:   return results;
956: }
957: 
958: /* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
959:    6. BACKGROUND RETRY JOB
960: ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
961: 
962: function startBackgroundJob(options: Partial<BackgroundJobConfig> = {}): boolean {
963:   if (backgroundJobInterval) {
964:     console.error('[retry-manager] Background job already running');
965:     return false;
966:   }
967: 
968:   const config = { ...BACKGROUND_JOB_CONFIG, ...options };
969:   shutdownRequested = false;
970:   retryAbortController = new AbortController();
971: 
972:   if (!config.enabled) {
973:     console.error('[retry-manager] Background job is disabled');
974:     return false;
975:   }
976: 
977:   console.error(`[retry-manager] Starting background retry job (interval: ${config.intervalMs}ms, batch: ${config.batchSize})`);
978: 
979:   runBackgroundJob(config.batchSize).catch((err: unknown) => {
980:     const message = err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err);
981:     console.error('[retry-manager] Initial background job failed:', message);
982:   });
983: 
984:   backgroundJobInterval = registerInterval(() => {
985:     runBackgroundJob(config.batchSize).catch((err: unknown) => {
986:       const message = err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err);
987:       console.error('[retry-manager] Background job iteration failed:', message);
988:     });
989:   }, config.intervalMs, { unref: true });
990: 
991:   return true;
992: }
993: 
994: function stopBackgroundJob(): boolean {
995:   shutdownRequested = true;
996:   retryAbortController?.abort();
997:   retryAbortController = null;
998:   if (!backgroundJobInterval) return false;
999: 
1000:   clearRegisteredTimer(backgroundJobInterval);
1001:   backgroundJobInterval = null;
1002:   backgroundJobRunning = false;
1003:   console.error('[retry-manager] Background retry job stopped');
1004:   return true;
1005: }
1006: 
1007: function isBackgroundJobRunning(): boolean {
1008:   return backgroundJobInterval !== null;
1009: }
1010: 
1011: async function runBackgroundJob(batchSize: number = BACKGROUND_JOB_CONFIG.batchSize): Promise<BackgroundJobResult> {
1012:   if (backgroundJobRunning) {
1013:     return { skipped: true, reason: 'Previous run still in progress' };
1014:   }
1015: 
1016:   backgroundJobRunning = true;
1017:   if (!retryAbortController || retryAbortController.signal.aborted) {
1018:     retryAbortController = new AbortController();
1019:   }
1020: 
1021:   try {
1022:     enforceRetryRetentionLimits();
1023:     if (shutdownRequested || retryAbortController.signal.aborted) {
1024:       return { skipped: true, reason: 'Shutdown requested' };
1025:     }
1026:     const stats = getRetryStats();
1027: 
1028:     if (stats.queue_size === 0) {
1029:       return { processed: 0, queue_empty: true };
1030:     }
1031: 
1032:     console.error(`[retry-manager] Background job: Processing up to ${batchSize} of ${stats.queue_size} pending embeddings`);
1033: 
1034:     const result = await processRetryQueue(batchSize);
1035: 
1036:     if (result.processed > 0) {
1037:       console.error(`[retry-manager] Background job complete: ${result.succeeded}/${result.processed} succeeded`);
1038:     }
1039: 
1040:     return result;
1041:   } catch (error: unknown) {
1042:     const message = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
1043:     console.error('[retry-manager] Background job error:', message);
1044:     return { error: message };
1045:   } finally {
1046:     lastBackgroundRunAt = new Date().toISOString();
1047:     retryHealthSnapshot.lastRun = lastBackgroundRunAt;
1048:     retryHealthSnapshot.retryAttempts = totalRetryAttempts;
1049:     retryHealthSnapshot.circuitBreakerOpen = isProviderCircuitOpen();
1050:     backgroundJobRunning = false;
1051:   }
1052: }
1053: 
1054: registerShutdownHook(() => {
1055:   stopBackgroundJob();
1056: }, { timeoutMs: 250 });
1057: 
1058: /* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
1059:    7. UTILITIES
1060: ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
1061: 
1062: function parseRow(row: RetryMemoryRow): RetryMemoryRow {
1063:   if (row.triggerPhrases && typeof row.triggerPhrases === 'string') {
1064:     try {
1065:       return { ...row, triggerPhrases: JSON.parse(row.triggerPhrases) };
1066:     } catch (_error: unknown) {
1067:       return { ...row, triggerPhrases: [] };
1068:     }
1069:   }
1070:   return { ...row };
1071: }
1072: 
1073: async function loadContentFromFile(filePath: string): Promise<string | null> {
1074:   try {
1075:     return await fsPromises.readFile(filePath, 'utf-8');
1076:   } catch (_error: unknown) {
1077:     return null;
1078:   }
1079: }
1080: 
1081: async function claimAndRetryEmbedding(
1082:   id: number,
1083:   content: string,
1084:   expectedStatus: RetryQueueStatus = 'pending',
1085: ): Promise<RetryResult | null> {
1086:   const memory = vectorIndex.getMemory(id) as RetryMemoryRow | undefined;
1087:   if (!memory) {
1088:     return { success: false, error: 'Memory not found' };
1089:   }
1090: 
1091:   const parsedMemory = parseRow(memory);
1092:   if (getRetryStatus(parsedMemory) !== expectedStatus) {
1093:     return null;
1094:   }
1095: 
1096:   const claim = claimRetryCandidate(parsedMemory);
1097:   if (!claim.claimed) {
1098:     return null;
1099:   }
1100: 
1101:   return retryEmbedding(id, content, claim.previousStatus);
1102: }
1103: 
1104: /* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
1105:    8. EXPORTS
1106: ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
1107: 
1108: export {
1109:   getRetryQueue,
1110:   getFailedEmbeddings,
1111:   getRetryStats,
1112:   getEmbeddingRetryStats,
1113:   getCircuitFlapState,
1114:   retryEmbedding,
1115:   claimAndRetryEmbedding,
1116:   markAsFailed,
1117:   resetForRetry,
1118:   processRetryQueue,
1119:   startBackgroundJob,
1120:   stopBackgroundJob,
1121:   isBackgroundJobRunning,
1122:   runBackgroundJob,
1123:   enforceRetryRetentionLimits,
1124:   BACKGROUND_JOB_CONFIG,
1125:   BACKOFF_DELAYS,
1126:   MAX_RETRIES,
1127:   MAX_RETRY_QUEUE_PENDING,
1128:   MAX_RETRY_QUEUE_AGE_MS,
1129:   getMaxRetryQueuePending,
1130:   getMaxRetryQueueAgeMs,
1131: };

1: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
2: // MODULE: Causal Boost
3: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
4: // Feature catalog: Causal neighbor boost and injection
5: // Graph-traversal score boosting via causal edge relationships.
6: // Walks the causal_edges graph up to MAX_HOPS, amplifying scores
7: // For results related to top seed results via weighted CTE.
8: //
9: // Sparse-First + Intent-Aware Traversal:
10: // - Sparse-first policy — density < 0.5 disables community
11: //   detection and constrains traversal to typed 1-hop expansion only.
12: // - Intent-aware edge traversal — maps classified query intents
13: //   to edge-type priority orderings; computes traversal score as:
14: //   score = seedScore * edgePrior * hopDecay * freshness
15: // Both requirements are gated behind SPECKIT_TYPED_TRAVERSAL (default ON, graduated).
16: import { isCausalBoostEnabled, isTypedTraversalEnabled as _isTypedTraversalEnabled, isGraphContextInjectionEnabled } from './search-flags.js';
17: import { routeQueryConcepts } from './entity-linker.js';
18: 
19: import type Database from 'better-sqlite3';
20: 
21: /** Maximum graph traversal depth. Beyond 2 hops, signal degrades and queries become expensive. */
22: const MAX_HOPS = 2;
23: /** Per-hop boost cap. 0.05 keeps causal nudge subtle relative to semantic scores (~0.5–1.0). */
24: const MAX_BOOST_PER_HOP = 0.05;
25: /** Combined causal + session boost ceiling to prevent runaway amplification. */
26: const MAX_COMBINED_BOOST = 0.20;
27: /** Top fraction of result set used as graph walk seed nodes. */
28: const SEED_FRACTION = 0.25;
29: /** Absolute cap on seed nodes regardless of result set size. */
30: const MAX_SEED_RESULTS = 5;
31: 
32: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
33: // SPARSE-FIRST POLICY CONSTANTS
34: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
35: /** Graph density threshold below which community detection is disabled
36:  * and traversal is constrained to typed 1-hop expansion only.
37:  * Density = edgeCount / totalMemories (see edge-density.ts). */
38: const SPARSE_DENSITY_THRESHOLD = 0.5;
39: 
40: /** Traversal depth used in sparse mode (1-hop typed expansion). */
41: const SPARSE_MAX_HOPS = 1;
42: 
43: /** Default traversal depth for typed traversal helper policies. */
44: const DEFAULT_TYPED_TRAVERSAL_DEPTH = SPARSE_MAX_HOPS;
45: 
46: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
47: // INTENT-AWARE EDGE TRAVERSAL
48: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
49: /** Per-hop decay factor applied to traversal score. 1-hop = 1.0, 2-hop = 0.5. */
50: const HOP_DECAY_BASE = 1.0;
51: 
52: /**
53:  * Intent-to-edge-type priority ordering.
54:  * Maps each query intent to an ordered list of edge relation types.
55:  * Earlier entries in the list receive higher edge prior scores.
56:  *
57:  * Scores are normalized: first-listed relation = 1.0, second = 0.75, remaining = 0.5.
58:  */
59: const INTENT_EDGE_PRIORITY: Record<string, string[]> = {
60:   fix_bug:        ['CORRECTION', 'DEPENDS_ON', 'supersedes', 'caused'],
61:   add_feature:    ['EXTENDS', 'DEPENDS_ON', 'enabled', 'derived_from'],
62:   find_decision:  ['PREFERENCE', 'CORRECTION', 'supersedes', 'contradicts'],
63:   // Defaults for other intents — use existing relation-weight ordering
64:   understand:     ['caused', 'enabled', 'derived_from', 'supports', 'supersedes', 'contradicts'],
65:   find_spec:      ['caused', 'enabled', 'derived_from', 'supports', 'supersedes', 'contradicts'],
66:   refactor:       ['caused', 'derived_from', 'enabled', 'supports', 'supersedes', 'contradicts'],
67:   security_audit: ['caused', 'contradicts', 'supersedes', 'enabled', 'supports', 'derived_from'],
68: };
69: 
70: /** Fallback priority list when intent is unrecognized. */
71: const DEFAULT_EDGE_PRIORITY = ['caused', 'enabled', 'derived_from', 'supports', 'supersedes', 'contradicts'];
72: 
73: /** Edge prior score tiers: 1st priority = 1.0, 2nd = 0.75, remaining = 0.5. */
74: const EDGE_PRIOR_TIERS = [1.0, 0.75, 0.5];
75: const DEFAULT_EDGE_PRIOR = 1.0;
76: const FRESHNESS_DECAY_WINDOW_MS = 30 * 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000;
77: 
78: /**
79:  * Relation-type weight multipliers for causal edge traversal.
80:  * Applied during CTE accumulation so stronger relation types (supersedes)
81:  * amplify the boost while weaker ones (contradicts) attenuate it.
82:  *
83:  * These multipliers serve a DIFFERENT purpose from RELATION_WEIGHTS in
84:  * causal-edges.ts. causal-edges weights are applied during chain traversal
85:  * scoring (getCausalChain), while these are applied during the causal-boost
86:  * CTE walk (getNeighborBoosts) for search result amplification. The value
87:  * ranges overlap but are tuned independently for their respective contexts:
88:  *   - causal-edges: traversal strength propagation (range 0.8–1.5)
89:  *   - causal-boost: search result boost amplitude (range 0.8–1.5)
90:  */
91: const RELATION_WEIGHT_MULTIPLIERS: Record<string, number> = {
92:   supersedes: 1.5,
93:   contradicts: 0.8,
94:   caused: 1.0,
95:   enabled: 1.0,
96:   derived_from: 1.0,
97:   supports: 1.0,
98: };
99: 
100: interface RankedSearchResult extends Record<string, unknown> {
101:   id: number;
102:   score?: number;
103:   rrfScore?: number;
104:   intentAdjustedScore?: number;
105:   attentionScore?: number;
106:   similarity?: number;
107:   sessionBoost?: number;
108:   baseScore?: number;
109:   causalBoost?: number;
110:   injectedByCausalBoost?: boolean;
111: }
112: 
113: interface CausalBoostMetadata {
114:   enabled: boolean;
115:   applied: boolean;
116:   boostedCount: number;
117:   injectedCount: number;
118:   maxBoostApplied: number;
119:   traversalDepth: number;
120:   /** True when sparse-first policy was active (density < threshold) */
121:   sparseModeActive?: boolean;
122:   /** Intent used for edge traversal scoring (if typed traversal enabled) */
123:   intentUsed?: string;
124: }
125: 
126: /** Options for applyCausalBoost — extended with sparse-first parameters. */
127: interface CausalBoostOptions {
128:   /** Graph density (edgeCount / totalMemories). Used for sparse-first gate. */
129:   graphDensity?: number;
130:   /** Classified query intent. Used for intent-aware edge priority mapping. */
131:   intent?: string;
132:   /** Freshness factor [0,1] applied to traversal scoring. Defaults to 1.0. */
133:   freshness?: number;
134: }
135: 
136: interface NeighborBoost {
137:   boost: number;
138:   hopCount: number;
139: }
140: 
141: let db: Database.Database | null = null;
142: 
143: /**
144:  * Check whether the causal boost feature flag is enabled.
145:  * Default: ON (graduated). Set SPECKIT_CAUSAL_BOOST=false to disable.
146:  * When enabled, causal graph traversal amplifies scores for results
147:  * connected to top-ranked results via causal edges.
148:  *
149:  * Delegates to the canonical flag check in search-flags.ts.
150:  */
151: function isEnabled(): boolean {
152:   return isCausalBoostEnabled();
153: }
154: 
155: /**
156:  * Check whether the typed traversal feature flag is enabled.
157:  * Default: ON (graduated). Set SPECKIT_TYPED_TRAVERSAL=false to disable.
158:  * Activates sparse-first + intent-aware traversal.
159:  */
160: function isTypedTraversalEnabled(): boolean {
161:   return _isTypedTraversalEnabled();
162: }
163: 
164: /** Store the database reference used by causal edge traversal queries. */
165: function init(database: Database.Database): void {
166:   db = database;
167: }
168: 
169: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
170: // SPARSE-FIRST POLICY
171: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
172: 
173: /**
174:  * Determine whether sparse mode should be active for this traversal pass.
175:  * Sparse mode is active when:
176:  *   - SPECKIT_TYPED_TRAVERSAL is enabled, AND
177:  *   - graphDensity is a finite number below SPARSE_DENSITY_THRESHOLD (0.5)
178:  *
179:  * When sparse mode is active:
180:  *   - Community detection is suppressed (caller must respect this)
181:  *   - Traversal is constrained to SPARSE_MAX_HOPS (1)
182:  */
183: function isSparseMode(graphDensity: number | undefined): boolean {
184:   if (!isTypedTraversalEnabled()) return false;
185:   if (typeof graphDensity !== 'number' || !Number.isFinite(graphDensity)) return false;
186:   return graphDensity < SPARSE_DENSITY_THRESHOLD;
187: }
188: 
189: interface SparseFirstTraversalPolicy {
190:   sparseModeActive: boolean;
191:   communityDetectionEnabled: boolean;
192: }
193: 
194: /**
195:  * Resolve the sparse-first policy gate for callers that need a simple
196:  * community-detection decision without running the full boost pipeline.
197:  */
198: function resolveSparseFirstTraversalPolicy(graphDensity: number | undefined): SparseFirstTraversalPolicy {
199:   const sparseModeActive = isSparseMode(graphDensity);
200:   return {
201:     sparseModeActive,
202:     communityDetectionEnabled: !sparseModeActive,
203:   };
204: }
205: 
206: /**
207:  * Resolve traversal depth for typed traversal helper callers.
208:  * Typed traversal defaults to 1-hop and stays within the supported range.
209:  * When the flag is off, preserve the legacy MAX_HOPS behavior.
210:  */
211: function resolveTraversalDepth(depth: number = DEFAULT_TYPED_TRAVERSAL_DEPTH): number {
212:   if (!isTypedTraversalEnabled()) {
213:     return MAX_HOPS;
214:   }
215: 
216:   if (!Number.isFinite(depth)) {
217:     return DEFAULT_TYPED_TRAVERSAL_DEPTH;
218:   }
219: 
220:   return Math.max(DEFAULT_TYPED_TRAVERSAL_DEPTH, Math.min(MAX_HOPS, Math.trunc(depth)));
221: }
222: 
223: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
224: // INTENT-AWARE EDGE TRAVERSAL HELPERS
225: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
226: 
227: /**
228:  * Compute the edge prior score for a given relation type and intent.
229:  * Returns a normalized score in [0.5, 1.0] based on priority position.
230:  *
231:  * Canonical relation names from causal-boost are lower-cased (e.g. 'supersedes').
232:  * Intent priority lists may include upper-case semantic labels (e.g. 'CORRECTION').
233:  * Matching is case-insensitive against both the label and any alias.
234:  *
235:  * Alias map: CORRECTION → supersedes | contradicts
236:  *            DEPENDS_ON → caused | enabled | derived_from
237:  *            EXTENDS    → enabled | derived_from
238:  *            PREFERENCE → supports
239:  */
240: const EDGE_LABEL_ALIASES: Record<string, string[]> = {
241:   CORRECTION:  ['supersedes', 'contradicts'],
242:   DEPENDS_ON:  ['caused', 'enabled', 'derived_from'],
243:   EXTENDS:     ['enabled', 'derived_from'],
244:   PREFERENCE:  ['supports'],
245: };
246: 
247: function resolveEdgePrior(relation: string, intent: string): number {
248:   if (!isTypedTraversalEnabled()) return 1.0;
249: 
250:   const priority = INTENT_EDGE_PRIORITY[intent] ?? DEFAULT_EDGE_PRIORITY;
251:   const relLower = relation.toLowerCase();
252: 
253:   for (let i = 0; i < priority.length; i++) {
254:     const entry = priority[i];
255:     if (!entry) continue;
256:     // Exact match (case-insensitive)
257:     if (entry.toLowerCase() === relLower) {
258:       return EDGE_PRIOR_TIERS[Math.min(i, EDGE_PRIOR_TIERS.length - 1)] ?? 0.5;
259:     }
260:     // Alias expansion: check if this priority label maps to the relation
261:     const aliases = EDGE_LABEL_ALIASES[entry.toUpperCase()];
262:     if (aliases && aliases.includes(relLower)) {
263:       return EDGE_PRIOR_TIERS[Math.min(i, EDGE_PRIOR_TIERS.length - 1)] ?? 0.5;
264:     }
265:   }
266: 
267:   // Relation not found in priority list — assign minimum tier
268:   return EDGE_PRIOR_TIERS[EDGE_PRIOR_TIERS.length - 1] ?? 0.5;
269: }
270: 
271: /**
272:  * Compute hop decay factor. Formula: HOP_DECAY_BASE / hopDistance.
273:  * 1-hop → 1.0, 2-hop → 0.5, etc. Capped at 1.0.
274:  */
275: function computeHopDecay(hopDistance: number): number {
276:   if (!Number.isFinite(hopDistance) || hopDistance <= 0) return 0;
277:   return Math.min(1.0, HOP_DECAY_BASE / hopDistance);
278: }
279: 
280: /**
281:  * Compute the intent-aware traversal score.
282:  * Formula: seedScore * edgePrior * hopDecay * freshness
283:  *
284:  * @param seedScore  - Base score of the seed node (0–1)
285:  * @param relation   - Edge relation type (e.g. 'supersedes', 'caused')
286:  * @param hopDistance - Hop distance from seed (1 = direct neighbor)
287:  * @param freshness  - Recency factor (0–1, default 1.0)
288:  * @param intent     - Classified query intent (default 'understand')
289:  */
290: function computeIntentTraversalScore(
291:   seedScore: number,
292:   relation: string,
293:   hopDistance: number,
294:   freshness: number,
295:   intent: string
296: ): number {
297:   const edgePrior = resolveEdgePrior(relation, intent);
298:   const hopDecay = computeHopDecay(hopDistance);
299:   return seedScore * edgePrior * hopDecay * freshness;
300: }
301: 
302: function getIntentEdgePriorities(intent: string): string[] {
303:   return (INTENT_EDGE_PRIORITY[intent] ?? []).slice(0, 2);
304: }
305: 
306: function computeIntentEdgePrior(intent: string, edgeType: string): number {
307:   const priorities = getIntentEdgePriorities(intent);
308:   if (priorities.length === 0) {
309:     return DEFAULT_EDGE_PRIOR;
310:   }
311: 
312:   const normalizedEdgeType = edgeType.toUpperCase();
313:   const directMatchIndex = priorities.findIndex(
314:     (priority) => priority.toUpperCase() === normalizedEdgeType,
315:   );
316:   if (directMatchIndex >= 0) {
317:     return EDGE_PRIOR_TIERS[Math.min(directMatchIndex, EDGE_PRIOR_TIERS.length - 1)] ?? 0.5;
318:   }
319: 
320:   return resolveEdgePrior(edgeType, intent);
321: }
322: 
323: function computeTraversalHopDecay(hopDistance: number): number {
324:   return computeHopDecay(hopDistance);
325: }
326: 
327: function computeTraversalFreshnessFactor(
328:   updatedAt: Date | string,
329:   now: Date | string = new Date(),
330: ): number {
331:   const updatedAtMs = new Date(updatedAt).getTime();
332:   const nowMs = new Date(now).getTime();
333:   if (!Number.isFinite(updatedAtMs) || !Number.isFinite(nowMs) || nowMs <= updatedAtMs) {
334:     return 1;
335:   }
336: 
337:   const ageMs = nowMs - updatedAtMs;
338:   return Math.exp(-(ageMs / FRESHNESS_DECAY_WINDOW_MS));
339: }
340: 
341: function computeIntentAwareTraversalScore(params: {
342:   seedScore: number;
343:   intent: string;
344:   edgeType: string;
345:   hopDistance: number;
346:   updatedAt: Date | string;
347:   now?: Date | string;
348: }): number {
349:   return params.seedScore *
350:     computeIntentEdgePrior(params.intent, params.edgeType) *
351:     computeTraversalHopDecay(params.hopDistance) *
352:     computeTraversalFreshnessFactor(params.updatedAt, params.now);
353: }
354: 
355: /**
356:  * Resolve the primary numeric score from a result, checking score, rrfScore,
357:  * and similarity (normalized to 0–1) in precedence order. Returns 0 if none present.
358:  */
359: function resolveBaseScore(result: RankedSearchResult): number {
360:   if (typeof result.score === 'number' && Number.isFinite(result.score)) return result.score;
361:   if (typeof result.rrfScore === 'number' && Number.isFinite(result.rrfScore)) return result.rrfScore;
362:   if (typeof result.similarity === 'number' && Number.isFinite(result.similarity)) return result.similarity / 100;
363:   return 0;
364: }
365: 
366: function buildPipelineRow(
367:   row: RankedSearchResult,
368:   baseScore: number,
369:   causalBoost: number
370: ): RankedSearchResult {
371:   const allowedBoost = Math.max(0, Math.min(causalBoost, MAX_COMBINED_BOOST));
372:   const score = Math.min(1, Math.max(0, baseScore * (1 + allowedBoost)));
373:   return {
374:     ...row,
375:     score,
376:     rrfScore: score,
377:     intentAdjustedScore: score,
378:     attentionScore: score,
379:     causalBoost: allowedBoost,
380:     baseScore,
381:     injectedByCausalBoost: true,
382:   };
383: }
384: 
385: /**
386:  * Deduplicate and validate a list of numeric IDs, truncating to integers
387:  * and dropping non-finite values to guard against DB query injection.
388:  */
389: function normalizeIds(inputIds: number[]): number[] {
390:   const ids = new Set<number>();
391:   for (const candidate of inputIds) {
392:     if (typeof candidate === 'number' && Number.isFinite(candidate)) {
393:       ids.add(Math.trunc(candidate));
394:     }
395:   }
396:   return Array.from(ids);
397: }
398: 
399: /**
400:  * Compute the hop-distance decay boost: MAX_BOOST_PER_HOP / hopDistance,
401:  * capped at MAX_BOOST_PER_HOP so closer neighbors get the full signal.
402:  */
403: function computeBoostByHop(hopDistance: number): number {
404:   if (!Number.isFinite(hopDistance) || hopDistance <= 0) return 0;
405:   const rawBoost = MAX_BOOST_PER_HOP / hopDistance;
406:   return Math.min(MAX_BOOST_PER_HOP, rawBoost);
407: }
408: 
409: /**
410:  * Walk causal edges up to maxHops from the given seed memory IDs,
411:  * returning a map of neighbor ID to boost score.
412:  *
413:    * Accepts an optional maxHops override. When sparse mode is active
414:  * (density < 0.5 and SPECKIT_TYPED_TRAVERSAL enabled), the caller passes
415:  * SPARSE_MAX_HOPS (1) to constrain traversal depth.
416:  */
417: function getNeighborBoosts(memoryIds: number[], maxHops: number = MAX_HOPS): Map<number, NeighborBoost> {
418:   const neighborBoosts = new Map<number, NeighborBoost>();
419:   if (!db) return neighborBoosts;
420: 
421:   const ids = normalizeIds(memoryIds);
422:   if (ids.length === 0) return neighborBoosts;
423: 
424:   const originIds = ids.map((value) => String(value));
425:   const placeholders = originIds.map(() => '?').join(', ');
426: 
427:   // Relation-weighted CTE — accumulates score with multiplier
428:   // Based on edge relation type and edge strength column.
429:   // 'supersedes' edges get 1.5x, 'contradicts' 0.8x, others 1.0x.
430:   const query = `
431:     WITH RECURSIVE causal_walk(origin_id, node_id, hop_distance, walk_score) AS (
432:       SELECT ce.source_id, ce.target_id, 1,
433:              (CASE WHEN ce.relation = 'supersedes' THEN 1.5
434:                    WHEN ce.relation = 'contradicts' THEN 0.8
435:                    ELSE 1.0 END * COALESCE(ce.strength, 1.0))
436:       FROM causal_edges ce
437:       WHERE ce.source_id IN (${placeholders})
438: 
439:       UNION
440: 
441:       SELECT ce.target_id, ce.source_id, 1,
442:              (CASE WHEN ce.relation = 'supersedes' THEN 1.5
443:                    WHEN ce.relation = 'contradicts' THEN 0.8
444:                    ELSE 1.0 END * COALESCE(ce.strength, 1.0))
445:       FROM causal_edges ce
446:       WHERE ce.target_id IN (${placeholders})
447: 
448:       UNION
449: 
450:       SELECT cw.origin_id,
451:              CASE
452:                WHEN ce.source_id = cw.node_id THEN ce.target_id
453:                ELSE ce.source_id
454:              END,
455:              cw.hop_distance + 1,
456:              (cw.walk_score * CASE WHEN ce.relation = 'supersedes' THEN 1.5
457:                                    WHEN ce.relation = 'contradicts' THEN 0.8
458:                                    ELSE 1.0 END * COALESCE(ce.strength, 1.0))
459:       FROM causal_walk cw
460:       JOIN causal_edges ce
461:         ON ce.source_id = cw.node_id OR ce.target_id = cw.node_id
462:       WHERE cw.hop_distance < ?
463:         AND (CASE WHEN ce.source_id = cw.node_id THEN ce.target_id ELSE ce.source_id END) != cw.origin_id
464:     )
465:     SELECT node_id, MIN(hop_distance) AS min_hop, MAX(walk_score) AS max_walk_score
466:     FROM causal_walk
467:     WHERE node_id NOT IN (${placeholders})
468:     GROUP BY node_id
469:   `;
470: 
471:   try {
472:     const rows = (db.prepare(query) as Database.Statement).all(
473:       ...originIds,
474:       ...originIds,
475:       maxHops,
476:       ...originIds
477:     ) as Array<{ node_id: string; min_hop: number; max_walk_score: number }>;
478: 
479:     for (const row of rows) {
480:       const neighborId = Number.parseInt(row.node_id, 10);
481:       if (!Number.isFinite(neighborId)) continue;
482:       // Combine hop-distance decay with relation-weighted walk score
483:       const hopBoost = computeBoostByHop(row.min_hop);
484:       const walkMultiplier = typeof row.max_walk_score === 'number' && Number.isFinite(row.max_walk_score)
485:         ? Math.max(0.1, Math.min(2.0, row.max_walk_score))
486:         : 1.0;
487:       const boost = hopBoost * walkMultiplier;
488:       if (boost <= 0) continue;
489:       const current = neighborBoosts.get(neighborId);
490:       if (!current || boost > current.boost) {
491:         neighborBoosts.set(neighborId, {
492:           boost,
493:           hopCount: Math.max(1, Math.min(maxHops, row.min_hop)),
494:         });
495:       }
496:     }
497:   } catch (error: unknown) {
498:     const message = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
499:     console.warn(`[causal-boost] Traversal failed: ${message}`);
500:   }
501: 
502:   return neighborBoosts;
503: }
504: 
505: function fetchNeighborRows(memoryIds: number[]): RankedSearchResult[] {
506:   if (!db || memoryIds.length === 0) return [];
507: 
508:   const placeholders = memoryIds.map(() => '?').join(', ');
509:   const rows = (db.prepare(`
510:     SELECT id, spec_folder, file_path, title, importance_tier, trigger_phrases, created_at
511:     FROM memory_index
512:     WHERE id IN (${placeholders})
513:   `) as Database.Statement).all(...memoryIds) as RankedSearchResult[];
514: 
515:   return rows;
516: }
517: 
518: /**
519:  * Apply causal graph boost to ranked search results, injecting
520:  * graph-discovered neighbors and amplifying scores for connected nodes.
521:  *
522:    * Sparse-first and intent-aware traversal extensions:
523:    * - When SPECKIT_TYPED_TRAVERSAL is enabled and graphDensity < 0.5,
524:  *   activates sparse mode: traversal depth = 1, community detection suppressed.
525:    * - When SPECKIT_TYPED_TRAVERSAL is enabled and intent is provided,
526:  *   applies intent-aware edge traversal scoring using the formula:
527:  *   score = seedScore * edgePrior * hopDecay * freshness
528:  *
529:  * @param results - Ranked search results from prior pipeline stages
530:    * @param options - Optional traversal parameters (graphDensity, intent, freshness)
531:  */
532: function applyCausalBoost(
533:   results: RankedSearchResult[],
534:   options: CausalBoostOptions = {}
535: ): { results: RankedSearchResult[]; metadata: CausalBoostMetadata } {
536:   const { graphDensity, intent = 'understand', freshness = 1.0 } = options;
537: 
538:   // Determine sparse mode and effective hop depth.
539:   const sparseMode = isSparseMode(graphDensity);
540:   const effectiveMaxHops = sparseMode ? SPARSE_MAX_HOPS : MAX_HOPS;
541: 
542:   const metadata: CausalBoostMetadata = {
543:     enabled: isEnabled(),
544:     applied: false,
545:     boostedCount: 0,
546:     injectedCount: 0,
547:     maxBoostApplied: 0,
548:     traversalDepth: effectiveMaxHops,
549:     sparseModeActive: sparseMode,
550:     intentUsed: isTypedTraversalEnabled() ? intent : undefined,
551:   };
552: 
553:   if (!metadata.enabled || !Array.isArray(results) || results.length === 0 || !db) {
554:     return { results, metadata };
555:   }
556: 
557:   const seedLimit = Math.max(1, Math.min(MAX_SEED_RESULTS, Math.ceil(results.length * SEED_FRACTION)));
558:   const seedResults = results.slice(0, seedLimit);
559:   const seedIds = seedResults.map((item) => item.id);
560:   // Pass effectiveMaxHops to constrain traversal in sparse mode.
561:   const neighborBoosts = getNeighborBoosts(seedIds, effectiveMaxHops);
562:   if (neighborBoosts.size === 0) {
563:     return { results, metadata };
564:   }
565: 
566:   // When SPECKIT_TYPED_TRAVERSAL is enabled, re-score neighbor boosts
567:   // using the intent-aware traversal formula: seedScore * edgePrior * hopDecay * freshness.
568:   // We use the average seed score as the seedScore proxy and the actual hop distance
569:   // returned by getNeighborBoosts(). This preserves backward compatibility: when flag
570:   // is OFF, the original boost values from getNeighborBoosts() are used unchanged.
571:   const intentBoosts: Map<number, number> = new Map();
572:   if (isTypedTraversalEnabled()) {
573:     const avgSeedScore = seedResults.length > 0
574:       ? seedResults.map((s) => resolveBaseScore(s)).reduce((a, b) => a + b, 0) / seedResults.length
575:       : 1.0;
576:     const clampedFreshness = Math.max(0, Math.min(1, Number.isFinite(freshness) ? freshness : 1.0));
577: 
578:     for (const [neighborId, neighborBoost] of neighborBoosts) {
579:       // We don't have the per-edge relation type at this aggregation level,
580:       // so we use the seed's dominant relation by querying the first-hop edge from the DB.
581:       // Fall back to 'caused' (neutral default) if unavailable.
582:       let dominantRelation = 'caused';
583:       if (db) {
584:         try {
585:           const edgeRow = (db.prepare(`
586:             SELECT relation FROM causal_edges
587:             WHERE source_id IN (${seedIds.map(() => '?').join(',')})
588:               AND target_id = ?
589:             LIMIT 1
590:           `) as Database.Statement).get(...seedIds.map(String), String(neighborId)) as
591:             { relation: string } | undefined;
592:           if (edgeRow?.relation) dominantRelation = edgeRow.relation;
593:         } catch (_err: unknown) {
594:           // Fall back to default relation on query failure
595:         }
596:       }
597: 
598:       const intentScore = computeIntentTraversalScore(
599:         avgSeedScore,
600:         dominantRelation,
601:         neighborBoost.hopCount,
602:         clampedFreshness,
603:         intent
604:       );
605:       intentBoosts.set(neighborId, intentScore);
606:     }
607:   }
608: 
609:   // Select the active boost map: intent-aware when the flag is on, classic otherwise.
610:   const classicBoosts = new Map<number, number>();
611:   for (const [neighborId, neighborBoost] of neighborBoosts) {
612:     classicBoosts.set(neighborId, neighborBoost.boost);
613:   }
614:   const activeBoosts = isTypedTraversalEnabled() ? intentBoosts : classicBoosts;
615: 
616:   const existingIds = new Set(results.map((item) => item.id));
617:   // Reduce avoids stack overflow on arrays >100K elements (spread pushes all onto call stack)
618:   const lowestScore = Math.max(0.0001, results.map((item) => resolveBaseScore(item)).reduce((a, b) => Math.min(a, b), Infinity));
619: 
620:   const boosted = results.map((item) => {
621:     const causalBoost = activeBoosts.get(item.id) ?? 0;
622:     if (causalBoost <= 0) {
623:       return item;
624:     }
625: 
626:     const sessionBoost = typeof item.sessionBoost === 'number' ? Math.max(0, item.sessionBoost) : 0;
627:     const allowedBoost = Math.max(0, Math.min(causalBoost, MAX_COMBINED_BOOST - sessionBoost));
628:     if (allowedBoost <= 0) {
629:       return item;
630:     }
631: 
632:     const baseScore = resolveBaseScore(item);
633:     const score = Math.min(1, Math.max(0, baseScore * (1 + allowedBoost)));
634:     metadata.boostedCount += 1;
635:     metadata.maxBoostApplied = Math.max(metadata.maxBoostApplied, allowedBoost);
636:     return {
637:       ...item,
638:       score,
639:       rrfScore: score,
640:       intentAdjustedScore: score,
641:       attentionScore: score,
642:       causalBoost: allowedBoost,
643:       baseScore,
644:     };
645:   });
646: 
647:   const injectIds: number[] = [];
648:   for (const [neighborId] of activeBoosts) {
649:     if (!existingIds.has(neighborId)) {
650:       injectIds.push(neighborId);
651:     }
652:   }
653: 
654:   const injectedRows = fetchNeighborRows(injectIds).map((row) => {
655:     const causalBoost = Math.max(0, Math.min(activeBoosts.get(row.id) ?? 0, MAX_COMBINED_BOOST));
656:     metadata.maxBoostApplied = Math.max(metadata.maxBoostApplied, causalBoost);
657:     const baseScore = lowestScore * 0.5;
658:     return buildPipelineRow(row, baseScore, causalBoost);
659:   });
660: 
661:   metadata.injectedCount = injectedRows.length;
662:   metadata.applied = metadata.boostedCount > 0 || metadata.injectedCount > 0;
663: 
664:   const merged = [...boosted, ...injectedRows].sort((left, right) => {
665:     const leftScore = resolveBaseScore(left);
666:     const rightScore = resolveBaseScore(right);
667:     if (rightScore === leftScore) {
668:       return left.id - right.id;
669:     }
670:     return rightScore - leftScore;
671:   });
672: 
673:   return { results: merged, metadata };
674: }
675: 
676: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
677: // ALWAYS-ON GRAPH CONTEXT INJECTION
678: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
679: 
680: /**
681:  * Graph context metadata returned by injectGraphContext.
682:  * Contains related memory IDs and edge types discovered from the graph
683:  * even when the causal boost has no seed results.
684:  */
685: interface GraphContextResult {
686:   /** Whether graph context injection was active and produced results. */
687:   activated: boolean;
688:   /** Canonical concept names matched from the query. */
689:   matchedConcepts: string[];
690:   /** Memory IDs of graph neighbors related to matched concepts. */
691:   relatedMemoryIds: number[];
692:   /** Edge relation types connecting seeds to neighbors. */
693:   edgeTypes: string[];
694: }
695: 
696: /**
697:  * Always-on graph context injection.
698:  *
699:  * Runs concept routing on the query and finds graph neighbors for matched
700:  * concepts, returning graph context metadata (related memory IDs, edge types).
701:  * Unlike applyCausalBoost, this runs even when there are no seed results,
702:  * enabling proactive graph-guided context discovery.
703:  *
704:  * Feature-gated by SPECKIT_GRAPH_CONTEXT_INJECTION (default ON).
705:  * Fail-open: returns empty result on any error.
706:  *
707:  * @param query - The search query string.
708:  * @param database - SQLite database instance for graph lookups.
709:  * @returns GraphContextResult with related memory IDs and edge types.
710:  */
711: function injectGraphContext(
712:   query: string,
713:   database: Database.Database,
714: ): GraphContextResult {
715:   const empty: GraphContextResult = {
716:     activated: false,
717:     matchedConcepts: [],
718:     relatedMemoryIds: [],
719:     edgeTypes: [],
720:   };
721: 
722:   if (!isGraphContextInjectionEnabled()) return empty;
723: 
724:   try {
725:     const routing = routeQueryConcepts(query, database);
726:     if (!routing.graphActivated || routing.concepts.length === 0) return empty;
727: 
728:     // Find seed memory IDs whose titles contain matched concept keywords
729:     const likeClauses = routing.concepts.map(() => 'LOWER(title) LIKE ?').join(' OR ');
730:     const likeParams = routing.concepts.map((c) => `%${c.toLowerCase()}%`);
731: 
732:     const seedRows = (database.prepare(`
733:       SELECT id FROM memory_index WHERE (${likeClauses}) LIMIT 10
734:     `) as Database.Statement).all(...likeParams) as Array<{ id: number }>;
735: 
736:     if (seedRows.length === 0) {
737:       return { ...empty, activated: true, matchedConcepts: routing.concepts };
738:     }
739: 
740:     const seedIds = seedRows.map((r) => String(r.id));
741:     const seedPlaceholders = seedIds.map(() => '?').join(', ');
742: 
743:     // Walk 1-hop causal edges to find neighbor nodes and edge types
744:     const neighborRows = (database.prepare(`
745:       SELECT DISTINCT
746:         CASE
747:           WHEN ce.source_id IN (${seedPlaceholders}) THEN CAST(ce.target_id AS INTEGER)
748:           ELSE CAST(ce.source_id AS INTEGER)
749:         END AS neighbor_id,
750:         ce.relation
751:       FROM causal_edges ce
752:       WHERE ce.source_id IN (${seedPlaceholders}) OR ce.target_id IN (${seedPlaceholders})
753:       LIMIT 20
754:     `) as Database.Statement).all(
755:       ...seedIds,
756:       ...seedIds,
757:       ...seedIds,
758:     ) as Array<{ neighbor_id: number; relation: string }>;
759: 
760:     const relatedMemoryIds: number[] = [];
761:     const edgeTypes = new Set<string>();
762:     const seedIdSet = new Set(seedRows.map((r) => r.id));
763: 
764:     for (const row of neighborRows) {
765:       if (!seedIdSet.has(row.neighbor_id) && Number.isFinite(row.neighbor_id)) {
766:         relatedMemoryIds.push(row.neighbor_id);
767:       }
768:       if (row.relation) {
769:         edgeTypes.add(row.relation);
770:       }
771:     }
772: 
773:     return {
774:       activated: true,
775:       matchedConcepts: routing.concepts,
776:       relatedMemoryIds: [...new Set(relatedMemoryIds)].slice(0, 10),
777:       edgeTypes: Array.from(edgeTypes),
778:     };
779:   } catch (error: unknown) {
780:     const message = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
781:     console.warn(`[causal-boost] injectGraphContext failed (fail-open): ${message}`);
782:     return empty;
783:   }
784: }
785: 
786: export {
787:   MAX_HOPS,
788:   MAX_BOOST_PER_HOP,
789:   RELATION_WEIGHT_MULTIPLIERS,
790:   // D3 Phase A exports
791:   SPARSE_DENSITY_THRESHOLD,
792:   SPARSE_MAX_HOPS,
793:   DEFAULT_TYPED_TRAVERSAL_DEPTH,
794:   INTENT_EDGE_PRIORITY,
795:   DEFAULT_EDGE_PRIORITY,
796:   DEFAULT_EDGE_PRIOR,
797:   EDGE_PRIOR_TIERS,
798:   EDGE_LABEL_ALIASES,
799:   init,
800:   isEnabled,
801:   isTypedTraversalEnabled,
802:   isSparseMode,
803:   resolveSparseFirstTraversalPolicy,
804:   resolveTraversalDepth,
805:   resolveEdgePrior,
806:   computeHopDecay,
807:   computeIntentTraversalScore,
808:   getIntentEdgePriorities,
809:   computeIntentEdgePrior,
810:   computeTraversalHopDecay,
811:   computeTraversalFreshnessFactor,
812:   computeIntentAwareTraversalScore,
813:   getNeighborBoosts,
814:   applyCausalBoost,
815:   // Always-on graph context injection
816:   injectGraphContext,
817: };
818: 
819: export type {
820:   RankedSearchResult,
821:   CausalBoostMetadata,
822:   CausalBoostOptions,
823:   SparseFirstTraversalPolicy,
824:   GraphContextResult,
825: };

(End of file - total 825 lines)
</content>
